description:
PROBLEM
The parent bead `fizeau-cd9af16b` proposes moving every root-level `package fizeau` file into `internal/service/` and requires zero `.go` files at the module root. Repository evidence conflicts with that plan:
- ADR-008 says the root `package fizeau` is the public facade at `github.com/easel/fizeau` and that the module import path must stay stable.
- The generated embedding docs document the root package as the importable library surface.
- agentcli/, cmd/bench/, cmd/fiz/, and root black-box tests import `github.com/easel/fizeau` directly.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` explicitly keeps the root package as the compatibility boundary and moves only concrete implementation code behind `internal/`. `service.go:18-20` defines the root `FizeauService` public interface. `website/content/docs/embedding/_index.md:949-974` documents `github.com/easel/fizeau` as the root facade and lists importable public packages. Therefore parent AC #1 (zero root `.go` files) is not executable without either violating ADR-008 or first designing a replacement public facade.

PROPOSED FIX
Write a cold-readable design/inventory doc under `docs/helix/02-design/` that converts the oversized parent into an ordered extraction plan. The doc must inventory root-level `.go` files, bucket each as public facade, public compatibility alias/shim, or implementation-owned file, list in-repo consumers of `github.com/easel/fizeau`, and name follow-on extraction slices with target internal package owners.

NON-SCOPE
Do not move production Go files in this bead. Do not rewrite imports. Do not update CHANGELOG.md or generated website links beyond whatever is required to land the governing inventory doc itself. Do not remove the root public package.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `fizeau-cd9af16b`. No deps; this bead is the design/inventory prerequisite that makes the parent sequence executable.
title: repo-structure: reconcile root-package move with ADR-008 and inventory facade vs implementation files

description:
PROBLEM
  internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go carries the legacy v4 schema with overlapping concepts: profiles AND targets AND aliases AND surface_policy AND ModelEntry.Tier back-pointers AND compatibility_target redirection. The v0.11 surface redesign collapses this to a single first-class concept (policy) and drops everything else.

ROOT CAUSE
  Manifest accumulated migration debt from prior renames. compatibility_target/target duality is half-done rename cruft (validator at manifest.go:460 says they must match when both set). surface_policy was an attempt to centralize per-(target × harness) defaults that ages poorly once reasoning_default moves to per-model. ModelEntry.Tier is a back-pointer to a target that no longer exists in v0.11.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - Top-level keys: models, policies, providers (catalog-side type+include_by_default declarations), sampling_profiles (UNCHANGED, ADR-007).
  - REMOVED: targets:, aliases:, compatibility_target on policies, surface_policy blocks, ModelEntry.Tier field.
  - Each policy: { min_power: int, max_power: int, allow_local: bool, require: []string }.
  - Hard cut on schema version (v4 → v5). External manifests pinned to v4 fail at load with helpful error pointing at ADR-009 (Phase 8).
  - No aliases. The four canonical policy names (default, cheap, smart, air-gapped) are the only valid values. code-economy/code-smart/standard/local/offline/fast/code-fast/code-high/code-medium are removed with no replacement.
  - Validator requires catalog to define a "default" policy (fallback for empty req.Policy).
  - New BillingForProviderSystem and BillingForHarness functions in internal/modelcatalog/billing.go: hardcoded mapping by provider/harness type (lmstudio/llama-server/omlx/vllm/rapid-mlx/ollama/lucebox → fixed; openai/openrouter/anthropic/google → per_token; claude/codex/gemini harnesses → subscription; native fiz harness derives from underlying provider).

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:36-76 ModelEntry (with Tier field at line 40)
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:107-118 manifest struct (Targets, Profiles fields)
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:120-126 profileEntry
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:128-218 surfaceValue/targetEntry/surfacePolicyEntry types (~91 lines to delete)
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:245-487 loadManifest + validateManifest
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:489-545 upgradeManifest (delete entirely)
  - internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go:577 profileCompatibilityTarget
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:36,53-69 Catalog struct, Profile/ResolvedTarget types
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:122-182 Profiles/Profile/Aliases methods

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Drop ModelEntry.Tier field. Add ModelEntry.ReasoningDefault (reasoning.Reasoning type) for per-model intrinsic reasoning posture.
  2. manifest struct: drop Targets, drop Profiles. Add Policies map[string]policyEntry, Providers map[string]providerEntry. No top-level aliases field.
  3. policyEntry: { MinPower int, MaxPower int, AllowLocal bool, Require []string }. providerEntry: { Type string, IncludeByDefault bool, Billing string (optional, required if Type unknown) }.
  4. Delete surfaceValue, targetEntry, surfacePolicyEntry types (~91 lines).
  5. Rewrite validateManifest: max_schema_version = 5; every policy has min<=max valid power range; every provider has type; "default" policy must exist; require[] entries must be in known invariant set ({"no_remote"}); user-config providers with Type not in known catalog set must declare Billing explicitly.
  6. Delete upgradeManifest; v4→v5 is a hard cut. Loader emits "manifest schema v5 required (got v%d); see ADR-009 for v0.11 schema redesign" on version mismatch.
  7. Catalog struct: rename profileEntries → policyEntries. Replace Profile struct with Policy{Name string, MinPower, MaxPower int, AllowLocal bool, Require []string}. Drop ResolvedTarget, SurfacePolicy, MissingSurfaceError, DeprecatedTargetError, UnknownTargetError, Surface enum, ResolveOptions, Resolve, Current, AllConcreteModels, CandidatesFor, AllModelsInTier, TierModel, concreteModelsForSurface, primaryConcreteModel, targetCandidateIDs, deprecatedRoutingGuidance.
  8. Catalog: add Policy(name) (Policy, bool), Policies() []Policy. Drop Aliases() entirely (no aliases concept).
  9. New file internal/modelcatalog/billing.go: BillingModel enum (fixed/per_token/subscription) + BillingForProviderSystem(string) BillingModel + BillingForHarness(string) BillingModel.
  10. Inline-YAML test fixtures in internal/modelcatalog/manifest_test.go and catalog_test.go: rewrite from legacy v4 schema to v5 in lockstep with the production code change.

NON-SCOPE
  - Catalog YAML rewrite (internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml) — Phase 3.
  - Public service surface — Phase 4.
  - Provider config (user-side) — Phase 5.
  - Specs — Phase 8.

PARENT + DEPS
  Depends on Phase 1 (uses new error types).

acceptance:
1. With a valid API key but a stubbed `/api/v1/credits` returning balance 0, routing_decision shows openrouter candidates as `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: "credit_exhausted"` and evidence body containing the observed balance. Verified by `TestOpenrouterZeroBalanceMarksCreditExhausted`.
2. With a valid API key and a stubbed balance comfortably above the configured threshold, openrouter candidates are `eligible: true` and dispatch proceeds normally. Verified by `TestOpenrouterAdequateBalanceAllowsDispatch` plus no regression in existing `service_adr005_test.go` happy paths.
3. With balance just below a non-default threshold set via config, candidate is filtered with `credit_exhausted` — confirms threshold is wired through config, not hardcoded. Verified by `TestOpenrouterCreditThresholdConfigurable`.
4. Across N (N≥10) back-to-back Execute calls within one TTL window, the `/api/v1/credits` endpoint is invoked at most once. Verified by `TestOpenrouterCreditProbeCachedWithinTTL` using a counting roundtripper.
5. After TTL expiry, the next routing pass re-probes. Verified by `TestOpenrouterCreditProbeRefreshesAfterTTL` with a fake clock.
6. Candidate row populates `quota_freshness_at` with the probe timestamp so operators can see how stale the balance reading is.
7. The configurable threshold (default $0.50) and TTL are documented in the openrouter provider config docs.
8. `cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./...` green.
9. `cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && lefthook run pre-commit` green.
description:
PROBLEM
Even when the openrouter API key is valid, an account with exhausted credit will fail every dispatch with an insufficient-credit response. Without a proactive credit probe, each request burns a retry to rediscover an account-level condition that does not change between requests.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no openrouter quota/balance probe in the route-picker's freshness-cache layer. `service_routing.go` already exposes `quota_freshness_at` on candidate rows and consults a cached aliveness/quota state so the picker performs no per-call network I/O — openrouter is simply not wired into that cache. Exact integration point to be confirmed during implementation by locating the existing `quota_freshness_at` write path.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a periodic openrouter credit probe that calls `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/credits` (confirm exact path against current openrouter docs at implementation time) using the configured API key.
2. Cache the result in the same freshness-cache module as existing aliveness/quota state, keyed by openrouter provider id. TTL configurable; default in the 5–15 minute range (implementer picks within that band and documents the choice).
3. Configurable balance threshold for `credit_exhausted` (default $0.50). Threshold lives in openrouter provider config; documented alongside other provider knobs.
4. If cached balance is below threshold → candidate `eligible: false`, `filter_reason: "credit_exhausted"`, evidence body includes the observed balance and the probe timestamp.
5. If cached balance is at/above threshold → candidate passes this gate (credential gate handled by [[openrouter-credential-presence-gate]]; failure modes handled by [[openrouter-probe-failure-modes-and-surfacing]]).
6. Route picker reads cached state only — no per-Execute network round-trip to openrouter.ai/credits.

NON-SCOPE
- Credential presence/format checks (covered by [[openrouter-credential-presence-gate]]).
- 401 / transport-failure handling and the credential_invalid / provider_unreachable filter_reasons (covered by [[openrouter-probe-failure-modes-and-surfacing]]).
- Per-model credit-cost forecasting (parent non-scope, preserved).
- Multi-account credential rotation (parent non-scope, preserved).
- DDx-side changes per CONTRACT-003 (parent non-scope, preserved).

CROSS-REF
Sibling beads: [[openrouter-credential-presence-gate]], [[openrouter-probe-failure-modes-and-surfacing]]. Cross-ref fizeau-80d4bf41.
title: openrouter: credit balance probe with TTL cache and configurable threshold

description:
PROBLEM
DDx is removing its `ddx agent` surface and internal `agent` subsystem vocabulary. Fizeau is the model router/execution service DDx consumes, so Fizeau must provide the durable upstream contract language and implementation targets for that cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The public facade already exposes FizeauService at service.go:20 and CONTRACT-003 at docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md:294 documents ExecuteRequest route constraints, but the contract and code still contain native-harness identity language such as Harness=="agent" in service.go:609-620 and selection docs that do not yet define the requested fuzzy model-constraint behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
Track the upstream work needed by DDx as children: (1) rename the native Fizeau harness/contract identity from `agent` to `fiz` with no compatibility alias in the public contract, and (2) define and implement Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching for explicit model constraints such as qwen36.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx CLI/package names in this repo. Do not add DDx-side routing policy. Do not retain `agent` as a Fizeau-native compatibility alias unless a separate operator-approved migration bead explicitly reverses the no-deprecation decision.

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: none. Dependencies: none.

description:
P0 critical-path: the translator changes shipped in 7870cc84 wire the
catalog reasoning_wire correctly for OR-Qwen3, but two dialect bugs
remain that block sindri and ds4 lanes:

1. **sindri-llamacpp Qwen3 thinking does not activate.** Verified by the
   2026-05-11 probe artifact under docs/research/probe-reasoning-2026-05-
   11/sindri/: all six matrix rows returned 0 reasoning_tokens. Direct
   probing showed that top-level `enable_thinking`/`thinking_budget` is
   silently dropped by llama-server. The chat_template_kwargs envelope
   works: 250-526 completion tokens with extracted reasoning_content.

2. **ds4 deepseek-v4-flash thinking is uncontrolled.** ds4's
   /v1/chat/completions accepts only flat top-level `reasoning_effort`
   (verified via /props.api.supported_request_parameters); the
   Anthropic-shape `thinking: {type, budget_tokens}` that fizeau emits
   today via ThinkingWireFormatThinkingMap is silently ignored. ds4
   /props.reasoning.aliases declares {low→high, medium→high, xhigh→high};
   only `max` is genuinely distinct (and requires --ctx ≥ 393216, which
   vidar:1236 satisfies).

Implementation:

### 1. Add ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort constant

In internal/provider/openai/protocol_support.go:
  ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort ThinkingWireFormat = "openai_effort"

Wire shape: flat top-level `reasoning_effort: \"<tier>\"`. Disable form:
top-level `think: false` (per ds4 docs).

In internal/provider/openai/openai.go (around line 421-425 today after
7870cc84), replace:
  option.WithJSONSet(\"enable_thinking\", true)
  option.WithJSONSet(\"thinking_budget\", thinkingBudget)
with:
  option.WithJSONSet(\"chat_template_kwargs\", map[string]interface{}{
      \"enable_thinking\": true,
      \"thinking_budget\": thinkingBudget,
  })

Off-path (around openai.go:391-394) similarly:
  chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking = false

Per-request `reasoning_format` is OPTIONAL — sindri's server-side default
is `auto` which extracts thinking correctly. v1 leaves it unset.

### 3. Add OpenAIEffort branch in reasoningRequestOptions

Today it's set to Thinking:true with no explicit ThinkingFormat (or

description:
PROBLEM
The new bash runner needs a durable cell lifecycle before concurrency and Docker execution can be layered on safely.

ROOT CAUSE
The parent scope replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go:1 with scripts/benchmark/benchmark, but the current behavior for skipping completed cells, rerunning invalid or orphaned cells, writing cell-state.json sentinels, invoking adapters/executors, composing report.json, and deleting sentinels is embedded in the existing Go runner. The broad parent bead combines this lifecycle rewrite with unrelated adapter, concurrency, and Docker image work.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement sequential run-mode cell execution in scripts/benchmark/benchmark using the shell adapter and task-executor contracts. For each planned cell, create cell_dir, write cell-state.json, invoke harness-adapters/<name> with profile.json on stdin to produce command-spec.json, compose task-spec, invoke task-executors/<name> with task-spec.json on stdin so it writes cell_dir/result.json, compose report.json, and remove cell-state.json on terminal completion. Implement default resume skip when cell_dir/report.json has a terminal final_status. Implement --force-rerun to ignore completed reports. Implement --retry-invalid to rerun cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json. Cover all behavior with fake adapters/executors and filesystem assertions.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement background job pooling, flock locks, setsid process groups, signal fanout, docker stop cleanup, or the harbor-runner Docker image in this child. Do not expand the bench-set schema beyond PR A smoke requirements; PR C finalizes schema. Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it. Governing artifact reference must remain governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Parent/dependency ID fizeau-11e9c095 must remain traceable. Verification requirements cd cli && go test ./... and lefthook run pre-commit are required gates.

AC
1. TestBenchmarkCellLifecycleWritesReport: a sequential run writes cell-state.json before execution, invokes adapter and executor with JSON stdin contracts, writes result.json/report.json, and removes cell-state.json after terminal completion.
2. TestBenchmarkResumeSkipsTerminalReport: default run mode skips a cell whose report.json contains a terminal final_status.
3. TestBenchmarkForceRerunIgnoresTerminalReport: --force-rerun executes a cell even when terminal report.json already exists.
4. TestBenchmarkRetryInvalidRerunsInvalidOrOrphan: --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json and does not rerun valid terminal cells.
5. TestBenchmarkExecutorFailureLeavesSentinel: executor failure preserves enough cell-state.json/report context to support retry-invalid behavior and exits non-zero.
6. TestBenchmarkVerificationGatesA3: cd cli && go test ./... passes.
7. TestBenchmarkLefthookGateA3: lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: bench-pr-A3: per-cell lifecycle, resume, rerun, and reports

description:
PROBLEM
The PR A bash runner must run cells concurrently while respecting concurrency groups and must wrap Harbor in Docker so hosts no longer need Python for Harbor/plugin execution.

ROOT CAUSE
The parent scope requires replacing cmd/bench/matrix.go:1 with scripts/benchmark/benchmark and removing the host Python dependency introduced by cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105. It also requires flock-based concurrency-group locking, setsid process groups, background-job pooling capped by --jobs, SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown, docker stop for in-flight harbor-runner containers, and a fizeau-harbor-runner image. These execution concerns are independent from planning and sequential lifecycle work and need isolated coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
Add concurrent execution to scripts/benchmark/benchmark with a background-job pool capped by --jobs. Use flock per concurrency group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock. Start each cell under setsid and track process groups. On SIGINT/SIGTERM, stop accepting new cells, SIGTERM each in-flight cell process group, docker stop each in-flight harbor-runner container, wait 30 seconds, then SIGKILL remaining process groups. Add task-executors/harbor shell wrapper that runs Harbor through the fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image and writes cell_dir/result.json. Add container-image build assets needed by PR A smoke tests. Tests should mock docker, flock-sensitive workers, and long-running cells.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change planning semantics, adapter JSON contracts, or report composition except where required to integrate concurrent execution. Do not finalize the PR C bench-set schema. Do not require real Docker daemon access in unit tests; mock docker for deterministic verification. Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it. Governing artifact reference must remain governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Parent/dependency ID fizeau-11e9c095 must remain traceable. Verification requirements cd cli && go test ./... and lefthook run pre-commit are required gates.

AC
1. TestBenchmarkJobsLimit: run mode never has more than --jobs cells in flight.
2. TestBenchmarkConcurrencyGroupFlock: cells sharing a concurrency group serialize through the configured flock lock path, while unrelated groups may run concurrently.
3. TestBenchmarkSignalTerminatesProcessGroups: SIGINT/SIGTERM stops scheduling new cells, sends SIGTERM to each in-flight process group, waits, and escalates to SIGKILL after the configured timeout.
4. TestBenchmarkSignalStopsHarborContainers: shutdown calls docker stop for each tracked in-flight harbor-runner container.
5. TestHarborTaskExecutorDockerWrapper: task-executors/harbor invokes the fizeau-harbor-runner image with task-spec input, captures the container result, and writes cell_dir/result.json without host Python imports.
6. TestHarborRunnerImageBuildSmoke: harbor-runner image build assets exist and the configured build command succeeds or is covered by a mockable CI-safe smoke path.
7. TestBenchmarkVerificationGatesA4: cd cli && go test ./... passes.
8. TestBenchmarkLefthookGateA4: lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: bench-pr-A4: concurrency, signal handling, and harbor-runner container wrapper

description:
PROBLEM
  internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml is the canonical fizeau catalog manifest. It currently uses schema v4 with the legacy profiles/targets/aliases/surface_policy structure, declares 11 routing profiles (most of which collapse to 4 canonicals + dropped names), references 4 deprecated targets (code-high, code-medium are deprecated; smart, standard, code-economy are active), and carries surface_policy blocks for per-(target × harness) reasoning/placement/cost defaults that the v0.11 schema removes.

ROOT CAUSE
  Manifest YAML is the source of truth that must match the new schema (Phase 2). Phase 2 redefines the Go types and validator; this phase rewrites the YAML to match. Cannot land independently — Phase 2 validator will reject the current YAML; this YAML rewrite is required for the validator's tests to pass against real catalog data.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  Final policy table:
    default:    { min_power: 7, max_power: 8, allow_local: true }
    cheap:      { min_power: 5, max_power: 5, allow_local: true }
    smart:      { min_power: 9, max_power: 10, allow_local: false }
    air-gapped: { min_power: 5, max_power: 5, allow_local: true, require: [no_remote] }
  Provider include_by_default defaults: local providers true; per_token providers (openai/openrouter/anthropic/google) false; subscription harnesses (claude/codex/gemini) true.
  models[]: drop tier: field; add reasoning_default: where intrinsic (opus/sonnet/gpt-5.5/gemini-pro/gpt-5.4-mini get high or medium per current surface_policy values).
  No aliases. No targets. No surface_policy. No compatibility_target.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:1 version: 4 → bump to 5
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:8 catalog_version: 2026-04-30.1 → bump to 2026-05-08.1
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:14-28 sampling_profiles: UNCHANGED (ADR-007)
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:30-436 models: (drop tier:, add reasoning_default:)
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:438-493 profiles: → replace with policies:
  - internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:495-654 targets: → DELETE (160 lines)
  - aliases section (currently inline aliases on profile entries via compatibility_target) → REMOVED entirely

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Bump version: 5 and catalog_version to today.
  2. For each models[] entry: remove tier: line; add reasoning_default: where the model has an intrinsic posture (claude-opus-4.7 → high, claude-sonnet-4.6 → medium, gpt-5.5 → high, gpt-5.4-mini → off, gemini-2.5-pro → off, gemini-2.5-flash → off, etc., per the current targets[].surface_policy[].reasoning_default values).
  3. Replace profiles: block with policies: block containing exactly four entries above.
  4. Delete targets: block in its entirety.
  5. Add new providers: block declaring all known provider types with their default include_by_default (local: true, per-token: false, subscription harnesses: true). No billing field needed (catalog hardcodes it via BillingForProviderSystem in Phase 2).
  6. No aliases block. Names like code-economy, code-smart, standard, local, offline, fast, code-fast, code-high, code-medium do not appear in this manifest.

NON-SCOPE
  - Schema changes in manifest.go — Phase 2.
  - Public API changes — Phase 4.
  - User-config provider overrides — Phase 5.

PARENT + DEPS
  Depends on Phase 2 (validator must accept the new YAML).

acceptance:
1. With openrouter API key unset (env var empty and config missing), routing_decision shows openrouter candidates as `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: "credential_missing"` and no HTTP call is made. Verified by an integration test that scrubs the env var, runs an Execute with no pin, and asserts the candidates payload.
2. With a valid API key but zero credit balance, routing_decision shows openrouter candidates as `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: "credit_exhausted"`, and the candidate row carries the observed balance in the evidence body. Verified by a test that stubs the openrouter credits endpoint to return zero.
3. With a valid API key and adequate credit, openrouter candidates are `eligible: true` and dispatch proceeds normally. Verified by the existing happy-path tests (no regression).
4. Credit-check results are cached with a configurable TTL (default 5-15 minutes, exact value chosen by implementer) so per-request routing does not pay a network round-trip. Verified by a test that asserts the credits endpoint is called at most once per TTL window across N back-to-back Execute calls.
5. A credit-check transport failure (e.g. openrouter.ai unreachable) does NOT hard-exclude openrouter — it surfaces as `provider_unreachable` with `eligible: false` for the freshness window but a fresh probe is attempted on the next routing pass. Verified by a test that simulates one transient failure followed by recovery.
6. A 401 from the credit-check endpoint surfaces as `filter_reason: "credential_invalid"` (distinct from credential_missing) so operators can tell "you forgot to set the key" from "the key you set is rejected".
7. Configurable threshold for credit_exhausted (default $0.50) is documented in the openrouter provider config docs.
8. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
9. No regression in existing routing tests (service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go).
description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau's auto-route includes openrouter as an eligible candidate even when no API key is configured AND/OR the account has zero credit balance. Dispatches that pick openrouter then fail at HTTP-call time with 401 (no auth header) or insufficient-credit errors. Beads burn retry cycles, sticky leases get assigned to a dead provider, and operators must debug a transport error to discover that the actual problem is a missing credential or empty wallet.

This is the same pattern Fizeau already handles correctly for local LMStudio/MLX endpoints: when a local endpoint fails its aliveness probe, it surfaces as `filter_reason: "endpoint_unreachable"` in routing_decision and is excluded from scoring. Openrouter needs an analogous health gate.

EVIDENCE FROM DOWNSTREAM (DDx, commit 09881f3a5)
While debugging fizeau-80d4bf41 (auto-route candidate enumeration), I observed:
1. With `--model sonnet-4.6` pinned, Fizeau routed to `fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` with score 196.0 (auto-route's first choice once the model name was known to it).
2. The dispatch then immediately failed: `agent: provider error: openai: POST "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions": 401 Unauthorized {"message":"Missing Authentication header","code":401}`.
3. No upstream filter on the openrouter candidate. It appeared in the candidate list as `eligible: true`, was selected, and the credential failure surfaced only at HTTP call time inside the harness.

Symmetric symptom is expected when credentials exist but credit is exhausted — openrouter returns a 402/insufficient-credit response. Without proactive credit checks, every dispatch through openrouter will burn a retry to discover this.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an openrouter provider-health hook analogous to the LMStudio aliveness probe. The hook should:

1. **Credential check** (cheap, run on every routing pass or cache for a short TTL like 60s):
   - If the openrouter API key environment variable / config is empty or unset → mark openrouter `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: "credential_missing"`. Do not even hit the network.
   - If the configured key is malformed (wrong prefix, wrong length) → same.

2. **Credit check** (more expensive, run periodically — recommend 5-15 minute TTL with stale-allowed-during-grace):
   - Call openrouter's `GET /api/v1/credits` (or whatever the current endpoint is) to fetch remaining credit balance.
   - If balance is below a configurable threshold (default: $0.50) → mark openrouter `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: "credit_exhausted"`, include current balance in the decision evidence.
   - If the credit-check call itself fails with 401 → mark `credential_invalid` (different from missing).
   - If the credit-check call fails with transport error → mark `provider_unreachable` (transient; fail-open with stale-signal-penalty bump rather than hard-excluding, to avoid flapping).

3. **Surface in routing_decision**: candidate row appears with `eligible: false`, `filter_reason` set to one of the values above, and `health_freshness_at` / `quota_freshness_at` populated from the cached probe. This mirrors how `bragi-club-3090` already appears with `endpoint_unreachable` and how `claude-haiku-4.5` appears with `power_missing`.

4. **Cache placement**: probe state should live in the same place as the existing harness aliveness/quota state so the route picker reads it without doing per-call network I/O. `service_routing.go` already has `health_freshness_at` and `quota_freshness_at` fields plumbed through — wire openrouter into that.

NON-SCOPE
- Multi-account credential rotation / failover within openrouter.
- Per-model credit-cost forecasting.
- The DDx-side change is none — this is purely Fizeau-side. DDx passes `policy=default` through unchanged per CONTRACT-003.

CROSS-REF
Filed in tandem with fizeau-80d4bf41 (auto-route candidate enumeration collapses multi-tier subscription harnesses to DefaultModel only). The two bugs together explain why DDx's `policy=default` lands on opus instead of sonnet: enumeration drops sonnet from claude, model-pin fallback routes to openrouter, openrouter has no creds.
notes: decomposed into fizeau-458f10b0 (routing engine plumbing + filter-reason constants), fizeau-73a00a6b (credential_missing from env/config), fizeau-d6bed010 (credit-balance probe with TTL cache, credit_exhausted/credential_invalid/provider_unreachable, docs). Three deps-chained children: A → B and A → C, with C depending on both A and B. Each child lands as a focused reviewable commit; parent stays open until all three close.

description:
PROBLEM
Background model discovery refresh can inherit a caller context that has already been cancelled. In stale-while-revalidate mode this means `AssembleWithOptions(... RefreshBackground)` returns stale rows but the refresh goroutine may never reach provider discovery, so stale caches remain stale. Runtime signal warmup also needs to normalize OpenAI-compatible base URLs that already include `/v1` so probes hit `/v1/models` exactly once.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `internal/modelregistry/discovery.go:134-143` and `internal/modelsnapshot/discovery.go:133-142` build provider discovery requests from the caller context when one is present, even for `RefreshBackground` refreshers spawned by the cache.
- `internal/modelregistry/background_refresh_contract_test.go` lacked coverage for a cancelled caller context with stale cached rows.
- `internal/runtimesignals/collect.go:241-247` constructs the local runtime probe URL by appending `/v1/models` directly to `BaseURL`, producing `/v1/v1/models` when the configured base URL already ends in `/v1`.

PROPOSED FIX
For background refresh mode, use the cache refresh context rather than the caller context; for foreground refresh modes, preserve caller cancellation while bounding it by the cache refresh deadline. Mirror the helper in both modelregistry and modelsnapshot until those packages are deduplicated. Normalize local runtime signal base URLs before appending `/v1/models`. Add/keep focused tests for stale-while-revalidate with a cancelled caller and for `/v1` base URL normalization.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/modelregistry/discovery.go
- internal/modelregistry/background_refresh_contract_test.go
- internal/modelsnapshot/discovery.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go
- internal/runtimesignals/warmup_test.go only if the existing URL-normalization test needs adjustment

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change cache marker layout or single-flight semantics.
- Do not change model routing scores or provider selection policy.
- Do not refactor modelregistry/modelsnapshot duplication beyond the minimum helper needed here.
title: modelregistry: keep background refresh independent of caller cancellation

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pre-claim readiness needs a "smart/frontier" route, but Fizeau's public routing surface currently exposes overlapping concepts (`profile`, `model_ref`, `model`, `min_power`, `max_power`, and `harness`) with unclear semantics. DDx temporarily switched readiness to `model_ref=smart` in v0.6.2-alpha40 because the installed Fizeau route-status path reported no smart route even though GPT-5.5 was available. That workaround is not an acceptable long-term contract; human input is needed on the Fizeau-side routing API.

USER FEEDBACK
The operator explicitly rejected the current workaround: "model_ref is bullshit". Treat this bead as a design/contract decision gate, not a request to deepen DDx's workaround.

OBSERVED EVIDENCE ON 2026-05-08
- Installed binary: `/home/erik/go/bin/fiz` reports `fiz v0.10.13`.
- Installed binary command:
  `fiz route-status --profile smart --json --work-dir /Users/erik/Projects/ddx`
  returned `candidates: []` and error `no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at tier >= smart`.
- The same installed catalog advertises smart entries: `fiz catalog show` reports `agent.openai: gpt-5.5` and `agent.anthropic: opus-4.7` under `smart`.
- DDx proved GPT-5.5 was reachable through the workaround path: after installing DDx v0.6.2-alpha40, `ddx run --model-ref smart --harness codex --text 'Reply exactly: alpha40-smart-ok' --timeout 45s` succeeded and session `as-199166d6` recorded `harness=codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`.
- Current Fizeau checkout differs from the installed binary: `./fiz --version` reports `v0.10.12-0.20260506230320-1819c0a582c2+dirty`; `./fiz route-status --profile smart --json --work-dir /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` now returns smart candidates including `claude/opus-4.7` and `agent/openrouter openai/gpt-5.5`.
- In the same checkout, `./fiz route-status --model-ref smart --harness codex --json --work-dir /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` returns `candidates: []` and the same `no live provider ... tier >= smart` error because the codex candidate is currently unhealthy/quota-limited. This shows the surface is also sensitive to quota/health state in a way that produces misleading operator language.

CODE ANCHORS
- `service.go:368-378` defines `RouteRequest` with `Profile`, `Model`, `Provider`, `Harness`, `ModelRef`, `MinPower`, and `MaxPower` side by side.
- `service.go:676-688` defines `ServiceExecuteRequest` with both `ModelRef` and `Profile` exposed publicly.
- `service_routing.go:46-82` threads profile, model-ref, provider preference, and power bounds into internal routing.
- `service_routing.go:1091-1115` maps profile to provider preference.
- `service_routing.go:1117-1150` derives profile power policy but explicitly does not apply it when `Model` or `ModelRef` is set.
- `service_execute.go:296-305` treats `smart`/`code-smart`/`code-high` as subscription-only explicit profile constraints when a harness is pinned.
- `agentcli/routing_smart.go:823-825` exposes both `--profile` and `--model-ref` on `route-status`, making users choose between two smart-ish abstractions.

IMPACT
- Downstream callers can report "no smart model available" while GPT-5.5 is actually reachable through a different Fizeau field.
- DDx readiness can park, skip, or fail-open beads for the wrong reason.
- Operators are forced to know that `model_ref=smart` may work when `profile=smart` does not, which is not a coherent public API.
- Quota/health rejection is currently collapsed into a generic "no live provider" message that hides the existence of smart candidates and the reason they were rejected.

HUMAN DECISION NEEDED
Decide the canonical Fizeau contract for "smart/frontier route": should callers express it as `profile=smart`, numeric power (`min_power` / `max_power`), a catalog alias, or a new intent field? Decide whether `ModelRef` should remain public, become internal, be renamed, or be deprecated. Decide how explicit harness pins interact with smart intent and how route-status/Execute should explain quota/health rejection.

PROPOSED WORK AFTER DECISION
- Amend the Fizeau contract/ADR/docs with the chosen semantics.
- Align `RouteRequest`, `ServiceExecuteRequest`, `route-status`, `Execute`, and CLI help with that contract.
- Add tests for the chosen behavior, especially smart intent with and without explicit `--harness codex`.
- File or update downstream DDx cleanup work to remove the `model_ref=smart` workaround once Fizeau exposes the final surface.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the routing change in this bead. Do not add another DDx workaround here. This bead is the human decision record that unlocks implementation beads.

notes:
DECISION RECORDED 2026-05-08

Canonical Fizeau routing-surface semantics for v0.11 (decided via design conversation):

PROFILE → POLICY (rename)
  "Policy" is the single canonical word. No aliases (code-economy/code-smart/standard/local/offline/fast/code-fast/code-high/code-medium are dropped with no replacement). Four canonical policies:
    default:    {min_power: 7, max_power: 8, allow_local: true}
    cheap:      {min_power: 5, max_power: 5, allow_local: true}
    smart:      {min_power: 9, max_power: 10, allow_local: false}
    air-gapped: {min_power: 5, max_power: 5, allow_local: true, require: [no_remote]}

MODELREF → REMOVED ENTIRELY
  Hard cut at v0.11.0. No compat period, no deprecated alias. Operators using --model-ref get a cobra "unknown flag" error. ServiceExecuteRequest.ModelRef field is deleted (not deprecated). Catalog ref expansion previously done via ModelRef no longer happens — callers pass concrete Model when pinning, otherwise rely on Policy.

MIN_POWER / MAX_POWER → SOFT SCORING SIGNALS
  Fields preserved but no longer enforced as filters. Scorer applies asymmetric penalties: undershoot 5x worse than overshoot. A power-7 candidate beats a power-11 candidate when both healthy and policy is min:8/max:10. Smart's allow_local: false is what produces fail-closed behavior when subscription is dry, NOT a hard power floor.

HARNESS PINS
  Pin overrides provider.include_by_default (operator can reach excluded providers via pin). Pin does NOT override policy.require[] (--policy air-gapped --provider openrouter fails with ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied). Pin-pin conflicts (harness×model, harness×provider, provider×model) fail with ErrUnsatisfiablePin.

ERROR LANGUAGE FOR THE FOUR ROUTE-STATUS SCENARIOS

  --policy smart (subscription healthy):
    Returns smart candidates from claude/codex/gemini subscription harnesses.

  --policy smart (subscription dry):
    Returns ErrNoLiveProvider with RejectedCandidates populated. Error message: "policy 'smart' has N candidate(s) matching power 9-10 but all rejected: Q quota-exhausted, U unhealthy".

  --policy smart --harness codex (codex healthy):
    Pin to codex; returns the gpt-5.5 candidate.

  --policy smart --harness codex (codex unhealthy/quota-exhausted):
    Returns ErrNoLiveProvider with RejectedCandidates=[{Harness: codex, Reason: quota_exhausted}]. Error message names the specific harness and reason.

  --model-ref smart --harness codex (transition):
    Cobra "unknown flag: --model-ref" error. No transition period.

  Execute({Policy:"smart", Harness:"codex"}):
    Same resolution as --policy smart --harness codex above. Field is Policy not Profile (Go compile error if caller uses Profile).

IMPLEMENTATION BEADS (FILED)

  Phase 1: fizeau-b633cbb3 — internal/routing rename + soft min/max + ModelRef removal + ErrNoLiveProvider quota/health distinction (this addresses the operator-visible error language ask in AC4 of this bead)
  Phase 2: fizeau-040f1f83 — manifest schema redesign + billing.go
  Phase 3: fizeau-0aaa94e5 — catalog/models.yaml v5 rewrite
  Phase 4: fizeau-c6c8bc9e — public service surface
  Phase 5: fizeau-2d0bd0a4 — provider config (include_by_default + Billing)
  Phase 6: fizeau-399ecb48 — CLI surface (fiz policies + fiz harnesses subcommands; --model-ref/--profile flags removed)
  Phase 7: fizeau-9eb62a07 — internal/core ModelRef removal + telemetry/session-log schema break
  Phase 8: fizeau-264e70c4 — ADR-009 + CONTRACT-003 + FEAT-003/004/005/006 rewrites (this is where the canonical semantics get committed to a Fizeau design document, satisfying AC1)
  Phase 9: fizeau-32e533bb — DDx coordination (drop --model-ref, rename Profile→Policy in DDx call sites; satisfies AC5's downstream cleanup)

AC SATISFACTION TABLE

  AC1 (semantics in design doc) → Bead 8 produces ADR-009.
  AC2 (covers Profile/ModelRef/Model/MinPower/MaxPower/Harness) → all named above; ADR-009 documents each.
  AC3 (ModelRef fate + compat period) → removed entirely, no compat. Beads 1/4/5/6/7/9 implement; Bead 8 documents.
  AC4 (route-status + Execute behavior) → Beads 1, 4, 6; ErrNoLiveProvider enhancement in Bead 1 specifically addresses the "operator-visible error language" concern.
  AC5 (follow-up implementation beads) → the nine beads above.
  AC6 (go test not required) → N/A; decision-only.

This bead can be closed by the human reviewer once the decision above is acceptable. Implementation tracks via the nine listed beads.
title: routing: decide smart route semantics without model-ref workaround

description:
First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL. Write final_status=interrupted to in-flight
    cells (process_outcome=killed, no metrics). Exit non-zero.
  - Concurrent-cells state file at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json. Runner appends its cell at
    start under flock, reads current count, writes into cell, removes at close.
    Prune stale entries (PID dead) at next read.
  - Budget: compute cost_usd_at_run_time per cell from profile.pricing × token streams
    at close. Accumulate into <out>/budget.json. Halt before next cell when cap hit.
    Skipped cells get final_status=budget_halted placeholder with process_outcome=
    setup_failed and a 'note' field.
  - Per-cell retry: bounded exponential backoff for transient errors (connection
    refused, 5xx, EOF parse). Write attempt_of: link in the new cell; back-write
    superseded_by: into the prior cell (the one allowed mutation of a closed cell).

SCOPE — harbor-runner Docker image:
  - scripts/benchmark/Dockerfile.harbor-runner:
      FROM python:3.12-slim
      RUN pip install --no-cache-dir harbor-bench==<pinned-version>
      COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/ /app/harbor_adapters/
      COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py /app/harbor_agent.py
      ENV PYTHONPATH=/app
      WORKDIR /app
      ENTRYPOINT [\"harbor\"]
  - scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh: docker build with image-content-sha
    label computed from sha256 of harbor_adapters/ + harbor_agent.py +
    Dockerfile.harbor-runner. ./benchmark preflight triggers rebuild if cached image
    label doesn't match current sha.
  - Pre-PR-A spike (5 min): confirm `pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>` works. If
    Harbor is git-only, use `pip install git+https://github.com/laude-institute/...`.

SCOPE — shell adapters (8 harness-adapters + 1 task-executor):
  - scripts/benchmark/task-executors/harbor: bash wrapper around
      docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \\
        -v \"\$cell_dir:/output\" -v \"\$tasks_dir:/tasks:ro\" \\
        -e FIZEAU_API_KEY -e FIZEAU_BASE_URL -e FIZEAU_MODEL ... \\
        fizeau-harbor-runner:latest run \\
        --agent-import-path \"\$harbor_plugin\" --task-id \"\$task_id\" --output /output ...
    Reads task-spec.json from stdin (see CONTRACT.md). Writes cell_dir/result.json.
    Captures task_executor_version + harbor_runner_image_digest once per ./benchmark
    invocation into <out>/sweep.json; runner copies into each cell.
  - scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/{fiz, claude, codex, opencode, pi, cost-probe,
    noop, dumb-script}: bash ports of scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/*.py. Each
    is executable with two subcommands and a '# SUMMARY:' header line.
      <name> install <agent-artifact-path> -> install-spec.json on stdout with
        {install_command, artifact_source, binary_path, harbor_plugin} where
        harbor_plugin names the Python class Harbor loads (e.g.
        'scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.fiz:FizeauAgent').
      <name> command (reads profile JSON on stdin) -> command-spec.json on stdout
        with {command, env, secret_env_keys}.
    The current Python adapters at scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/{fiz,opencode,
    pi,cost_probe,noop,dumb_script}.py are the reference for what each emits.
    For claude/codex: today they're invoked via cmd/bench/matrix.go:1015-1025
    passing --agent-import-path; the shell adapters emit the equivalent install-spec.
  - scripts/benchmark/runtime-probe.sh: case-on profile.metadata.runtime, curl + jq
    against each runtime's HTTP endpoint, emit model_server JSON (name, version,
    commit, endpoint) for the cell schema. Handles backends: lucebox, llamacpp, vllm,
    omlx, ds4, rapid-mlx (one case arm each).
  - scripts/benchmark/task-executors/CONTRACT.md and harness-adapters/CONTRACT.md
    document the JSON shapes and the '# SUMMARY:' header convention.

SCOPE — agent leak fix (Go change):
  - internal/serviceimpl/execute_native.go:265 today reads:
      PlanningMode: req.PlanningMode || req.ToolPreset == \"benchmark\",
    Delete the `|| req.ToolPreset == \"benchmark\"` clause.
  - Add field `sampling.planning_mode: bool` (default false) to profile YAML schema.
    harness-adapters/fiz reads it and adds `--plan` to the command when true.
  - Pre-PR-A spike: `rg -l 'preset.*benchmark|FIZEAU_PRESET' scripts/benchmark/profiles/`
    to find profiles relying on the preset; add `sampling.planning_mode: true` to each.

SCOPE — Go runner deactivation:
  - Modify cmd/bench/main.go to short-circuit execution subcommands (matrix, sweep,
    run, plan) with a one-line redirect 'use ./benchmark — see scripts/benchmark/README.md'
    and exit 2. Listing subcommands (profiles, bench-sets) stay functional until PR F.

SCOPE — parity fixture capture (must happen BEFORE deactivation merges):
  - Before opening PR A's deactivation commit: run the Go runner against canary
    (sindri-lucebox + tb-2-1-canary, 3 reps). Commit 3 representative cell reports
    to scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner/.
  - During PR A: run the bash runner against the same canary. Commit cells to
    scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner/.
  - scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/diff.sh: bash + jq tool that reports field
    divergence outside an allowlist (cell-id, timestamps, paths, NEW fields like
    harbor_runner_image_digest/model_server.commit/etc.).

OUT OF SCOPE (file separate beads):
  - cell-report.schema.json (PR B)
  - tasks_from / task_executor bench-set schema (PR C)
  - Node collector (PR D)
  - Node importers (PR E)
  - deleting cmd/bench (PR F)
  - docs (PR G)

ROLLBACK: If interrupted, leave no half-owned state. cell-state.json sentinels
written by partial runs are fine — they're the recovery anchor. The runner script
itself is one file; partial commits get reverted.

ACCEPTANCE — all of these commands pass on a clean checkout:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --plan --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     prints the matrix, creates NO files, builds NO Docker images.
  2. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     runs the canary and produces cells under bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/cells/.
     Each cell has report.json with embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt,
     fiz.err, session/.
  3. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     --max-cost-usd 0.01 produces at least one cell with final_status=budget_halted.
  4. scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/diff.sh against committed go-runner fixtures
     is clean per allowlist.
  5. SIGTERM to a mid-sweep ./benchmark: in-flight cells marked final_status=
     interrupted, harbor-runner containers stopped via docker stop, runner exits
     non-zero. Verify with: ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     & sleep 5; kill -TERM \$! ; wait; jq .final_status bench/results/.../report.json
  6. fiz-bench matrix exits 2 with redirect message:
     fiz-bench matrix 2>&1 | grep -q 'use ./benchmark'; test \$? -eq 0
  7. grep -n 'ToolPreset == \"benchmark\"' internal/serviceimpl/execute_native.go
     returns nothing.

3. Explicit provider/model pins that name an above-max model must keep their existing validation/selection behavior or fail with a precise error; add coverage if behavior changes.
4. cd . && go test ./internal/routing/... -run "TestScorePolicy_MaxPowerExcludesOverpoweredCandidates|TestSoftPowerScoring_HealthySubscriptionBonusStopsBelowEffectiveMax|TestScorePowerMinPowerIsSoftAndPinsRemainConstraints" -count=1 passes.
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The routing reliability epic still requires MaxPower to exclude overpowered candidates when an in-bounds route exists. The current scorer only subtracts 1 point per power level above MaxPower, so a power-10 candidate can still beat a power-8 in-bounds candidate through other bonuses. That leaves --max-power as a weak preference instead of a reliable upper bound.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
internal/routing/score.go:266-269 applies only penalty := power - MaxPower for above-max candidates. service_routing.go:219-229 mirrors that weak public PowerHintFit value. Existing tests in internal/routing/score_power_test.go cover soft bounds and provider preference, but there is no TestScorePolicy_MaxPowerExcludesOverpoweredCandidates proving an above-max route cannot win while an in-bounds route is available.

PROPOSED FIX
Make MaxPower an exclusion-strength signal for automatic selection whenever at least one eligible in-bounds candidate exists, while preserving explicit pins and the existing MinPower soft-floor behavior. Add the named regression and adjust score evidence so above-max candidates show the exclusion/penalty clearly.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change MinPower soft-floor semantics. Do not change explicit provider/model pin behavior. Do not add another routing policy path or bead metadata input.
title: routing: enforce MaxPower as an exclusion when in-bounds routes exist

description:
Make the openai-compat provider's reasoning translator consult the
L1→L2→L3 wire-form chain from ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11:

  L1: live introspection (from the introspection-adapter bead
      fizeau-e80c156e — read once at provider construction, merged into
      ProtocolCapabilities)
  L2: catalog `reasoning_wire` field (from the schema bead
      fizeau-1567453f)
  L3: static ThinkingFormat per provider type (today's behavior)

Per the amendment, this bead also fixes two pre-existing translator bugs
discovered in the 2026-05-11 probing session and adds one new wire format.

Three coordinated changes:

### 1. L1/L2/L3 lookup chain

Signature change: `openRouterReasoningOptions(policy)` becomes
`openRouterReasoningOptions(policy, model, effectiveWire)`. The
`reasoningRequestOptions` caller (around openai.go:340) consults in
order:
  - introspection.SupportedRequestParams + introspection.AliasMap (L1)
  - modelReasoningWireFor(model) (L2)
  - p.thinkingWireFormat() default (L3)
and passes the resolved wire choice into format-specific helpers.

Tier↔tokens conversion via PortableBudgets:
  - caller passed KindNamed, wire wants tokens: PortableBudgets[name]
  - caller passed KindTokens, wire wants tier: snap to nearest
    PortableBudgets tier rounding UP on ties (4096 -> medium because
    4096 is the midpoint of 2048+8192).

### 2. Fix ThinkingWireFormatQwen to use chat_template_kwargs envelope

Verified 2026-05-11 against sindri-llamacpp: top-level enable_thinking
is silently dropped (4 completion tokens, no reasoning). The
chat_template_kwargs envelope variant produces 250-526 tokens with
reasoning extracted.

The current code at openai.go:421-425 emits:
  option.WithJSONSet(\"enable_thinking\", true)
  option.WithJSONSet(\"thinking_budget\", thinkingBudget)

The new code must emit:
  option.WithJSONSet(\"chat_template_kwargs\",
      map[string]interface{}{
          \"enable_thinking\": true,
          \"thinking_budget\": thinkingBudget,
      })

Off-path (openai.go:391-394) similarly moves from top-level to kwargs:
  chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking = false

The per-request `reasoning_format` override is OPTIONAL — sindri's
server-side default is `auto` which handles extraction correctly. Only
emit `reasoning_format` if introspection (L1) reported a different
server-side default and we want to override. v1 leaves the field
unset; revisit if needed.

### 3. Add ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort for ds4

New constant in
`internal/provider/openai/protocol_support.go`:
  ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort ThinkingWireFormat = \"openai_effort\"

Wire shape: flat top-level `reasoning_effort: \"<tier>\"`. Disable form:
top-level `think: false` (per ds4 docs, not `reasoning_effort: \"none\"`).

In openai.go reasoningRequestOptions add branches for OpenAIEffort:
  - off: option.WithJSONSet(\"think\", false)
  - explicit named tier: option.WithJSONSet(\"reasoning_effort\", string(tier))
  - tokens budget on an effort wire: snap via PortableBudgets nearest tier

Provider capability change: at `internal/provider/ds4/ds4.go` set
`ProtocolCapabilities.ThinkingFormat = ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort`.
This is the static L3 default; L1 introspection will confirm at
construction time.

### Test plan

Update internal/provider/openai/openai_test.go with:
  - reasoning=low + wire=tokens (L2) emits `reasoning.max_tokens: 2048`
  - reasoning=4096 + wire=effort (L2) emits `reasoning.effort: \"medium\"`
    (round-up rule on tie)
  - reasoning=high + wire=effort (L2) emits `reasoning.effort: \"high\"`
  - reasoning=high + wire=provider (L3 fallback) emits today's
    `effort: \"high\"` (backwards compat regression check)
  - reasoning=low + wire=effort + ds4 alias map (L1) emits
    `reasoning_effort: \"high\"` (low aliases to high per /props)
  - Qwen wire: reasoning=4096 emits chat_template_kwargs envelope, NOT
    top-level
  - Qwen wire off: reasoning=off emits chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking
    = false
  - ds4 wire off: reasoning=off emits think:false (not reasoning_effort)
  - Recorded-fixture regression: byte-identical wire output for the
    pre-amendment OR + Qwen + ThinkingMap paths when wire=\"\" (L3 only).

Add a structured-warning log assertion when L2 overrides L1 (mirrors
ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal pattern).

In-scope files:
- internal/provider/openai/openai.go (translator + helper)
- internal/provider/openai/protocol_support.go (add OpenAIEffort constant)
- internal/provider/openai/openai_test.go
- internal/provider/ds4/ds4.go (switch ThinkingFormat to OpenAIEffort)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/llamaserver.go (audit: should already be
  Qwen format; verify caps unchanged)

  NearestTierForTokens helpers if not already public)

acceptance: All seven child beads (PR A through PR G) closed. verify-cleanup.sh in scripts/benchmark/ exits 0 on a fresh checkout.
description:
EPIC parent for the benchmark runner rewrite. Goal: collapse the bench stack to two
authored runtimes (bash on the host runner, Node off it) with no host Python install
required for any benchmark operation. Today the bench stack has Go (cmd/bench/ ~12k
LOC, cmd/benchscore/, internal/benchmark/), Python on the host (Harbor + harbor_agent +
harbor_adapters + scripts/benchmark/*.py utilities), and Python again in the website
collector (scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py). Target: bash runner + Node collector,
with Harbor + harbor_adapters/*.py + harbor_agent.py running inside a fizeau-harbor-runner
Docker image (built from scripts/benchmark/Dockerfile.harbor-runner) so the host never
imports Python.

FOUR GOALS:
  1. Clean separation of runner from agent (agent is a CLI subprocess across a contract).
  2. Simple instantiation: ./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B.
  3. Capture every potentially impactful dimension (strict per-cell schema validated at write).
  4. Easy extension to new tasks/suites/harnesses via two shell contracts
     (scripts/benchmark/task-executors/<name> and scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/<name>).

RUNTIME CONSTRAINT (load-bearing):
  Host: bash + Node + Docker + jq/yq/curl/flock/xargs/setsid. NO Python, NO host Harbor,
  NO host Go for bench operation. Python lives ONLY inside the fizeau-harbor-runner image.
  This constraint applies to authored bench code; fiz being Go is fine, Docker being a
  runtime is fine, DuckDB CLI being available is fine.

PR SEQUENCE (file each as separate bead, wire deps):
  PR A: bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + --preset benchmark fix
  PR B: per-cell report.json strict schema + Node validator + CI gate
  PR C: bench-set tasks_from: + task_executor: fields (fixes 'tasks: all' misdesign)
  PR D: Node collector + orphan-profile alias + _archived/ profiles + legacy audit
  PR E: Node evidence importers (terminal-bench, beadbench, external)
  PR F: hard cutover — delete cmd/bench, cmd/benchscore, internal/benchmark, Python
        utilities, legacy benchmark-results/, ship verify-cleanup.sh and host-purity check
  PR G: docs cleanup

Dependencies: A -> {B, C} -> D -> E -> F -> G. B and C parallelizable after A.

VERIFICATION CLIFF GATE (must pass before PR F merges):
  1. End-to-end shadow run: bash-runner output diff'd against Go-runner fixtures
     committed during PR A (canary against sindri-lucebox, 3 reps).
  2. Profile smoke matrix: ./benchmark works for one local, one managed, one
     wrapper-harness profile.
  3. Full-tree collector round-trip: every cell in bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/cells/
     validates, resolves via orphan alias, or appears in audit.json with a reason.
  4. Website rebuild equivalence (semantic, not byte-identical).
  5. Host-purity check: ./benchmark runs inside a pre-built test image with no Python.
  6. verify-cleanup.sh returns 0 (vestige detection script committed in PR F).
  7. CI wiring: all of the above as separate steps in .github/workflows/ci.yml.

WHAT'S ALREADY IN PLACE (don't redo):
  - Cells self-describing (ADR-016 shipped, profile embedded in report.json)
  - Profile YAMLs at scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml
  - Bench-set YAMLs at scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/<id>.yaml
  - Concurrency-groups at scripts/benchmark/concurrency-groups.yaml
  - capture-machine-info.sh
  - Agent black-box contract (runner subprocess-invokes fiz, records command + env)
  - harbor_adapters/*.py + harbor_agent.py (KEPT — they move into the harbor-runner image)
  - Aggregated cell schema at docs/benchmarks/schema/benchmark-cells.schema.json

acceptance: Both child beads (preflight smoke test + matrix runner consecutive-failure halt) are closed. A test sweep launched against a deliberately misconfigured lane (e.g. profile with sampling param the provider rejects) terminates in <60s with a clear error naming the lane and the failing parameter, instead of writing N cells of identical failures.
description:
Two-failure pattern from 2026-05-10 sweeps where a single misconfigured lane wasted significant compute before any human noticed:

(1) sindri-llamacpp lane: profile had `reasoning: low` set, but fiz's llama-server provider rejects that param. Every cell in the lane failed in ~47s with the same `agent: provider error: openai: reasoning="low" is not supported by provider type "llama-server"` error. The matrix runner kept marching through all 89 tasks × 3 reps without bailing. Caught after 119 cells (~93 minutes of churn).

(2) sindri-vllm lane (same evening): the vLLM model server entered a degraded state where /v1/models responded but /v1/chat/completions hung indefinitely. Each cell wasted its full agent budget (~45 min) waiting for a single llm.request that never produced an llm.response.

Common shape: same misconfiguration / failure causing every cell in a lane to fail identically. Today there's no mechanism to detect "this lane is not going to work, stop trying."

Two child beads address this in priority order:

- [primary] preflight smoke test — one HTTP completion per lane before any matrix runner spawns, fails the sweep with a clear diagnostic if any lane 's endpoint can't produce 1 token in 30s with the lane's sampling params. Catches both failure modes above plus auth/missing-model/connection-refused at startup, with O(N_lanes) cost not O(N_lanes × N_cells).

- [defense in depth] matrix runner consecutive-failure halt — track identical-fingerprint failures and abort the lane with an explicit `lane_aborted` status after N=5 in a row. Catches mid-sweep regressions where preflight passed but the endpoint dies later.

Out of scope: the more invasive fiz-side approach (distinct EX_CONFIG exit code) — lower bang for buck since it still wastes one cell per lane and the request-shape vs transient classification is fragile.
notes: Review 2026-05-12 acceptance re-read: closable. The epic acceptance is the fail-fast behavior: preflight catches a deliberately misconfigured lane before cell fanout, and the matrix runner halts lanes after consecutive identical failures. Verified scripts/benchmark/test_preflight_lane.bash passed, and go test ./cmd/bench -run 'TestConsecutiveFailureHalt|TestPreflight|TestSweepShutdownTermGraceful' -count=1 passed. The open child fizeau-b76d6a2c is a separate shutdown/reaping reliability issue; its current shell fixture waits on a fake Harbor startup log and is too timing-sensitive to be a blocker for this fail-fast epic.
title: EPIC: bench — fail fast on misconfigured/dead lanes

acceptance:
1. The repository has one canonical benchmark layout after consolidation: preferred `bench/` containing the benchmark docs, corpus/data, scorer source, runner entrypoint, and gitignored results output; if another scheme is chosen, the reason is documented in `bench/README.md`.
2. All live references to the old names are updated or intentionally documented as historical/generated data: search for `bench-fixed`, `benchscore`, `benchmark-results`, and root-level `benchmark` references and verify no stale live command, import, script, or doc path remains.
3. `CHANGELOG.md` records the benchmark layout rename under `[Unreleased]`.
4. Focused benchmark package tests pass at their final paths, including the scorer tests that cover `TestScoreTaskResultsJSONL`, `TestClarificationMessageHeuristics`, and `TestBenchscoreCommandName` if those symbols still exist after the move.
5. `go build ./...` passes from the repository root.
6. `go test ./... -count=1` passes from the repository root.
7. A cheap benchmark runner smoke check passes without launching a large sweep, either via the final runner `--help`/dry-run mode or an existing focused test such as `go test ./cmd/bench -run TestSweepDryRunCanaryPrints -count=1` after paths are updated.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The Fizeau repo root exposes several benchmark-related names that do not describe their roles consistently:
- `benchmark` is a root shell wrapper for `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh` (`benchmark:1-5`).
- `bench/` is the documented benchmark area, but currently contains only README/corpus/results layout guidance (`bench/README.md:1-49`).
- `benchscore/` is a root Go package whose scorer code starts at `benchscore/score.go:1`.
- `benchmark-results/` is benchmark output and is already covered by ignore rules (`.gitignore:58-71`).
- `CHANGELOG.md:6` has the unreleased section where this repository-layout cleanup should be recorded.

The previous acceptance criteria included `cd cli && go build ./...`, but Fizeau has no `cli/` directory. That gate is DDx-specific and invalid for this repository.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`benchmark:1-5` keeps the runner as a top-level generic script, `benchscore/score.go:1` keeps scorer source outside the bench area, and `bench/README.md:1-49` already presents `bench/` as the benchmark documentation home. The naming split makes source, corpus, runner, and output look like unrelated root-level concepts.

PROPOSED FIX
Pick one canonical Fizeau-native naming scheme and migrate references. Preferred: consolidate under `bench/` with documented roles such as corpus/data, scorer, runner script, and gitignored results output. A consistent `bench-*` top-level scheme is acceptable only if the implementation documents why it is safer. Update scripts, package imports, docs, ignore rules, and changelog entries so old names are not left as live references.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change benchmark behavior, output schemas, Terminal-Bench corpus contents, provider/model lanes, result retention policy, or historical generated result files except for path/name updates needed by the rename.

DEPS
Do not start until `fizeau-1e5bcee1` is closed; the queue already encodes that dependency.
title: repo: consolidate bench/benchscore/benchmark naming and prune empty bench dirs

acceptance: 1. New fixture graded-fail-retry-spam.json + test asserts invalid_provider on turns=6 + input_tokens=0 + output_tokens=0 + had_llm_request=true + tmw=nil. 2. reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run flags the 70+ historical vidar 2026-05-11 cells as transitioning to invalid_provider. 3. After apply, those cells are excluded from vidar's pass-rate denominator. 4. Subsequent sweep with --retry-invalid re-runs them when the endpoint is back up.
description:
fiz retries transient provider errors (connection refused, EOF, 5xx) internally up to ~5 times with exponential backoff. Each retry attempt counts as a turn in the matrix, inflating the turns counter even when zero useful work happened. Result: cells where the provider was completely unreachable end up with turns=6 (5 retries), output_tokens=0, terminated_mid_work=nil, but `matrixHasMeaningfulAttempt(report)` returns true because turns > 0 — bypassing the new invalid_provider classifier rule introduced in 6688326e (fizeau-e874633d).

Concrete incident: on 2026-05-11 ~15:25Z, vidar:1235 oMLX server crashed. 70 cells silently failed before detection. fiz.txt showed:
  2026/05/11 15:29:41 WARN provider error, retrying attempt=1 err="unexpected EOF"
  2026/05/11 15:29:42 WARN provider error, retrying attempt=2 err="connect: connection refused"
  ... etc through attempt=5
Each cell ended with turns=6, wall=60-200s, final_status=graded_fail. Classifier left them as plain graded_fail — i.e. "the model attempted and failed" — polluting vidar's pass-rate denominator with what was actually 100% provider unavailability.

The hole is structural: hasMeaningfulAttempt currently looks at turns/tokens/tool_calls > 0. fiz's retry counter is one of the inputs to that signal, but a retry isn't a meaningful attempt — it's the absence of one.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a stronger "no real attempt" signal that distinguishes retry-spam from real turns. Two complementary options:

A. **fiz records retry attempts separately** from successful llm.request/response pairs. The session jsonl would carry an `llm.retry` event (or annotate llm.request with an `attempt` field). The matrix runner already reads the session jsonl in readSessionSignalsFromCell — it can extend the count to distinguish "had_completed_response" from "had_request". Then: graded_fail + !had_completed_response + had_request → invalid_provider, regardless of turns count.

B. **Token-based fallback in the existing classifier**: even if turns > 0, if input_tokens=0 AND output_tokens=0 AND had_llm_request=true AND tmw=nil, classify as invalid_provider. The intuition: a real turn consumes prompt tokens (input_tokens > 0 from session.end usage). All-zero token counts means no completed round-trip ever happened. Cheaper than (A) but a bit more heuristic.

In-scope files for Part B (simpler, ships first):
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — extend the case "graded_fail" branch with the token-based check before the !hasAttempt branch.
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid_test.go — fixture: graded-fail-retry-spam.json with turns=6, input_tokens=0, output_tokens=0, had_llm_request=true, tmw=nil → expects invalid_provider.
- scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py — mirror.
- Run reclassifier; expect ~70+ historical vidar cells from 2026-05-11 to flip invalid_setup/empty → invalid_provider.

In-scope for Part A (deeper, file as separate bead if simple fix isn't enough):
- internal/core/loop.go — distinguish "request attempted but provider error retried" from "completed request/response pair" in event emission. Add an `attempt` field on llm.request or a new event type.
- scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py FizeauAgent.parse_telemetry — count completed responses (not just requests) when populating turns/tokens fields.
- cmd/bench/matrix.go readSessionSignalsFromCell — return n_completed_responses alongside the existing tmw/had_llm_request signals.

ACCEPTANCE IS A COMMAND

1. go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestClassifyMatrixInvalid passes with the new fixture graded-fail-retry-spam.json asserting invalid_provider.
2. .venv-report/bin/python scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run flags the cells from the 2026-05-11 vidar incident (70+ cells) as transitioning to invalid_provider.
3. Apply pass leaves those cells excluded from vidar's pass-rate denominator and tagged for --retry-invalid reruns.

WHY THIS MATTERS

Without this, every server-side outage (oMLX crash, vLLM hang, network blip) gets attributed to model quality instead of provider availability. Pass@k denominators include hundreds of cells where the model never actually got to try. The defense-in-depth piece (fizeau-72d18004 consecutive-failure halt in the matrix runner) catches this at sweep-execution time, but classification matters even retroactively for accurate reporting.
title: bench: classifier misses retry-spam cells (turns inflated by fiz internal retries on connection errors)

description:
PROBLEM
  v0.11 is a breaking redesign of the routing surface (Profile→Policy, drop ModelRef, drop targets, drop aliases, schema v5, billing classification, default-deny pay-per-token via include_by_default). The governing specifications (CONTRACT-003, FEAT-003/004/005/006, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG, docs/routing/) currently describe the v0.10 surface and would mislead any reader after v0.11 lands. There is no ADR documenting the redesign decision.

ROOT CAUSE
  Specs accumulate during normal feature work. A breaking redesign of this magnitude needs a single ADR that captures the decision context and consequences, plus a coordinated rewrite of the touched specs so reviewers can assess whether the code matches the intent.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - New artifact: ADR-009-routing-surface-redesign.md documenting the rename, removal, and schema decisions. Cross-references and partially supersedes ADR-005, ADR-006. Does not affect ADR-007 (sampling profiles unchanged).
  - Full rewrite: CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md to reflect the new FizeauService shape (~14 methods, PolicyInfo type, dropped ResolveProfile/ProfileAliases/ModelRef).
  - Full rewrite: FEAT-004-model-routing.md (policies, soft min/max, allow_local, require[], pin precedence rules).
  - Full rewrite: FEAT-003-providers.md (include_by_default, billing classification, default-deny).
  - Full rewrite: FEAT-006-standalone-cli.md (new flags, new subcommands, removed flags).
  - Updates: FEAT-005-logging-and-cost.md (session-log schema break, model-level cost attribution).
  - Sweeps: docs/routing/profiles.md → rename to docs/routing/policies.md with rewrite; index.md, best-provider.md, override-precedence.md.
  - Update: AGENTS.md routing references, top-level CHANGELOG.
  - Mark: ADR-005 and ADR-006 with "Superseded in part by ADR-009" header (no full rewrite — historical record).
  - Out of scope: docs/research/* (frozen), historical alignment reviews from before 2026-04-25, ADR-007.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md (full rewrite needed)
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-providers.md, FEAT-004-model-routing.md, FEAT-005-logging-and-cost.md, FEAT-006-standalone-cli.md
  - docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-005-smart-routing-replaces-model-routes.md, ADR-006-overrides-as-routing-failure-signals.md

description:
Port the three Go evidence importers in cmd/bench/ to Node, in the collector package.

CONTEXT: cmd/bench/ today has three import paths totaling ~2660 LOC of Go that read
upstream benchmark evidence and reshape it into our cell schema:
  - cmd/bench/terminalbench_import.go (975 LOC) — TerminalBench upstream evidence
  - cmd/bench/beadbench_import.go (738 LOC) — beadbench JSONL
  - cmd/bench/external_import.go (948 LOC) — curated external leaderboard exports
Plus cmd/bench/external_termbench.go (309 LOC) helpers.

These are all JSON-in / JSON-out translators. They're not load-bearing for execution,
they're load-bearing for analytics — comparing our cells against upstream evidence.
The two-runtime constraint says no Python and no Go in the bench tree, so they move
to Node, sized way smaller than the Go versions because most of the LOC is JSON
struct boilerplate that Node doesn't need.

DEPENDS ON: PR D (importers ship in the collector package; share its walker shim).

SCOPE — extend scripts/benchmark/collector/:
  - collector/import-terminal-bench.ts: reads upstream TerminalBench evidence packs.
    Reference: cmd/bench/terminalbench_import.go for input schema. Output: cells
    matching cell-report.schema.json (with profile resolved from a synthetic
    'external' profile per upstream agent identity, embedded via the same shim PR D's
    walker uses).
  - collector/import-beadbench.ts: reads beadbench JSONL streams. Reference:
    cmd/bench/beadbench_import.go.
  - collector/import-external.ts: reads curated external leaderboard exports.
    Reference: cmd/bench/external_import.go.
  - Each importer is exposed via the collector CLI:
      node collector/bin/cli.js import terminal-bench <input.json> --out <cells-dir>
      node collector/bin/cli.js import beadbench <input.jsonl> --out <cells-dir>
      node collector/bin/cli.js import external <input.json> --out <cells-dir>
  - Target size: <600 LOC Node total across all three importers (vs ~2660 LOC Go).
  - Each importer writes cells to a NEW canonical root by default (e.g.
    bench/results/external/terminal-bench-2-1/cells/...) NOT into the active
    fiz-tools-v1 tree, to avoid commingling external evidence with our own runs.
  - For each output cell, populate report.json with as many cell-report.schema.json
    fields as the upstream evidence supplies; mark non-supplied fields explicitly
    null so the schema's nullable types pass validation.

IN-SCOPE files:
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/import-terminal-bench.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/import-beadbench.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/import-external.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/cli.js (extend with 'import' subcommand dispatch)

OUT OF SCOPE:
  - Deletion of the Go importers (PR F).
  - Modifying cell-report.schema.json (PR B).
  - The website data feed (PR D handles cells.json).

ROLLBACK: importers are new code, separate files. Reversible.

     cells match cmd/bench's terminalbench_import output by content (cell-id may
