description:
CONTEXT
The routing engine already scores with power, cost, quota, locality, utilization, performance, context headroom, cooldown, and sticky affinity. SD-005 names the public evidence components as `power_weighted_capability`, `power_hint_fit`, `latency_weight`, `placement_bonus`, `quota_bonus`, `marginal_cost_penalty`, `availability_penalty`, and `stale_signal_penalty`. Today those facts are present indirectly through buckets such as `power`, `cost`, `quota_health`, and `performance`, which makes route explanations harder to compare to the design.

CHANGE
Align public route candidate score component evidence with SD-005 vocabulary while preserving existing route-order behavior unless a test explicitly documents a deliberate change. Keep typed filter reasons separate from scoring. Power hints must remain asymmetric: being below `MinPower` is penalized more strongly than being above `MaxPower`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/routing/score.go
- internal/routing/*score*_test.go or routing preference tests
- service_routing.go candidate component mapping
- service_events.go if event JSON mirrors component names

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Snapshot assembly.
- Rewriting eligibility gates.
- Changing public request field names.
title: routing: align score components with SD-005 evidence names

description:
One-shot script that walks benchmark-results/**/cells/, embeds resolved profile metadata into every cell's report.json, and reorganizes paths to the new framework/task/cell-id layout.

For each cell at benchmark-results/<canonical>/cells/<framework-dataset>/<task>/<profile_id>/rep-NNN/report.json:
1. Read existing profile_id and profile_snapshot.
2. Resolve to a current scripts/benchmark/profiles/*.yaml. Apply PROFILE_ALIASES rules from scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py:150 area for renamed profiles.
3. For the 4 deleted profile IDs, recover the YAML from git history (the commit that deleted it):
   - sindri-club-3090
   - vidar-qwen3-6-27b-openai-compat
   - sindri-club-3090-llamacpp
   - gpt-5-3-mini
   Use: 'git log --diff-filter=D --name-only --pretty=format: -- scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml | head -1' to find the deleting commit, then 'git show <commit>~1:scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml' to recover.
4. Embed the resolved profile into the cell's report.json under a 'profile' key.
5. Add 'framework' and 'dataset' fields from the path's <framework-dataset> segment.
6. Mint 'cell_id' from the cell's started_at timestamp plus a random suffix.
7. Move the cell to benchmark-results/<canonical>/cells/<framework>-<dataset>/<task>/<cell-id>/ (drop profile_id and rep-NNN from path).
8. If profile is irrecoverable: 'rm -r' the cell directory; log to a manifest.

Idempotent: re-running on already-backfilled cells (those with cell.profile.* populated) is a no-op skip.

description:
PROBLEM
After the release-blocker CI fixes landed on master, GitHub CI failed in the Rename noise check step with `rename-noise: bufio.Scanner: token too long`. The gate aborts before reporting whether old-name hits exist, so a large tracked text line can block release unrelated to rename policy.

ROOT CAUSE
internal/renamecheck/renamecheck.go scans candidate files with bufio.Scanner and a 1 MiB max token. The repository includes tracked external benchmark fixtures with lines longer than that limit, and CI runs the gate over git-tracked text candidates.

Make renamecheck handle long text lines deterministically without weakening the old-name rules. Add regression coverage for a line exceeding the old Scanner token limit and keep make rename-noise-check green.

description:
Replace cmd/bench sweep execution with a thin scripts/benchmark/benchmark shell driver. Per the 2026-05-15 plan, execution moves out of Go to a shell script that invokes fiz directly (or Harbor + FizeauAgent for TerminalBench cells). cmd/bench survives only as analytics tooling (aggregate, import-evidence, validate).

Operator contract:
  ./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B           # run
  ./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --plan    # validate + print matrix, no side effects
  ./benchmark validate                            # fast offline validation
  ./benchmark preflight --profile P               # endpoint probe (explicit)
  ./benchmark profiles                            # list
  ./benchmark bench-sets                          # list

Rules (from spec):
1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls, no preflight, no benchmark-results/ dir creation.
2. Cell directory created only when cell is about to start.
3. --profile and --bench-set both required (no implicit defaults).
4. validate is fast and offline (schema + cross-reference only).
5. preflight is the only command that probes endpoints.
6. Manifest reading: one Go helper (e.g. 'fiz-bench plan --json' or 'fiz-bench manifest --json') emits JSON; shell consumes JSON, never YAML.

Concurrency: shell reads profile.concurrency_group, looks up scripts/benchmark/concurrency-groups.yaml, enforces semaphore via GNU parallel or background-job counter.

TerminalBench cells: shell invokes Harbor with FizeauAgent (scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py), forwarding profile-derived env. Simple prompt cells: shell invokes fiz directly with profile-derived flags.

Resume: 'cell exists with terminal final_status in {pass, fail, timeout} -> skip' unless --force-rerun. 'final_status == invalid' -> skip unless --retry-invalid.

Old fiz-bench sweep execution path continues to work until PR 4.

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/benchmark (new shell script)
- cmd/bench/plan.go or cmd/bench/manifest.go (new Go helper that emits JSON)
- cmd/bench/main.go (register the new subcommand)
- cmd/bench/testdata/plans/ (golden plan fixtures for regression guard)
- cmd/bench/sweep_test.go or new plan_test.go (parity fixtures)

Out-of-scope:
- Deleting cmd/bench/lanes*.go or sweep.go (PR 4)
- Backfill of existing cells (PR 2)
- Analytics switch (PR 3)

description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau work watchers keep dirtying the main checkout by refreshing DDx liveness state. `git status --short` shows `.ddx/run-state.json` modified while `ddx work --watch` is running, even though this state is operational coordination data and should not be committed every heartbeat.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`.gitignore:33-34` already ignores `.ddx/run-state.json` and `.ddx/run-state/`, but those legacy files are still in the git index (`.ddx/run-state.json:1` and `.ddx/run-state/20260515T145312-3f9f0cd7.json:1`). Because tracked files bypass ignore rules, every live worker refresh writes a tracked path and makes the repo dirty.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove the legacy run-state files from the git index with `git rm --cached -r .ddx/run-state.json .ddx/run-state`, leaving the local ignored files on disk for live workers. Do not delete execution evidence or tracker state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau product code or DDx runtime behavior in this bead. Do not alter bead records except to close this hygiene cleanup.
title: repo hygiene: untrack legacy DDx run-state files

description:
PROBLEM
  internal/config/config.go user-side ProviderConfig has no include_by_default field, has DefaultModelRef in RoutingConfig, has ModelRef in BackendPoolConfig and ProviderOverrides, and routes through a model-ref → catalog.Resolve pipeline that the v0.11 surface no longer supports.

ROOT CAUSE
  Config schema accreted ModelRef threading when model-ref was the canonical routing input. With ModelRef removed (Phase 4), those fields are dead. Per-provider include_by_default is the v0.11 mechanism for default-deny on per-token providers (Phase 2 catalog ships the defaults; user config overrides per-provider).

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - ProviderConfig gains IncludeByDefault *bool. Pointer so unset means "use catalog default"; set means "override catalog default for this user".
  - User config CAN add provider entries with Type values not in the catalog's known set, but MUST declare Billing explicitly (per_token | subscription | fixed). Catalog-known types inherit billing from BillingForProviderSystem.
  - DefaultModelRef field in RoutingConfig: REMOVED. ModelRef field in BackendPoolConfig and ProviderOverrides: REMOVED.
  - Concrete-model selection chain after this phase: overrides.Model → BackendPool.Model → ProviderConfig.Model. No catalog-ref expansion in the config layer.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - internal/config/config.go:38-68 ProviderConfig (no IncludeByDefault, no Billing)
  - internal/config/config.go:140-178 RoutingConfig (DefaultModelRef field at line 147)
  - internal/config/config.go:182-193 BackendPoolConfig (ModelRef at line 185)
  - internal/config/config.go:196-200 ProviderOverrides (ModelRef at line 198)
  - internal/config/config.go:686-720, 728-740, 837-870 ResolveProviderConfig, BuildProviderWithOverrides, ResolveBackend (model-ref → catalog.Resolve pipeline)

PROPOSED FIX
  1. ProviderConfig: add IncludeByDefault *bool yaml:"include_by_default,omitempty"; add Billing string yaml:"billing,omitempty" (validated: required when Type unknown to catalog).
  2. RoutingConfig: drop DefaultModelRef field.
  3. BackendPoolConfig: drop ModelRef field.
  4. ProviderOverrides: drop ModelRef field.
  5. Rewrite ResolveProviderConfig, BuildProviderWithOverrides, ResolveBackend: drop the modelref→catalog.Resolve path; concrete model selection becomes overrides.Model → bc.Model → pc.Model with no catalog expansion.
  6. Catalog stays in scope only for cat.LookupModel(id) lookups for power/billing/context-window enrichment.
  7. Effective IncludeByDefault resolution: if user-config value set (pointer non-nil), use it; else use catalog default (cat.Provider(name).IncludeByDefault).
  8. Validator: if user-config provider's Type is not in catalog's known set AND Billing is empty, fail with "provider %q: type %q is not catalog-known; declare billing field explicitly (per_token | subscription | fixed)".

NON-SCOPE
  - Catalog YAML providers block — Phase 3.
  - Public service surface — Phase 4.
  - CLI flags — Phase 6.

PARENT + DEPS
  Depends on Phase 2 (catalog Provider type/methods).

description:
PROBLEM
The credit probe in [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]] can fail in two distinct ways that operators need to distinguish:
- 401 from `/api/v1/credits` → the key is set but rejected (e.g. revoked, typo'd). This is different from "never set a key" because the fix is different (rotate vs configure).
- Transport failure (DNS, TCP, TLS, 5xx) → openrouter.ai is unreachable. This is transient and we must not hard-exclude openrouter forever, or one bad minute permanently demotes the provider.

Without this distinction operators cannot triage; without fail-open recovery a transient blip causes flapping or sustained mis-routing.

ROOT CAUSE
The probe path introduced by [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]] needs explicit failure-classification branches and a freshness-window policy. The error handling for the openrouter credits roundtripper response must inspect status code and error type, then write a typed `filter_reason` into the cache rather than a generic error string. Exact integration point: the cache-write call in the credit-probe routine.

PROPOSED FIX
1. **401 from /credits** → cache `filter_reason: "credential_invalid"` (distinct from `"credential_missing"` emitted by [[openrouter-credential-presence-gate]]). Candidate `eligible: false`. Operators reading routing_decision can tell "forgot to set" from "key rejected".
2. **Transport error / non-401 5xx** → cache `filter_reason: "provider_unreachable"`. Candidate `eligible: false` for the current freshness window ONLY — do not hard-exclude. Next scheduled probe pass attempts a fresh call; on success the candidate returns to `eligible: true` automatically.
3. **Fail-open semantics**: a `provider_unreachable` cache entry must NOT be sticky beyond the probe interval. After the next probe attempt the cache reflects the new state (success or another failure-class). No manual operator intervention required to recover.
4. **Evidence in routing_decision**: each filter_reason carries the originating HTTP status code (or transport error class) in the candidate evidence body so the operator does not have to grep logs.

NON-SCOPE
- Credential presence/format checks (handled by [[openrouter-credential-presence-gate]]).
- The credit balance probe and its caching layer itself (handled by [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]]).
- Retry-with-backoff against openrouter.ai on transport failure during the probe — the probe is best-effort; the next scheduled pass is the retry.
- Multi-account credential rotation / failover (parent non-scope, preserved).
- Per-model credit-cost forecasting (parent non-scope, preserved).
- DDx-side changes per CONTRACT-003 (parent non-scope, preserved).

CROSS-REF
Sibling beads: [[openrouter-credential-presence-gate]], [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]]. Cross-ref fizeau-80d4bf41.
title: openrouter: probe failure-mode handling (credential_invalid vs provider_unreachable, fail-open recovery)

acceptance:
1. The router selects a healthy subscription/cloud route when configured local providers are all unreachable within one routing decision (no per-bead-attempt 30s timeout). Integration test: TestRouter_DeadLocalProvider_FallsThroughToSubscription.
2. With --min-power 8 set, a free subscription candidate at power 8 outscores a metered candidate at power 10 of the same harness family under the default policy. Unit test: TestScorePolicy_MinPowerPreservesSubscriptionPreference.
3. With --max-power 8 set, candidates above power 8 are not just penalized weakly (current (power-max)*1) but visibly excluded from selection when an in-bounds candidate exists. Unit test: TestScorePolicy_MaxPowerExcludesOverpoweredCandidates.
4. When literally every configured provider's first-dial check fails AND no subscription route has quota, the router returns no_viable_provider within (number_of_providers × 5s) — not multiplied. Integration test: TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast.
5. RouteDecision evidence (per service_routing.go RouteDecision shape) includes a per-candidate scoring breakdown with the components map from scoreComponents() so operators can audit any single route decision.
6. Existing routing tests in internal/routing/ and service_routing_test.go all green: go test ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes the auto-router reliability fix and points at this bead.
description:
GOAL
Fizeau's auto-router must reliably select an executable route whenever ANY configured provider is reachable and within quota. Today the drain stalls or routes to dead hosts even when subscription/cloud providers are healthy. Caller (DDx) should receive provider_connectivity / no_viable_provider ONLY when literally every configured provider is unavailable.

OBSERVED FAILURE MODES (from a 27-bead harness-interface refactor drain on 2026-05-14)
1. Policy-scoring disabled under bounds. When --min-power or --max-power is set, internal/routing/score.go:60 and :108 skip the policy-aware preference block entirely. A free subscription candidate at the floor power loses to a metered candidate at higher power purely due to the absent policy bonus. Tracked in child bead fizeau-dc3cf359.

2. Dead-host route loop without cross-provider escalation. When the smart-routing scorer ranks fiz/bragi/qwen3.5-27b above claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 (because the default-policy local-deployment bias is winning), and bragi is unreachable, the request fails after exhausting bragi's ports (1234, 8020) and grendel's ports (1234, 1235, 8000) within a single bead-attempt, then errors with provider_connectivity — even though claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 is sitting at score 186 in fiz route-status. Cross-provider escalation needs to fall through to subscription/cloud once local providers fail.

3. Cross-process route-health amnesia. Each new ddx-try process spawns a fresh in-process routehealth.Store. Bragi being unreachable was learned by the previous attempt and persisted into the bead's work-failed-routes (via fizeau-8134d6db + ddx-71629938), but the *next* bead's first dial still goes to bragi for 5s before falling back. Aggregate cost: ~10–20s of dial timeouts per fresh ddx-try. Tracked separately in fizeau-7cd66e6b (pending ADR-012 carve-out decision).

4. Score for the same candidate varies wildly between bounded and unbounded queries. fiz route-status shows claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 at score 186 (no bounds, default policy); the same candidate scores ~110 with --min-power 8. That delta is the missing policy bonus. Tracked in fizeau-dc3cf359.

CONTRACT (what 'reliable' means)
- Given a healthy provider configured in fizeau.config.yaml — local OR subscription OR openrouter — and a request without an explicit provider pin, the router MUST select a route that succeeds. Provider liveness is determined by route-health signal + first-attempt dial.
- Caller-supplied --harness, --min-power, --max-power constraints MUST NOT cause systematic preference for more-expensive in-bounds candidates over cheaper-but-equally-eligible ones.
- Once a provider has been observed unreachable in the current ddx-try attempt's per-request route exclusion list, the router MUST NOT re-attempt that provider until the exclusion expires or the worker is restarted.
- If literally every configured provider is unreachable AND every subscription provider has zero quota AND no cloud route is reachable, the router emits no_viable_provider and the caller can decide.
- The router MUST emit a clear routing-decision evidence event naming each candidate scored, why it was selected or rejected, and the final decision — so operators can audit 'why did it pick X over Y'.

SCOPE
- Fix the policy-scoring-under-bounds bug (fizeau-dc3cf359) — surgical change to internal/routing/score.go.
- Add an integration test: with bragi unreachable (mock dial-failing endpoint), a smart-policy request lands on claude (subscription) within one routing decision, no per-bead 30s timeout.
- Add an integration test: with --min-power 8, default-policy request picks the free-subscription candidate over the metered candidate at the same power.
- Add an integration test: with all configured providers dial-failing, the router returns no_viable_provider in under 2× per-attempt dial timeout (not 5× or more).
- Document the routing contract in docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003 §Routing (or a new section) so callers can rely on it.

NON-SCOPE
- Route-health cross-process persistence (fizeau-7cd66e6b is its own bead; depends on ADR-012 carve-out decision).
- Cooldown policy changes (ddx-side bead ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Connect-timeout tuning (fizeau-b5757624 already shipped).
- Provider config schema changes.

DEPS
- Decompose into child beads if scope exceeds 5 ACs / 500 lines / 5 unrelated files. Otherwise treat as one focused fix touching internal/routing/score.go + service_routing.go projection + tests.

CHILDREN
- fizeau-dc3cf359 (policy-scoring-under-bounds; surgical)
notes: Closed after child beads fizeau-dc3cf359, fizeau-126f03c0, fizeau-f79d434b, fizeau-f02ada30, fizeau-a77e62b6, and fizeau-ce4216aa landed. Local verification: go test ./... -count=1 passed; go test ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passed; targeted routing tests for dead-local fallback, all-dead fail-fast, min/max power scoring, and raw score components passed. CONTRACT-003, FEAT-004, SD-005, ADR-012, and CHANGELOG now describe cache-first route hot paths and score evidence.
title: routing: complete reliability — succeed against any available provider, fail only when no providers remain

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead fizeau-11e9c095 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: bench-pr-A: bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + --prese: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead fizeau-11e9c095 is too broad for one execution pass. This child creates the executable bash runner entrypoint and read-only planning/discovery surface needed by later PR A children.

ROOT CAUSE
The current runner is concentrated in cmd/bench/matrix.go:1 and is approximately 2232 LOC, making matrix behavior, discovery, validation, execution, resume, and concurrency hard to replace safely in one pass. It also injects the repo root into host PYTHONPATH at cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105, which is part of the host-Python dependency being removed by the parent scope.

PROPOSED FIX
Add scripts/benchmark/benchmark as a bash executable. Implement argument parsing and dispatch for profiles, bench-sets, task-executors, harness-adapters, validate, preflight, and run mode. Implement --plan-only planning for --profile, --bench-set, --reps, --jobs, --max-cost-usd, and --out without executing cells. Read profile, bench-set, and concurrency-group YAML through yq -o=json. Expand profile x task x rep in deterministic order. Resolve bench-set tasks_from paths relative to the bench-set file and inline tasks lists. For PR A smoke coverage, default task_executor to harbor when framework == terminal-bench. Add shell-focused tests around CLI parsing, YAML conversion, matrix planning, tasks_from resolution, and terminal-bench default executor.

NON-SCOPE
Do not execute cells, invoke harness adapters, invoke task executors, write result.json/report.json, implement resume/rerun behavior, implement flock concurrency, manage process groups, stop Docker containers, build the harbor-runner image, or port Python harness adapters in this child. Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it. Governing artifact reference must remain governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Parent/dependency ID fizeau-11e9c095 must remain traceable. Verification requirements cd cli && go test ./... and lefthook run pre-commit are required gates.

AC
1. TestBenchmarkDispatchSubcommands: scripts/benchmark/benchmark supports profiles, bench-sets, task-executors, harness-adapters, validate, preflight, and no-subcommand run-mode dispatch with documented usage failures for invalid arguments.
2. TestBenchmarkPlanMatrixExpansion: --plan expands profile x task x rep in deterministic order from yq -o=json YAML input and writes/prints a machine-readable plan without running cells.
3. TestBenchmarkTasksFromResolution: bench-set tasks_from references are resolved relative to the bench-set file and merge with inline tasks lists.
4. TestBenchmarkTerminalBenchDefaultsHarbor: matrix planning defaults task_executor to harbor when framework == terminal-bench and no explicit task_executor is present.
5. TestBenchmarkVerificationGatesA1: cd cli && go test ./... passes.
6. TestBenchmarkLefthookGateA1: lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: bench-pr-A1: bash runner discovery and planning shell

description:
Collect volatile per-provider runtime signals (status, quota
remaining, recent p50 latency) and write them to the runtime tier of
M1's cache. Consumed by M2's snapshot assembler at read time to set
KnownModel.Status, .QuotaRemaining, .RecentP50Latency.

## Public API sketch

```go
package runtimesignals

type Signal struct {
    Provider          string
    Status            modelregistry.ModelStatus
    QuotaRemaining    *int        // nil when unknown
    QuotaResetAt      *time.Time
    RecentP50Latency  time.Duration
    LastSuccessAt     *time.Time
    LastErrorAt       *time.Time
    LastErrorMsg      string
    RecordedAt        time.Time
}

func Collect(ctx context.Context, providerName string, cfg *config.ProviderConfig) (Signal, error)
```

## Sources per provider type

- **openrouter, openai-compat**: parse rate-limit headers from any
  recent response (already plumbed via commit 7776890e
  `quota_exhausted`); fetch `/usage` if available
- **claude-subscription**: existing `quota_cache.go` code in
  `internal/harnesses/claude/`; expose its data through this
  surface
- **codex-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go`
- **gemini-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_cache.go`
- **local providers (ds4, llama-server, omlx)**: status only (alive
  check via /v1/models or /props ping); no quota concept

## Aggregation

p50 latency comes from a sliding-window in-memory counter (recent N
requests per provider). On crash/restart, signal is reset (cache file
is empty until next collection). Acceptable — runtime signals are
short-TTL by design.

## Cache integration

Each provider gets its own `runtime/<provider>.json` file in M1's
cache. 5-minute TTL. Stale-while-revalidate: snapshot reads return
immediately, background refresh fires.

## In-scope files

- internal/runtimesignals/signal.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go — per-provider-type collectors
- internal/runtimesignals/headers.go — rate-limit header parsing
  (extends commit 7776890e)
- internal/runtimesignals/latency.go — sliding-window p50
- internal/runtimesignals/signal_test.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect_test.go

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M1 (cache infrastructure)
- Existing `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex,gemini}/quota_cache.go`
  (consumed, not modified — this bead exposes their data uniformly)

## Out of scope

- Snapshot assembly (M2).
- CLI (M4).
- Persistent historical metrics (out-of-scope per ADR-012; this is
  current-state only).
title: Runtime signal collector: status, quota, recent latency → runtime cache

description:
PROBLEM
The parent bead replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go (~2232 LOC Go) with a bash runner at scripts/benchmark/benchmark. This child delivers the runner's outer shell: arg parsing, subcommand dispatch, YAML loading, and matrix expansion — without per-cell execution, adapters, or Docker.

ROOT CAUSE
cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105 puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH so Harbor can import scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*, forcing host Python. The Go runner also owns arg parsing (cmd/bench/matrix.go arg-handling block) and matrix expansion (profile × task × rep). We need a bash entry point that can stand alone before adapters/Docker land.

PROPOSED FIX
Create scripts/benchmark/benchmark (bash, no extension, +x). Implement:
  - Subcommand dispatch: profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate | preflight | (default = run).
  - Run-mode flags exactly as parent spec: --profile, --bench-set, --plan, --force-rerun, --retry-invalid, --reps, --jobs, --max-cost-usd, --out.
  - YAML loaders that shell out to `yq -o=json` for profile, bench-set, concurrency-group files.
  - Matrix expansion: resolve `tasks_from:` (relative to bench-set file) and inline `tasks:`; default task_executor to `harbor` when framework == terminal-bench; emit ordered cell list to stdout as JSON when --plan is set.
  - listing subcommands (profiles, bench-sets, task-executors, harness-adapters) print names found on disk; stub validate/preflight that exits 0 with a 'not yet implemented' note (filled in by sibling A.4).

NON-SCOPE
- No per-cell execution loop, no adapter invocation, no Docker. Those land in siblings A.2/A.3/A.4.
- No removal of cmd/bench/matrix.go in this child.
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. scripts/benchmark/benchmark exists, is executable, and `./benchmark --help` prints subcommand+flag usage matching the parent spec.
2. `./benchmark --plan --profile P --bench-set B` emits a JSON cell list with correct profile×task×rep expansion including `tasks_from:` resolution; covered by TestBenchmarkRunnerPlanExpansion (Go test in cli/ that shells out to the script against fixture YAML).
3. Unknown subcommand or missing required flag exits non-zero with a usage hint; covered by TestBenchmarkRunnerArgParsing.

5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

description:
PROBLEM: The benchmark runner still lives as the large Go matrix implementation, so PR A cannot provide the new bash entry point, no-side-effect plan mode, YAML-driven matrix expansion, or per-cell report flow. Governing artifact: plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Dependency ID: fizeau-163efede. Sibling dependency: expects A1 adapter and task-executor contracts to exist, but this child must include any minimal test fixtures needed to run its own tests.
ROOT CAUSE: cmd/bench/matrix.go:1 is the monolithic fiz-bench matrix implementation, and cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105 couples benchmark execution to host Python instead of a shell runner invoking adapter contracts.
PROPOSED FIX: Add scripts/benchmark/benchmark as an executable bash script. Implement dispatch for profiles, bench-sets, task-executors, harness-adapters, validate, preflight, and run mode when no subcommand is provided. Implement run flags --profile, --bench-set, --plan, --force-rerun, --retry-invalid, --reps, --jobs, --max-cost-usd, and --out. Read profile, bench-set, and concurrency-group YAML through yq -o=json. Expand profile x task x rep into a deterministic ordered cell list. Resolve tasks_from relative to the bench-set file and inline tasks lists; for PR A smoke tests default task_executor to harbor when framework == terminal-bench while leaving final schema work to PR C. In --plan mode print the matrix, create no files, and build no Docker images. In preflight mode invoke the harbor-runner image rebuild check from A1 only when the cached image-content-sha label does not match. In run mode create cell-state.json, call harness-adapters/<name> command with profile JSON on stdin, compose command-spec and task-spec, call task-executors/<name> with task-spec JSON on stdin, compose report.json with embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/, result, and sweep metadata, then remove the sentinel. Implement resume defaults: skip existing terminal report.json cells, --force-rerun ignores the skip, and --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json. Implement bounded exponential backoff for transient connection refused, 5xx, EOF parse, and equivalent transient errors, writing attempt_of on the new cell and superseded_by into the prior closed cell as the only allowed mutation.
NON-SCOPE: cell-report.schema.json (PR B); final tasks_from / task_executor bench-set schema beyond PR A smoke behavior (PR C); Node collector (PR D); Node importers (PR E); deleting cmd/bench (PR F); docs beyond required local command help and existing redirect references (PR G). Do not implement budget, SIGTERM process cleanup, in-flight.json, or Go runner deactivation in this child.
ROLLBACK: If interrupted, leave cell-state.json sentinels as recovery anchors; revert only this child bead's script and test changes.

description:
PROBLEM
The release gate `go test ./... -count=1` failed in `agentcli/TestCLI_SessionLogs_UseWorkDirWhenRelative` during Go TempDir cleanup: `unlinkat /tmp/TestCLI_SessionLogs_UseWorkDirWhenRelative...: directory not empty`. The test shells out to the built `fiz` CLI repeatedly; if those subprocesses inherit live/default cache, state, quota, or temp roots, service startup can trigger quota refresh/projection activity outside the intended per-run sandbox and race Go TempDir cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE
`agentcli/catalog_cli_test.go:356-365` constructed a local `fiz` subprocess environment independently and only set `HOME`/`XDG_CONFIG_HOME`. The shared helper introduced by fizeau-3549f616 isolated cache/data and PATH, but still pointed some quota caches at missing per-home files and omitted Codex/Gemini quota cache, XDG state, CODEX_HOME, and TMPDIR isolation. With missing per-test quota fixtures, `fizeau.New` startup paths can treat harness quota as unavailable and start refresh/projection work instead of staying fully inside deterministic test state.

PROPOSED FIX
Use one shared agentcli subprocess environment helper for these tests. Under the temporary HOME, isolate config, cache, data, state, Fizeau cache, Codex home, and TMPDIR; seed fresh Claude, Codex, and Gemini quota fixture files; and keep PATH deterministic. Keep the test focused on session-log work-dir behavior; do not change runtime session-log semantics.
notes: Verified release blocker at final code head. Primary fix: ce2ca1344e2397df3a24f873769c7183d3b42bff. Follow-up hardening commit: dbf97d12dd59b512e76d4221d919536fbb694047 seeds fresh quota cache fixtures and isolates TMPDIR/CODEX_HOME so CLI subprocess tests cannot launch real agent tooling or race external telemetry. Verification: go test ./agentcli -run TestCLI_SessionLogs_UseWorkDirWhenRelative -count=50; go test ./agentcli -count=1; go test ./... -count=1; lefthook run pre-commit.
title: agentcli: isolate session-log CLI subprocess env

description:
PROBLEM
Once per-tier candidates are enumerated under a multi-tier subscription harness, the default scoring must select the cheapest tier that meets the power_hint_fit for the prompt. Downstream evidence: a 2207-token implementation prompt at role=implementer scored opus 100.5 (the lone claude candidate) under current behavior; the same prompt with sonnet-4.6 pinned scored 196.0. With both tiers enumerated by the sibling fix, default weights should produce sonnet>opus on impl-band prompts because cost_input_per_m (3 vs 15) and cost_output_per_m (15 vs 75) dominate.

ROOT CAUSE (pointers; confirm during fix)
- This child depends on per-tier enumeration landing (sibling child enumerate-per-tier-candidates). Once both claude/opus-4.7 and claude/sonnet-4.6 reach the scorer, score_components in the routing_decision event should show cost dominance for sonnet at impl power band. Verify scoring weights at internal/routing scorer (the same package that emits power_hint_fit / score_components events).
- Sonnet's power=8 is sufficient for role=implementer at ~2000-3000 tokens; opus's power=10 is not required to clear the hint. The fix here is to confirm the existing weights produce sonnet>opus given correct enumeration, and to add a regression test pinning that behavior; if weights need tuning to satisfy the AC, scope the tune narrowly.

PROPOSED FIX (direction)
Deterministic scoring test that constructs a routing.Inputs for a 2000-3000-token implementation prompt at role=implementer with both claude/opus-4.7 and claude/sonnet-4.6 in the candidate pool and asserts the chosen tier is sonnet-4.6. Only adjust scoring weights if the assertion fails with current weights — and only by the minimum needed.

NON-SCOPE
- Candidate enumeration mechanics (covered by sibling child enumerate-per-tier-candidates; this child assumes it lands first).
- --model pin routing destination (sibling child model-pin-subscription-route).
- filter_reason emission for excluded tiers (sibling child explicit-filter-reason).
- Re-tuning scoring across other roles/power bands beyond what is required to satisfy the AC.
- openrouter 401, DDx-side changes, catalog metadata.

CROSS-REF
https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6
title: auto-route: cost-aware tier scoring picks cheapest tier meeting power_hint_fit

2. jq -r 'select((.labels // []) | index("plan-2026-05-13-snapshot-routing") and .parent == "fizeau-4847c99e") | .id' .ddx/beads.jsonl shows exactly the eight child beads.

description:
Follow-on epic for the approved routing plan from 2026-05-13.

End state: every unpinned autorouting decision is a pure function of client inputs plus the current fiz models snapshot. The snapshot is the single source of routing facts: health, quota, model availability, context/tools/reasoning support, billing kind, effective cost, actual-cash-spend flag, locality, reliability, latency, utilization, and freshness. Route code must not maintain a second hidden provider-probe path.

Freshness is a fiz contract, not a required fiz daemon. Fiz provides synchronous refresh APIs/CLI behavior with cross-process locks, single-flight/coalescing, TTLs, cooldowns, and atomic snapshot writes. Autorouting can synchronously refresh stale routing-relevant fields before scoring. CLI commands must not create refresh storms. fiz models is quick and reads the snapshot; if stale fields exist it prints clear refresh guidance rather than launching uncoordinated background probes. Foreground --refresh and --refresh-all are available for serverless operation.

Fiz also exposes an optional async refresh/warmup mechanism that an external long-lived owner such as ddx server can call on a heartbeat to maintain freshness. That async maintainer is an optimization only; route correctness must not depend on ddx server integration.

Routing gates only on true constraints or dispatchability: pins, no_remote, metered actual-spend refusal, required tools/context/reasoning, known down endpoints, quota exhaustion, and pin conflicts. Everything else is scoring. Subscription-backed models use PAYG-equivalent shadow/effective cost for scoring, with actual_cash_spend=false, so cheap naturally prefers nano/mini/local over GPT-5.5 without policy-specific exclusion gates.

Closes when child beads specify, implement, and verify: specs, snapshot schema, coordinated refresh, CLI refresh behavior, routing purity, effective-cost scoring, and route traceability.
notes: Verified 2026-05-13: all eight child beads under plan-2026-05-13-snapshot-routing are closed; go test ./... -count=1 passed locally. Acceptance child count updated from seven to eight to match the filed implementation split.
title: EPIC: Make autorouting snapshot-first with coordinated freshness

description:
Add `fiz models` as a top-level CLI subcommand. Default view shows
the assembled snapshot from M2; flags filter and refresh. Detail view
via `fiz models <ref>` with shortform fuzzy resolution per ADR-012's
naming convention.

Also updates FEAT-006 (standalone CLI) doc to register the new
subcommand and document the interface.

## CLI surface

```
fiz models [flags]                     # list view
fiz models <ref> [flags]               # detail view
fiz cache prune                        # related cache GC
```

## Flags (list view)

- `--refresh`         Force-refresh all sources synchronously before showing
- `--no-refresh`      Don't trigger background refresh on stale data
- `--json`            Machine-readable output
- `--provider <name>` Filter to one provider
- `--power-min <n>`   Filter Power >= n
- `--power-max <n>`   Filter Power <= n
- `--include-noise`   Show low-power, unranked, deprecated entries
  (default suppresses to keep output clean)

## Default filter rules (suppress noise)

- Within (family, tier): keep top 2 versions (current + previous)
- Drop entries with Power < 3 (noise)
- Drop entries with Tier == TierUnknown AND Power == 0
- Show AutoRoutable=false but mark them clearly

`--include-noise` shows everything (useful for debugging the
snapshot, OR's long tail, etc.)

## Detail view (`fiz models <ref>`)

Resolution per ADR-012:
- Full canonical (`<provider>/<id>`) → exact lookup
- Shortform (no `/`) → search snapshot:
  - Exactly one match: use it
  - Multiple matches: list candidates with full canonical IDs, exit 1
  - No matches: show fuzzy-match suggestions, exit 1

Detail output: full KnownModel struct + raw discovery data + full
catalog entry + recent runtime signals + AutoRoutable composition
breakdown (which factor blocked, if any).

## Output format (default)

Tabular, columns: PROVIDER MODEL FAMILY VERSION TIER POWER COST/M
STATUS QUOTA AUTO

Color: AutoRoutable=true green, =false dim. Pre-release in italics.
Status colored (Available green, RateLimited yellow, Unreachable red).

`--json` emits the snapshot serialized as JSON.

## In-scope files

- cmd/fiz/models.go — subcommand registration
- cmd/fiz/models_list.go — list view + filters
- cmd/fiz/models_detail.go — detail view + resolution
- cmd/fiz/models_test.go
- cmd/fiz/cache_prune.go — `fiz cache prune` subcommand
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-standalone-cli.md — register
  `fiz models` and `fiz cache prune` subcommands; document flags

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M2 (snapshot assembler)
- M3 (runtime signals)

## Out of scope

- Routing's consumption of the snapshot (handled in routing-layer
  beads downstream; this bead is the operator UI)
- OR sub-provider syntax in resolution (M5 deferred)
notes: Operator refined ACs into named test/build/doc checks and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.
title: fiz models top-level CLI subcommand with shortform fuzzy resolution

- Provider-specific MTP semantics beyond carrying operator-specified env/sampling/profile metadata.

cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105 puts the repo root on host PYTHONPATH so Harbor can import scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*, coupling benchmark execution to host Python. The parent scope requires porting matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/ and having scripts/benchmark/benchmark invoke harness-adapters/<name> with profile.json on stdin to produce command-spec.json.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead fizeau-11e9c095 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: bench-pr-A: bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + --prese: part 2

acceptance:
1. `TestPreflightLaneMisconfiguredReasoning` covers sindri-llamacpp configured with `reasoning: low`: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero in under 60s with a `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED` message naming the unsupported reasoning parameter and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. `TestPreflightLaneOK` covers correctly configured sindri-llamacpp: the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. `TestPreflightLaneConnectionRefused` covers stopped llama-server: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` or curl error 7, and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. `TestPreflightManagedCloudSkipped` covers cloud lanes: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call OpenRouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.
5. `scripts/benchmark/test_preflight_lane.bash` or an equivalent shellspec file defines the four test cases above against a mock HTTP server.
description:
Add a per-lane preflight check to scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh that fires a single 1-token chat completion against each requested lane's endpoint before any matrix runner spawns. If the endpoint cannot produce a token within 30s using the lane's actual sampling params, fail the sweep with a non-zero exit and a clear message naming the lane, the endpoint, the model id, and the underlying error.

Why this matters: see parent epic. The 2026-05-10 sindri-llamacpp incident wasted 119 cells (~93 min) on a `reasoning: low` parameter the llama-server provider rejects. The same hour, sindri-vllm wasted multiple cells on a hung model engine where /v1/models responded but /v1/chat/completions never streamed tokens. A single curl-to-completion check per lane catches all these classes (plus auth failures, missing-model 404s, connection-refused, wrong content-type) at sweep startup, before any matrix runner spawns.

Behavior:

- Fire after `Resolved tasks:` is printed, before the per-lane "starting" markers.
- For each lane in the resolved plan: build a JSON body using the lane's `fizeau_env.FIZEAU_MODEL` and the lane's sampling block (temperature, top_p, top_k, reasoning when present). POST to `<base_url>/chat/completions` with `max_tokens: 1` and a 30s timeout.
- Pass condition: the response is JSON-parseable and `.choices[0].message.content` is a non-null string (even an empty string is OK — the model started decoding).
- Fail condition: any non-2xx, timeout, malformed JSON, or missing message content. Print the lane id, base_url, model id, sampling params attempted, and the response body (truncated to 1KB).
- Skip preflight for managed-cloud lanes that have always-on SLAs (openrouter, openai, anthropic) — these are well-known and a preflight just burns quota. Identify by checking the resource group's `provider_type` against an allow-list (vllm, llama-server, omlx, lmstudio, rapid-mlx, ollama, lucebox).

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh — add preflight_lane() function + per-lane invocation loop right before the existing per-lane plan output.
- scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml — no schema change needed; the existing `fizeau_env` and `sampling` blocks are sufficient.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Pre-resume validation that the model server is the same version as last time (different problem; would need version stamps on cells).
- Per-cell health probe during the sweep (defense-in-depth piece is fizeau-XXXX, the consecutive-failure halt bead).
- Adding the smoke check to non-TerminalBench benchmark scripts.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With sindri-llamacpp configured to use `reasoning: low` (the broken state we just fixed), `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: reasoning="low" is not supported by provider type "llama-server"` (or equivalent error text) and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. With sindri-llamacpp correctly configured (no `reasoning` param), the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. Test by killing the llama-server process: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` (or curl error 7) and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. The cloud lane skip works: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call openrouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.

Cost budget: at most ~30s × N_lanes added to sweep startup wall time. For an 11-lane full sweep that's ~5 min, much cheaper than waiting for the first cell of each broken lane to fail.
notes: Operator made ACs mechanically verifiable with explicit test names and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.

description:
Refactor the TB-2.1 sweep plan schema so lanes and task-subsets are orthogonal. Today scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml couples them: each phase carries a task subset AND a curated lane bundle, and each lane lists which phases it's enrolled in. The runner errors when --lanes intersected with selected phase(s) yields empty (cmd/bench/sweep.go:773 "none of --lanes ... are present in selected phase(s)"). Today's incident: `--lanes fiz-sindri-lucebox-qwen3-6-27b --phase all` aborted because `all` iterates phases lucebox isn't enrolled in.

Target: introduce subsets[] (pure task lists) and recipes[] (curated lane+subset bundles, pure CLI sugar). Runner's matrix becomes subset × lanes; no YAML enrollment constraint. CLI gains --subset and --recipe; --phase becomes a deprecated alias. lanes[].phases is removed (always dead data per sweep.go:71-84). resource_groups[].runner_jobs_default added to replace the wrapper's per-phase MATRIX_JOBS_MANAGED mapping.

Plan reviewed across two independent fresh-eyes passes (internal Plan agent + Codex CLI); v3 spec in commit-side branch notes. Cells/ tree, comparison_groups, SD-010 D5, and SD-012 are explicitly NOT modified (codex feedback corrected v2 plan's overreach there).
title: sweep schema v2: orthogonal subsets and lanes

description:
PROBLEM
When a caller pins --model sonnet-4.6 (no --harness pin) and a claude subscription harness is available, dispatch currently routes to fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 instead of the same-auth claude subscription harness. This means the operator-friendly workaround "pin sonnet by name" does not deliver sonnet on the subscription path; it falls through to a separate per-token provider (which then 401s if openrouter is not authed).

ROOT CAUSE (tentative)
When --model is pinned, the routing engine resolves candidates per (harness, provider, resolved-model). The claude harness exposes sonnet via its SupportedModels list, but harness selection for a pinned model appears to prefer the harness whose surface_id matches the catalog's canonical-by-surface lookup. Need to verify whether the engine prefers subscription harnesses for pinned models that match their SupportedModels.

SCOPE
- Identify where --model X is resolved to (harness, provider) pair for non-pinned-harness requests.
- For subscription harnesses with the pinned model in their SupportedModels/AutoRoutingModels, ensure they appear as a viable candidate (and preferably beat openrouter on score for cost-aware scoring with a healthy subscription quota).
- Unit test with a claude harness mock and openrouter HarnessEntry: pin --model sonnet-4.6, assert decision.Harness == "claude".

NON-SCOPE
- The openrouter 401 itself (separate config issue).
- Multi-tier enumeration under policy=default (sibling bead fizeau-f7fa1d87).

CROSS-REF

description:
Design ADR (no implementation in this bead) for cost-based routing
where quota-remaining is a factor in the cost score. Implementation
follows in a separate EPIC after the ADR is accepted.

Per operator direction 2026-05-11:

> For a given model power, we need to intelligently choose based on
> "cost", where remaining usage quota impacts the cost.

Cost model (proposed for ADR-011):

- **Subscription with quota remaining**: effective cost ≈ 0 (or
  quota-fraction-aware: lower-cost the more quota remains).
- **Subscription with quota exhausted**: drop from candidates.
- **Per-token API**: effective cost = catalog `cost_input_per_m` ×
  estimated input tokens + `cost_output_per_m` × estimated output
  tokens.
- **Local provider** (free): effective cost = 0 if eligible (per bead
  D's IncludeByDefault filter), else dropped.

Quota signal sources:

- HTTP rate-limit response headers (per recent commit 7776890e —
  `quota_exhausted` already plumbed).
- Subscription `/usage` endpoints when available.
- In-memory recent-rejection rate as fallback.

Quota-pool semantic (from bead E1):

- Models declare a `quota_pool` (defaulting to provider system).
- Pool exhaustion drops all models in that pool from the candidate set.
- Models in a different pool (e.g., `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` in
  `openai-codex-spark`) become eligible as fallbacks even if not
  version-newest, satisfying the "use spark when main pool is empty"
  operator request.

Power-floor semantic:

- Caller / DDx specifies required `MinPower` (and optional `MaxPower`).
- Routing filters candidates to `MinPower ≤ candidate.power ≤ MaxPower`.
- Among survivors, pick min effective cost.
- Tie-break: prefer subscription over PAYG; prefer lower-power if it
  exceeds requirement (cheapest qualified candidate, not most powerful).

## ADR scope

The ADR document itself, written to
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md`.
Standard ADR structure: Context, Decision, Consequences, Out of scope,
References.

## Decision points the ADR must resolve

1. Quota fraction → cost mapping (linear? step function? thresholds?).
2. Quota window time handling (do we reserve quota for later? probably
   not in v1 — use available now).
3. Token estimate source (the request itself? a heuristic? a learned
   model?). v1 likely uses a coarse system-prompt-length-based
   estimate.
4. Fallback chain ordering when quota exhausts mid-request (single try?
   retry on alternate?).
5. How `IncludeByDefault: false` (bead D) composes with cost ranking
   (drops from default candidate set entirely; not just deprioritized).

## In-scope files

- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md
  (new)

## Out of scope

- Implementation (separate EPIC after ADR acceptance).
- Quota signal infrastructure beyond what 7776890e already provides
  (any new signals needed get filed as their own beads under the EPIC).
- Per-tenant or per-key quota tracking (flag as future scope in the
  ADR).
notes: Operator refined ACs into mechanical file/rg/git checks and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.
title: ADR-011: Cost-based routing with quota-pool-aware fallback

description:
Problem
Adding a TerminalBench lane currently requires hand-editing several independent surfaces: scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml lane entries, recipe lane lists, scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml, and shell aliases in scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh. The fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp incident shows this is too easy to leave half-wired: the lane existed, but its profile was missing, recipes did not include it, and its short alias did not exist.

Goal
Make lane addition a single, validated workflow. An operator should be able to add or clone a lane, validate it before running a sweep, and get precise diagnostics when the profile, recipe enrollment, resource group, alias, or runtime switch is missing.

Out of scope
Do not run paid/full TerminalBench sweeps. Do not redesign benchmark result storage or evidence import.
title: EPIC: simplify benchmark lane authoring

description:
Add end-to-end fixture coverage that proves the combined snapshot-first autorouting contract across CLI/service/routing surfaces.

Scenarios to cover:
- cheap chooses nano/mini/local over gpt-5.5 when sufficient.
- default chooses the cheapest routine-sufficient candidate, not frontier, when lower-cost candidates are healthy.
- smart can select gpt-5.5 when quality dominates.
- no_remote forces local selection or a clear constraint failure when all locals are down.
- metered opt-out rejects actual-cash-spend pay-per-token providers; opt-in lets them win if utility is best.
- exact model pin selects the pinned model or fails clearly when undispatchable.
- stale unknown health is refreshed or penalized according to snapshot freshness state; known-down is excluded.
- repeated fiz models does not create refresh storms.

In scope: fixture snapshots, integration-style tests, CLI golden JSON where useful.

Out of scope: large live-provider tests; this bead should be deterministic/offline.
title: Add end-to-end snapshot autorouting fixtures

description:
Amend the routing and snapshot governing docs so implementation is not relying on chat context.

Required design contract:
- Autorouting decision = route(client_inputs, fiz_models_snapshot).
- Client inputs include policy/profile, pins, no_remote, metered opt-in, tools/context/reasoning needs, and other explicit constraints.
- The fiz models snapshot is the only source of routing facts: health, quota, model availability, effective cost, actual_cash_spend, billing kind, context/tools/reasoning support, locality, reliability, latency, utilization, and per-field freshness.
- Fiz owns synchronous freshness: refresh operations use cross-process locks, single-flight/coalescing, TTLs, cooldowns, bounded concurrency, and atomic snapshot writes. Autorouting may synchronously refresh stale routing-relevant fields before scoring.
- Fiz exposes an optional async refresh/warmup entrypoint that a long-lived ddx server can call on a heartbeat. This is a cache-warming optimization; route correctness must not require ddx server.
- fiz models is quick; it reads the current snapshot and, when fields are stale, prints refresh guidance instead of starting an uncoordinated background probe. fiz models --refresh blocks on routing-relevant stale fields; fiz models --refresh-all blocks on all refreshable fields.
- Hard gates are limited to explicit user constraints and dispatchability. Cost, quality, health risk, latency, utilization, and power fit are scoring inputs.
- Subscription models use PAYG-equivalent effective/shadow cost for scoring while retaining actual_cash_spend=false. Pay-per-token providers are gated only when dispatch creates actual metered spend and the user has not opted in.

In scope: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md, docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md, docs/routing/*.md, ADR-012 snapshot/cache docs if present.

Out of scope: implementation code; create follow-up beads instead of partially implementing.
title: Specify snapshot-first autorouting and freshness ownership

description:
Design ADR for the per-source on-disk cache that backs the available-
models snapshot. Defines file layout, locking semantics, single-flight
refresh algorithm, atomic-write contract, force-refresh behavior, TTLs,
crash recovery, and the mandatory test matrix for the implementation.

This is design-only. Implementation is bead M1.

## ADR scope (sections to write)

### Context
- The `fiz models` snapshot needs live data from multiple sources
  (provider /v1/models, harness PTY, /props introspection, /usage
  endpoints, rate-limit response headers).
- Discovery sources are slow (PTY ~30s, OR /api/v1/models ~316ms).
- Runtime signals (quota, status, latency) change frequently.
- UI must never hang (100ms budget).
- Multi-process: `ddx work`, `ddx try`, `fiz models`, routing all run
  concurrently and must share cache state.
- fizeau already has a battle-tested file-locking idiom in
  `cmd/bench/matrix.go:acquireMatrixLock` (PID + atomic-create-or-fail
  with crash recovery via PID-alive check). Build on it.

### Decision

#### File layout
```
~/.cache/fizeau/   (os.UserCacheDir())
├── discovery/                          # slow, stable
│   ├── openrouter.json                 # 24h TTL
│   ├── claude-subscription.json        # 24h TTL
│   ├── codex-subscription.json         # 24h TTL
│   ├── vidar-ds4.json                  # 1h TTL (local)
│   └── sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.json  # 1h TTL (local)
└── runtime/                            # hot, volatile
    ├── openrouter.json                 # 5min TTL
    ├── claude-subscription.json        # 5min TTL
    └── ...
```
Each data file is JSON. Keys map to fizeau's canonical model identity:
`<provider>/<model_id>`.

#### Lock + marker pattern (two-tier)
- `<source>.lock`: brief mutation lock. Same idiom as
  `acquireMatrixLock` — `O_CREATE|O_EXCL`, JSON `{PID, StartedAt}`,
  crash recovery via `processAlive(PID)`. Held during state
  transitions only (claim refresh, finish refresh).
- `<source>.refreshing`: long-lived refresh marker. JSON
  `{PID, StartedAt, Deadline}`. Held during refresh IO. Other
  processes see this and wait OR return stale.

#### Algorithms (full pseudocode in ADR)
1. `claim_refresh(source) -> ClaimedByMe | AlreadyInFlight(marker)`
2. `refresh_and_commit(source)` — IO, atomic-rename, marker cleanup
3. `wait_for_refresh(source, marker, max_wait)` — poll marker
4. `read(source) -> (data, fresh_bool)` — UI never blocks
5. `force_refresh(source)` — sync; wait or claim
6. In-process single-flight via `golang.org/x/sync/singleflight`
   (composes with file-based marker for cross-process)

#### TTL defaults (operator-configurable via env or config)
- Discovery PTY: 24h (PTY is slow; refresh rare)
- Discovery HTTP /v1/models: 24h
- Discovery HTTP /v1/models (local): 1h
- Runtime signals: 5 min
- Lock acquisition timeout: 100ms
- Refresh deadline: 60s for PTY, 10s for HTTP discovery, 5s for runtime
- Marker staleness threshold: 2× refresh deadline

#### Crash recovery
- Stale `.refreshing` markers (past deadline OR dead PID) are
  presumed orphan; next caller overrides. No manual cleanup needed.
- Stale `.lock` files (dead PID) are removed on next attempt
  (existing `acquireMatrixLock` semantics).

#### Force-refresh semantics
- Synchronous. Waits for in-flight refresh to complete (up to
  refresh deadline). Then reads fresh data.
- Bypasses TTL check.

#### Cache prune
- `fiz cache prune` removes data files for sources not in current
  config. Called explicitly OR auto-pruned at startup (operator
  decision; ADR proposes manual to avoid surprises).

### Mandatory test matrix (10 tests)

The implementation bead (M1) MUST ship all 10 tests passing. Skipping
any = the cache is dangerous. Spawn child Go processes for multi-
process scenarios (standard `TestMain` + `os.Args[0]` -test.run
helper-process pattern).

1. **Concurrent claim from 2 procs**: exactly one ClaimedByMe.
2. **Reader during refresh**: atomic rename → no torn read; reader
   sees old until rename, new after.
3. **Crash during refresh** (kill -9): next process detects dead PID
   in marker, claims.
4. **Refresh timeout**: marker deadline expires, next process
   claims; orig process's late commit rejected (lock check).
5. **Force-refresh waits + reads fresh**.
6. **Concurrent normal+force**: normal returns stale immediately,
   background refresh single-flighted with force.
7. **Atomic rename verified**: 100 concurrent readers + 1 writer
   producing 100 versions; every read is a complete consistent
   version (checksum).
8. **`fiz cache prune` doesn't race active sources**.
9. **Stale-while-revalidate**: stale read returns immediately,
   background refresh fires.
10. **PID reuse safety**: marker PID gets reused by unrelated
    process; deadline check is the safety net.

### Consequences

**Positive:**
- UI never hangs (read path is always cache-bounded < 100ms).
- Multi-process safe across `ddx work` and `fiz` invocations.
- Crash-safe via PID-alive + deadline checks.
- Reuses fizeau's proven `acquireMatrixLock` idiom; no new
  cross-platform lock library required.
- Each source has independent lifecycle; OR re-discovery doesn't
  block PTY discovery.

**Negative:**
- Two file roles per source (lock + marker) is more complexity
  than a single mutex.
- File-based coordination has inherent edge cases (PID reuse,
  filesystem semantics differences); test matrix is non-trivial.
- Stale data may be served briefly during refresh (acceptable
  trade for non-blocking UI).

### Out of scope
- Persistent storage of historical signals (cache is per-source,
  current-state only).
- Multi-machine cache sharing (per-host only).
- Compression (JSON, plain).
- Encryption at rest.
- OR sub-provider cache (deferred M5).

### References
- Bead A (`fizeau-b2c5c826`) — version-aware ranker, parses model IDs
- Bead D (`fizeau-d18e11f5`) — IncludeByDefault filter (composes with
  AutoRoutable in snapshot)
- Bead E1 (`fizeau-c04be6b0`) — quota_pool catalog field (consumed in
  M2 enrichment)
- Bead E2 (`fizeau-5b6512ef`) — ADR-011 cost-based routing (downstream
  consumer of snapshot)
- `cmd/bench/matrix.go:acquireMatrixLock` — prior-art lock idiom
- `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_cache.go` — atomic-rename
  prior art

## In-scope files
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-012-per-source-on-disk-cache.md (new)

## Out of scope
- Implementation (bead M1).
title: ADR-012: Per-source on-disk cache for discovery + runtime signals

description:
PROBLEM
Unpinned `fiz run` requests can preselect the configured/default local provider before handing the request to the service. When the local provider is unavailable, that caller-side preselection prevents the service route resolver from choosing a healthy subscription or alternate provider even though no provider was explicitly requested.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `agentcli/run.go:922-925` resolves an empty provider name to `cfg.DefaultName()` inside `resolveProviderForRun`, so ordinary unpinned runs inherit the configured provider as if it were a caller pin.
- `agentcli/run.go:426-431` builds the service request from the caller-side selection, so the service receives provider/model fields even when only ambient Fizeau config supplied them.
- `agentcli/run_options_test.go` lacked coverage proving unpinned runs keep `ServiceExecuteRequest.Provider` and `ServiceExecuteRequest.Model` empty while preserving explicit `--model` intent.

PROPOSED FIX
For `fiz run` with no explicit `--provider`, skip direct provider construction and pass only model intent/default route metadata into the service selection. Keep explicit provider pins on the existing direct-provider path. Add regression tests for an unpinned request and an unpinned request with explicit `--model`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- agentcli/run.go
- agentcli/run_options_test.go

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change service route scoring weights or provider-health policy.
- Do not change `--provider` semantics.
- Do not modify DDx tracker files by hand.
title: agentcli: delegate unpinned run routing to service

3. In a test with stale fields and no long-running maintainer, fiz models exits 0, does not invoke uncoordinated provider probes, and prints a hint mentioning --refresh and DDx server freshness.
4. In a test with stale fields and a fake coordinator, fiz models exits 0 and records at most one coordinated refresh request.

description:
Update fiz models CLI behavior to match the approved freshness contract.

Required CLI behavior:
- fiz models returns the current cached snapshot quickly.
- If stale routing-relevant fields are present, fiz models may request a best-effort coordinated refresh through Fizeau's lock/single-flight path, but it must not start duplicate uncoordinated probe work.
- If stale routing-relevant fields remain stale, fiz models prints a concise stale-data hint: run fiz models --refresh or start/configure a DDx server freshness heartbeat.
- fiz models --refresh performs a bounded foreground refresh of routing-relevant stale fields before printing.
- fiz models --refresh-all performs a bounded foreground refresh of all refreshable fields before printing.
- Output marks stale/fresh/refresh_failed state clearly in JSON and human views.

In scope: agentcli/cmd fiz models command flags, user-facing text, JSON fields, tests with fake coordinator.

Out of scope: implementing route scoring; implementing the low-level coordinator if the prior bead has not landed; implementing DDx server itself.
title: Make fiz models refresh UX coordinator-aware

acceptance: 1. go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestConsecutiveFailureHalt covers the four behaviors above and passes. 2. Misconfigured fixture sweep exits code 75 after exactly 5 cells with aborted.json written. 3. --no-consecutive-failure-halt runs all cells without aborting. 4. lane_aborted appears as a known invalid class in matrix.json's invalid_by_class breakdown.
description:
Add a consecutive-failure halt to the matrix runner (cmd/bench/matrix.go) that aborts the entire lane after N=5 cells fail with byte-identical error fingerprints in a row. Writes a single `lane_aborted` summary cell explaining why; subsequent cells in the lane are skipped (not run, not written).

Why this matters: see parent epic. The preflight smoke test (sibling bead fizeau-586e476a) catches misconfigured lanes at sweep startup. This bead is the defense-in-depth piece for cases where preflight passed but the endpoint dies mid-sweep — e.g. vLLM model engine enters a degraded state after running fine for a while, or a model server gets OOM-killed and the wrapper restarts it broken. Today such regressions cause every remaining cell in the lane to fail identically until human intervention.

Behavior:

- After each cell in a lane completes, compute a fingerprint = SHA256 of the report's `error` field (truncated to first 256 bytes) + `final_status`. Store the last N=5 fingerprints in lane-scoped state inside the existing per-lane matrix runner process (no on-disk state).
- When 5 consecutive cells share the same fingerprint AND are all `graded_fail` or `harness_crash` (not `graded_pass`), halt the lane:
  * Write one synthetic `report.json` to a path like `<cells_root>/.lane_aborted/<lane_id>/aborted.json` with `final_status: "lane_aborted"`, `error: "halted after 5 consecutive identical failures: <fingerprint preview>"`, plus the fingerprint and the list of the 5 task ids that triggered.
  * Print `[matrix] lane=<lane_id> ABORTED after 5 consecutive identical failures (last task: <task>): <error preview>` to stderr.
  * Exit the matrix runner with a distinct exit code (e.g. 75 / EX_TEMPFAIL) so the sweep script can attribute the abort to this cause vs a normal lane completion.
- Add `final_status: "lane_aborted"` to the matrix invalid-class machinery so the website / aggregates display these explicitly (not as silent missing cells).
- Add a `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` flag to disable the safety net for operators debugging a known-broken lane.

Failure-mode test cases (must all be covered):

- 5 cells with identical `agent: provider error: ...` errors → halt fires.
- 5 cells with identical `harness_crash: context canceled` (the docker subnet exhaustion pattern from earlier in this codebase) → halt fires.
- 4 identical errors then 1 graded_pass → halt does NOT fire; counter resets.
- 4 identical errors then a 5th error with even one byte different → halt does NOT fire.
- `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` flag → halt never fires regardless.

In-scope files:
- cmd/bench/matrix.go — add ConsecutiveFailureTracker (or inline state) inside the matrix-runner main loop; integrate into the per-cell write path; add the flag.
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — register `lane_aborted` as a known invalid class (so summarizeMatrixCells counts it correctly, and the regen pipeline treats it as systemic, not model failure).
- cmd/bench/matrix_test.go — add the four behavior tests above using the existing test fixture pattern.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Auto-restart of the lane after the user fixes the issue. (For now, operator manually re-runs.)
- Cross-lane abort cascade (one lane's repeated failures shouldn't halt other lanes).
- Surfacing the halt count in the website data — that's a follow-up once we see how often this fires in practice.

Acceptance is a command:

1. `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestConsecutiveFailureHalt` passes (covers the four behaviors above).
2. With a deliberately-misconfigured fixture profile that produces identical errors, `fiz-bench matrix --profiles broken-fixture --reps 10 --out /tmp/abort-test` exits with code 75 after exactly 5 cells, writes `aborted.json` under `<out>/.lane_aborted/`, and the remaining 5 cells are not present in the output dir.
3. The same fixture with `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` runs all 10 cells to completion (each failing) without aborting.
title: bench: matrix runner halts lane after 5 consecutive identical failures

2. Add `TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest` proving `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` reaches the service execution stub with `Harness="codex"`, `Policy="default"`, `Model=""`, `Provider=""`, `EstimatedPromptTokens > 0`, and `RequiresTools == false`.
3. Add `TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest` proving a run with an enabled tool preset or explicit tool support sets `RequiresTools == true` in the service request.
4. If `Execute` returns a route-resolution error before dispatch, JSON output must not invent an unrelated `selected_provider`, `selected_route`, or `resolved_model` from a stale pre-service selection; add `TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection` or an equivalent focused regression.
5. `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz -run 'TestBuildServiceExecuteRequest_ForwardsPromptFeatures|TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection' -count=1` passes.
6. `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` either succeeds or fails before dispatch, but its error/output must not contain `prompt of 0 tokens` and must not invoke or report `qwen3.5-27b`.
7. `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz . -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After installing Fizeau v0.14.3, the live smoke `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` no longer invokes Codex with `qwen3.5-27b`, but it still fails before dispatch with `ResolveRoute: no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at policy default`. The prompt text is nonempty, so Fizeau's own CLI run path is still sending an unset prompt feature envelope into route selection.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`agentcli/run.go:408-441` constructs `serviceExecuteRequestParams` from the parsed prompt, policy, and harness, but it does not compute or pass prompt feature fields. `agentcli/run.go:590-620` defines `serviceExecuteRequestParams` without `EstimatedPromptTokens` or `RequiresTools`, and `agentcli/run.go:675-690` builds `fizeau.ServiceExecuteRequest` without setting those fields. `service.go:817-823` documents that `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` drive auto-selection gates, and `service_routing.go:1761-1773` forwards those fields directly into `RouteRequest`; the current CLI path therefore forwards zero/false regardless of a nonempty prompt or tool preset.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the CLI service-run envelope populate prompt feature fields before calling `Execute`: compute a conservative nonzero `EstimatedPromptTokens` from `Prompt` + `SystemPrompt` when no stronger estimate is available, and set `RequiresTools` when explicit tools or a tool preset will be available to the run. Thread both fields through `serviceExecuteRequestParams` and `buildServiceExecuteRequest`. Keep route selection in Fizeau service/routing; this bead only fixes the CLI request envelope and failure metadata.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx request forwarding; DDx's analogous bug is already tracked separately. Do not pin provider/model/harness to bypass routing. Do not change policy scoring or provider health refresh behavior.
title: run: forward CLI prompt features into service routing

No network — purely a string/env check. The bug surfaces when api_key in fizeau config is the literal string '${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}' because the env var was not set during config load (see internal/config/config_test.go:258-266 which shows the literal string is preserved verbatim). Currently this bypasses ConfigError, the provider survives routing-input construction, openrouter is picked, and the dispatch fails with HTTP 401. This bead makes that case appear in routing_decision as eligible=false + filter_reason=credential_missing without burning a dispatch.

Depends on fizeau-458f10b0.
