description:
Extend the fiz models snapshot data model so every fact needed by routing is visible to operators and fixture tests.

The snapshot must represent, per candidate/model row where applicable:
- billing kind: subscription, metered/per-token, fixed/local, test, or unknown
- actual_cash_spend: whether dispatching this candidate creates incremental cash spend
- effective_cost: comparable routing cost used for scoring, including PAYG-equivalent shadow cost for subscription models
- effective_cost_source/provenance
- health state and freshness timestamp/source
- quota state and freshness timestamp/source
- model availability/discovery freshness
- context/tools/reasoning support
- locality, reliability, latency, utilization signals when known

Root cause: subscription-backed models currently look equally included for scoring, so cheap can pick maximum-quality frontier subscription models. The model snapshot must carry comparable effective cost separately from actual cash spend so routing can prefer the cheapest sufficiently capable route without hidden probes or if/else frontier bans.

In scope: model snapshot structs, assembler/enrichment code, JSON output schema, tests and fixtures.

Out of scope: changing route scoring; changing CLI refresh behavior; server refresh orchestration.
title: Expose routing cost and freshness facts in the models snapshot

Verified by `TestOpenrouterCredentialMissingNoNetwork` in `service_routing_test.go` which scrubs the env var, installs a panicking HTTP roundtripper for the openrouter host, runs an Execute with no model pin, and asserts the candidates payload + zero roundtripper invocations.

3. With a syntactically valid key present, the credential hook does not exclude the candidate (allowing downstream credit/health checks to decide). Verified by `TestOpenrouterValidKeyPassesCredentialGate`.

description:
PROBLEM
When the openrouter API key env var / config is empty, unset, or malformed (wrong prefix / wrong length), Fizeau still surfaces openrouter candidates as `eligible: true` in routing_decision. Auto-route then selects openrouter and the dispatch fails at HTTP-call time with `401 Missing Authentication header`. Operators see a transport error instead of the actual root cause (no key).

ROOT CAUSE
`service_routing.go` candidate-enumeration path (the same module that already plumbs `health_freshness_at` / `quota_freshness_at` for LMStudio aliveness) has no provider-health hook for openrouter. The candidate row is constructed unconditionally from the configured model list with no inspection of the credential the provider will need to authenticate.
Exact line range to be confirmed during implementation by grepping for the LMStudio `endpoint_unreachable` filter_reason emission — the credential hook plugs in alongside it.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a synchronous, network-free credential-presence check that runs on every routing pass for openrouter candidates:
- Look up the openrouter API key from env var and config (mirroring however the openai-compatible provider currently loads it).
- If empty/unset → mark candidate `eligible: false`, `filter_reason: "credential_missing"`.
- If present but obviously malformed (wrong prefix `sk-or-...`, length outside expected range) → same `credential_missing` (distinct from `credential_invalid`, which is reserved for server-side rejection in [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]]).
- Populate the candidate evidence body so operators can see WHICH key location was checked.
- Do NOT issue any HTTP call in this path; this hook must be O(1) and side-effect free.

NON-SCOPE
- Credit balance probing (covered by [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]]).
- 401 / transport-error distinction (covered by [[openrouter-probe-failure-modes-and-surfacing]]).
- Multi-account credential rotation / failover within openrouter (parent non-scope, preserved).
- Per-model credit-cost forecasting (parent non-scope, preserved).
- DDx-side changes — DDx passes `policy=default` through unchanged per CONTRACT-003 (parent non-scope, preserved).

CROSS-REF
Filed in tandem with fizeau-80d4bf41 (auto-route candidate enumeration). Sibling beads: [[openrouter-credit-balance-probe-and-cache]], [[openrouter-probe-failure-modes-and-surfacing]].
title: openrouter: gate candidate on credential presence before network I/O

description:
Operator integration bead — runs after the wire-format dialect fix
(fizeau-073fe18b) and token-extraction (fizeau-8f62bcbb) beads land. Reps
the 2026-05-11 probe against the corrected wire forms, populates the
remaining catalog entry for ds4, and lands the end-to-end verification.

Note: the prior populate bead fizeau-b83502c0 is already closed, but it
only addressed the OR-Qwen3 catalog value and the surface-ID lookup
fix. ds4 catalog entry was deferred (vidar was unreachable from the
exec env), and sindri was found to be still-broken at probe time
because the Qwen wire format hadn't been fixed yet.

Steps:

1. **Re-probe all four lanes** with cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning under
   docs/research/probe-reasoning-2026-05-11-v2/<lane>/:
   - fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b — expect reasoning_tokens ≈ 2048 ± 30%
     at `reasoning: low` (token-budget honored).
   - sindri-club-3090-llamacpp — expect reasoning_tokens > 0 across all
     non-off rows (chat_template_kwargs envelope active per
     fizeau-073fe18b).
   - vidar-ds4 — expect reasoning_content populated; reasoning_tokens
     derived via fizeau-8f62bcbb extractor fallback.
   - vidar-qwen3-6-27b (legacy oMLX) — if still in use; else delete the
     profile.

2. **Catalog entry for deepseek-v4-flash** in
   internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml. Reference the
   qwen3.6-27b entry structure. Set:
   - family: deepseek
   - display_name: DeepSeek V4 Flash
   - status: active
   - provider_system: openai
   - deployment_class: local_free
   - power: TBD (operator's call based on benchmark results)
   - context_window: 393216 (matches ds4 /props.runtime.ctx_size)
   - reasoning_levels: [high, max]   # per /props.aliases collapse
   - reasoning_control: tunable      # two practically-distinct tiers
   - reasoning_wire: effort          # flat reasoning_effort top-level
   - reasoning_default: 'high'       # per /props.reasoning.default
   - surfaces:
       agent.openai: deepseek-v4-flash
   - reasoning_budgets: (omit — no client-budget control on this build)

3. **Verification sweep** — run a single short TB-2.1 task on each of
   the four lanes. report.json must carry:
   - reasoning_tokens > 0 (either direct or via reasoning_content
     fallback)
   - reasoning_tokens_approx flag accurate to source
   - The wire body in the session jsonl matches what the L1/L2/L3
     selection would predict

4. **Documentation** — add a note in docs/research/ summarizing the
   2026-05-11 wire-form findings, the probe artifacts, and a
   one-paragraph operator runbook for what to do when a new model is
   added.

In-scope files:

description:
Modify the cell-write boundary so every report.json embeds the full resolved profile snapshot, plus framework, dataset, and a new cell_id.

The current cell write at cmd/bench/matrix.go (around the area that stamps profile_id + profile_snapshot, near line 751) writes profile_id only. New behavior: serialize the resolved Profile struct into the report.json as an embedded 'profile' field.

Cell record additions:
- profile: <full resolved Profile struct, including all metadata, sampling, limits, pricing, harness, surface, concurrency_group, versioning>
- framework: string (from bench-set, e.g. 'terminal-bench')
- dataset: string (from bench-set, e.g. 'terminal-bench-2-1')
- cell_id: string (ISO timestamp + short random, e.g. '20260516T103045Z-a4c1')

cell_id generation:
  time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z") + "-" + shortRandom(4)
where shortRandom returns a 4-char hex from crypto/rand.

The cell directory layout changes: new cells write to cells/<framework>-<dataset>/<task>/<cell-id>/report.json (no profile_id in path, no rep-NNN). Backfill (PR 2) brings old cells into this layout.

profile_id and profile_snapshot string fields stay on the cell record for backward-compatibility through PR 3 (analytics switch); PR 4 deletes them.

In-scope files:
- cmd/bench/matrix.go (cell write boundary)
- cmd/bench/matrix_test.go (extend coverage)
- internal/benchmark/profile/profile.go (if a helper for resolved-snapshot serialization is needed)

Out-of-scope:
- New shell driver (PR 1d)
- Backfill of existing cells (PR 2)
- Analytics switch (PR 3)
- Deletion of lane scaffolding (PR 4)

4. `TestAssembleSuppressesCatalogOnlyModels` verifies catalog-only entries that no configured provider discovers do not appear in the snapshot.

description:
Build the in-memory `ModelSnapshot` view by enumerating all configured
providers (incl harnesses-as-providers), reading per-source caches
(via M1), parsing model IDs via the version-aware ranker (bead A), and
joining with catalog metadata (power, cost, reasoning_levels,
quota_pool from bead E1).

## Public API sketch

```go
package modelregistry

type Source string
const (
    SourceNativeAPI    Source = "native_api"
    SourceHarnessPTY   Source = "harness_pty"
    SourcePropsAPI     Source = "props_api"
)

type ModelStatus string
const (
    StatusAvailable     ModelStatus = "available"
    StatusRateLimited   ModelStatus = "rate_limited"
    StatusUnreachable   ModelStatus = "unreachable"
    StatusUnknown       ModelStatus = "unknown"
)

type KnownModel struct {
    // Identity (per ADR-012; matches ModelRef)
    Provider      string
    ID            string

    // How fizeau learned of it
    DiscoveredVia Source
    DiscoveredAt  time.Time

    // Parsed via bead A
    Family        string
    Version       []int
    Tier          modelcatalog.Tier
    PreRelease    bool

    // From catalog join
    Power            int
    CostInputPerM    float64
    CostOutputPerM   float64
    ContextWindow    int
    ReasoningLevels  []string
    QuotaPool        string  // E1

    // Composed eligibility (consumed by routing)
    AutoRoutable    bool      // catalog × IncludeByDefault × Status
    ExclusionReason string
}

type ModelSnapshot struct {
    Models  []KnownModel
    AsOf    time.Time
    Sources map[string]SourceMeta
}

type SourceMeta struct {
    LastRefreshedAt time.Time
    Stale           bool
    Error           string
}

func Assemble(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, cat *modelcatalog.Catalog, cache *discoverycache.Cache) (ModelSnapshot, error)
```

## Provider enumeration

Iterates `cfg.Providers` (existing user config) plus implicit
subscription harnesses (claude-subscription, codex-subscription) when
their CLIs are installed and authenticated.

## Per-source discovery

Each provider type has a discovery adapter:
- openai-compat (openrouter, openai, vidar-ds4, sindri-llamacpp): GET
  `/v1/models` via cache
- ds4: also GET `/props`
- claude / codex harnesses: PTY-based cassette read via cache (existing
  model_discovery.go code, now wrapped through M1's cache)

Adapters return `[]string` (model IDs); assembler produces
`KnownModel` per ID.

## Tier ranker integration

Each model ID parsed via `modelcatalog.tiers.Parse(id)` (bead A). Sets
Family/Version/Tier/PreRelease.

## Catalog join

For each (provider, model) candidate, look up the catalog entry. If
present, populate Power/Cost/ContextWindow/ReasoningLevels/QuotaPool.
If not, leave zero/empty (snapshot still includes the entry —
provider-discovered models without catalog metadata are still
routable, just with limited info).

## AutoRoutable composition

`AutoRoutable = catalog.AutoRoutable() AND providerConfig.IncludeByDefault
AND status != StatusUnreachable AND status != StatusUnknown`. (Per
bead D's `IncludeByDefault` filter wiring.)

## Catalog-only suppression

Per operator direction: if a catalog model is NOT discovered by any
configured provider, it does NOT appear in the snapshot. The catalog
is implementation detail.

## In-scope files

- internal/modelregistry/types.go — KnownModel, ModelSnapshot, etc.
- internal/modelregistry/assemble.go — top-level Assemble function
- internal/modelregistry/discovery.go — per-provider-type adapters
  (uses existing code in internal/provider/openai/discovery.go and
  internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/model_discovery.go through M1's
  cache wrapper)
- internal/modelregistry/enrich.go — catalog join + AutoRoutable
- internal/modelregistry/assemble_test.go
- internal/modelregistry/discovery_test.go
- internal/modelregistry/enrich_test.go

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M1 (cache + ParseModelRef) implemented
- Bead A (`fizeau-b2c5c826`, version-aware ranker)
- Bead E1 (`fizeau-c04be6b0`, quota_pool field)

## Out of scope

- Runtime signal collection (M3 — Status comes from M3's runtime cache).
- CLI (M4).
- OR sub-provider expansion (M5).

acceptance:
1. TestRouteHealth_PersistsAcrossInstances asserts that a Store with PersistRouteHealth set, after recording a route failure and shutting down, is re-readable by a new Store instance with the same path, returning the failed-route record within HealthSignalTTL.
2. TestRouteHealth_TTLExpiresStaleSignals asserts that signals older than HealthSignalTTL are not returned by the new Store instance.
3. TestRouteHealth_MissingFileBootsClean asserts that an unreadable / non-existent persistence file results in an empty Store with a single-line warning log, never an error returned to the caller.
4. TestRouteHealth_CorruptFileBootsClean asserts the same for malformed JSON.
5. ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth defaults to "" (no persistence) — regression guard for callers that don't opt in.
6. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes PersistRouteHealth + HealthSignalTTL.
description:
PROBLEM
internal/routehealth.Store tracks per-route attempt feedback (failures, cooldowns, sticky leases) inside a single Fizeau process. ddx work and ddx try are short-lived processes that each instantiate their own Store on startup, so health learned during one ddx invocation is invisible to the next. Observed today: bragi:1234 was unreachable. Worker 1's ddx try learned that, recorded a route-failure, and excluded bragi for its remaining attempts. When worker 1 finished and worker 2's next ddx try started, the new Fizeau process had a fresh empty routehealth.Store and immediately re-routed to bragi — same dial timeout, same failure. The exclusion list visible via ddx-side work-failed-routes (which IS persisted in .ddx/beads.jsonl per bead) helps within a bead's attempts but not for cross-bead provider health: every new bead starts assuming bragi is healthy, eats 5-30s of dial timeout, then learns again.

ROOT CAUSE
service.go:974-976 (NewService) constructs routehealth.NewStore() / NewLeaseStore() / NewUtilizationStore() with no persistence backing. The package internal/routehealth has no on-disk format; everything is in-process maps. There is no documented contract for whether health signals should persist, expire, or be considered process-local by design.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an optional ServiceOptions field PersistRouteHealth string (path or directory) that, when set, causes routehealth.Store to read/write a small JSON or jsonl health snapshot on shutdown and at periodic intervals (default 60s).
- Snapshot scope: per-route failure counts, cooldown-until timestamps, success counters. Treat the persistence as best-effort; missing/corrupt file ⇒ start with empty state and log a warning.
- Health snapshots expire on read: any entry whose recorded timestamp is older than HealthSignalTTL (default 10 min) is dropped. So a brief outage learned by one process expires before the next process even if the file is not rewritten.
- DDx side will set PersistRouteHealth to ".ddx/routehealth.json" so all ddx work / ddx try / ddx run invocations within a project share the signal. Filed in companion bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the in-process routehealth API surface; persistence is additive.
- Do not introduce a network-backed health store (out of scope; persistence is per-machine).
- Do not change cooldown semantics; this is only about cross-process visibility of what was already learned.

DEPS
None.
title: routing: persist provider-health signals across ddx process boundaries

description:
Replace scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py with a Node collector under
scripts/benchmark/collector/. Port the PROFILE_ALIASES rescue table for orphan cells.
Recover deleted profile YAMLs from git history. Audit the legacy benchmark-results/
tree and emit a coverage report for PR F's deletion decision.

CONTEXT: today's analytics pipeline is scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py — Python.
It walks bench/results/.../cells/, applies PROFILE_ALIASES + EXCLUDED_PROFILES from
~line 150 to handle cells with profile_id matching one of four deleted profiles
(sindri-club-3090, vidar-qwen3-6-27b-openai-compat, sindri-club-3090-llamacpp,
gpt-5-3-mini — collectively ~663 cells), and emits cells.json for the website. We're
replacing the Python with Node (the website is already Node — no extra runtime). The
orphan rescue table is preserved, ported to Node.

DEPENDS ON: PR B (collector validates cells against cell-report.schema.json).

SCOPE — extend scripts/benchmark/collector/ (created in PR B):
  - collector/walk-cells.ts — recursive read of bench/results/<canonical>/cells/**/
    report.json. Validates each via ajv against cell-report.schema.json. Applies
    forward-compat shim (fills nulls for missing fields, maps legacy fiz_tools_version
    -> report_schema_version). Emits row objects matching
    docs/benchmarks/schema/benchmark-cells.schema.json (213-line aggregated shape).
  - collector/to-parquet.ts — writes rows to <out>/cells.parquet using @dsnp/parquetjs.
  - collector/to-json.ts — writes rows to <out>/cells.json matching benchmark-cells.schema.json.
  - collector/to-summary.ts — writes <out>/summary.csv and <out>/summary.md (counts by
    profile, framework, task, rep; pass rates).
  - collector/orphan-profile-aliases.json — port of PROFILE_ALIASES + EXCLUDED_PROFILES
    from scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py:150. Structure:
      { \"aliases\": { \"<orphan_id>\": \"<current_or_archived_id>\" },
        \"excluded\": [ \"<id>\", ... ] }
  - collector/cost-recompute.ts — optional pass that recomputes cost from current
    pricing files and emits cost_usd_recomputed alongside cost_usd_at_run_time in the
    aggregated output. Cells are NOT back-rewritten.
  - collector/audit-legacy.ts — one-shot mode: invoked as
      node collector/bin/cli.js collect --audit-legacy benchmark-results/
    Walks the legacy benchmark-results/ tree, reports cells present only there (by
    profile + framework + task + rep), writes <out>/legacy-audit.json. Output drives
    PR F's delete/migrate decision.
  - collector/bin/cli.js — extend with full subcommands:
      node collector/bin/cli.js collect [--canonical fiz-tools-v1]
        [--out bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/indexes/] [--audit-legacy <dir>] [--dry-run]
  - Walker handles cells with profile_id missing OR profile.id missing:
      1. Try to read embedded profile: block. Use directly if present.
      2. Else, look up profile_id in scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml.
      3. Else, look up in orphan-profile-aliases.json:
           - If 'excluded': skip with reason 'excluded by alias table'.
           - If 'aliases.<orphan_id>': look up <alias_target> in profiles/ then
             profiles/_archived/ (PR D ships archived YAMLs — see below).
      4. Else: emit row with profile_resolved=false in <out>/audit.json (the
         walker's own audit, not the --audit-legacy one). Do NOT silently skip.

SCOPE — recover archived profile YAMLs:
  - scripts/benchmark/recover-archived-profiles.sh: one-shot bash script. For each
    of the four deleted profiles {sindri-club-3090, vidar-qwen3-6-27b-openai-compat,
    sindri-club-3090-llamacpp, gpt-5-3-mini}:
      commit=\$(git log --all --diff-filter=D --pretty=%H -- \\
        \"scripts/benchmark/profiles/\${id}.yaml\" | head -1)
      git show \"\${commit}^:scripts/benchmark/profiles/\${id}.yaml\" \\
        > \"scripts/benchmark/profiles/_archived/\${id}.yaml\"
    Then prepend YAML frontmatter to the recovered file:
      # source_commit: <sha>
      # archived_at: <date>
      # archived_reason: deleted from main; retained for orphan-cell resolution
    Run the script once, commit the 4 _archived/ files. Script may be deleted from
    main after the recovery commit (it's a one-shot).

SCOPE — deletes:
  - scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py
  - scripts/website/test_build_benchmark_data.py
  - scripts/website/__pycache__/ (rm -rf; gitignore already excludes)

SCOPE — audits (no deletion, just confirms compatibility):
  - scripts/website/build-benchmark-workbench.mjs — read this Node file, confirm it
    consumes the new cells.json shape correctly. If the expected shape diverges,
    patch the .mjs (smallest diff to align). Document outcome in the PR description.

SCOPE — CI gate:
  - Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml (after PR B's gate):
      - name: Collector dry-run against fixture cells
        run: node scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/cli.js collect --dry-run \\
               --root scripts/benchmark/testdata/cells/
    Must exit 0 with zero silent skips reported in stdout.

IN-SCOPE files:
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/walk-cells.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/to-parquet.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/to-json.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/to-summary.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/orphan-profile-aliases.json (new — ported data)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/cost-recompute.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/audit-legacy.ts (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/cli.js (extend PR B's stub)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/package.json (add @dsnp/parquetjs, js-yaml deps)
  - scripts/benchmark/recover-archived-profiles.sh (new one-shot)
  - scripts/benchmark/profiles/_archived/sindri-club-3090.yaml (recovered)
  - scripts/benchmark/profiles/_archived/vidar-qwen3-6-27b-openai-compat.yaml (recovered)
  - scripts/benchmark/profiles/_archived/sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.yaml (recovered)
  - scripts/benchmark/profiles/_archived/gpt-5-3-mini.yaml (recovered)
  - scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py (DELETE)
  - scripts/website/test_build_benchmark_data.py (DELETE)
  - scripts/website/build-benchmark-workbench.mjs (audit + maybe patch)
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml (extend — add collector dry-run step)

OUT OF SCOPE:
  - Evidence importers (PR E).
  - Deleting Go (PR F).

ROLLBACK: collector is new code; deletion of build-benchmark-data.py is reversible
via git revert. Archived profile YAMLs once committed stay committed.

ACCEPTANCE — all pass on a clean checkout:
  1. node scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/cli.js collect produces
     bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/indexes/{cells.parquet, cells.json, summary.csv,
     summary.md} from the existing cell tree, with ZERO silent skips reported.
     Every cell either validates, resolves via orphan alias, or appears in
     <out>/audit.json with a specific reason.
  2. DuckDB query works:
       duckdb -c \"SELECT model_family, COUNT(*), AVG(CAST(passed AS INT))
                   FROM 'bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/indexes/cells.parquet'
                   GROUP BY model_family;\"
     Returns rows; doesn't crash.
  3. Synthetic test: create a cell with profile_id: sindri-club-3090 (a deleted
     profile). Run the collector. The cell appears in cells.json with non-null
     model_family resolved via orphan-profile-aliases.json + the _archived/ YAML.
  4. node scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/cli.js collect --audit-legacy
     benchmark-results/ produces a coverage report at the configured --out path,
     listing cells present only in benchmark-results/.
  5. ls scripts/benchmark/profiles/_archived/{sindri-club-3090,
     vidar-qwen3-6-27b-openai-compat,sindri-club-3090-llamacpp,gpt-5-3-mini}.yaml
     all exist with 'source_commit:' frontmatter.
  6. test ! -f scripts/website/build-benchmark-data.py
  7. test ! -f scripts/website/test_build_benchmark_data.py
  8. CI: the collector dry-run step in .github/workflows/ci.yml passes when triggered.

description:
PROBLEM
Independent verification of closed bead fizeau-7adc4c19 failed its fourth acceptance criterion. The command `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --subset terminalbench-2-1-canary --lanes fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp --dry-run` exits 2 with `unknown flag: --subset`, so operators cannot run the documented ad-hoc subset dry-run through the wrapper even though the underlying bench sweep supports it. The same error path prints shell noise like `line 136: all: command not found` because the usage heredoc evaluates backticked text.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh:198-244` parses wrapper flags but only accepts `--phase`, `--lanes`, output/tasks/plan/dry-run/prepare/budget options, and rejects unknown flags; it never forwards `--subset`. `cmd/bench/sweep.go:211-218` defines the underlying `fiz-bench sweep --subset` flag, and `cmd/bench/sweep.go:957-1001` implements the ad-hoc subset + lanes path. `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh:136-156` uses an unquoted heredoc while embedding backticks around words like `all`, `full`, and `tb21-all`, which shell-evaluates those words while printing usage.

PROPOSED FIX
Teach the wrapper to accept `--subset <id>` and `--subset=<id>`, treat it as mutually exclusive with `--phase`, display it in usage/resume output, and forward it to `fiz-bench sweep` so the ad-hoc subset path can validate profiles and print a dry-run plan. Quote or escape the usage heredoc so help/error output is clean. Add focused tests around wrapper argument behavior instead of requiring a full TerminalBench run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh`
- A focused test file under the existing Go or shell test surface for benchmark wrapper argument parsing/dry-run behavior
- Minimal test hooks in `cmd/bench` only if needed to run the wrapper without external downloads

NON-SCOPE
- Full TerminalBench execution
- Changing recipe/subset YAML semantics
- Changing DS4 provider behavior or MTP performance claims
- Reworking benchmark result directories or generated artifacts
title: benchmark wrapper: support ad-hoc subset dry runs

description:
Add a strict JSON schema for the per-cell report.json the runner writes. Validate at
write time via a minimal Node validator. Wire into CI.

CONTEXT: today the per-cell report.json shape is de facto (look at any cell under
bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/cells/*/report.json for the current shape). There's an
aggregated cells schema at docs/benchmarks/schema/benchmark-cells.schema.json (213 lines)
that the collector targets, but the per-cell source has no formal contract. Result:
fields drift, importers silently miss things, the runner can write half-baked cells
without notice.

DEPENDS ON: PR A (the bash runner needs to exist to call the validator).

SCOPE:
  - Author docs/benchmarks/schema/cell-report.schema.json covering all fields below.
    Required + optional structure, types, enums, value constraints.
  - Create scripts/benchmark/collector/ directory with:
      - package.json declaring 'ajv' and 'ajv-formats' as deps and a
        bin: { benchctl: \"./bin/cli.js\" } entry (cli.js is a stub here; PR D extends it)
      - bin/validate-cell.js — small standalone validator that takes a path arg,
        reads cell-report.schema.json, validates the JSON file, exits 0 on pass,
        prints the validation error and exits non-zero on fail.
      - bin/validate-cell.js also implements a MINIMAL forward-compat shim: maps
        legacy fiz_tools_version -> report_schema_version when validating old cells.
        PR D extends the shim for the full walker.
  - Modify scripts/benchmark/benchmark (the runner from PR A) to call
      node scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/validate-cell.js \$cell_dir/report.json
    at cell close. Validation failure -> cell marked final_status=setup_failed,
    invalid_class=invalid_setup, with the validator's error message in a 'note' field.
    (~50ms Node fork overhead per cell; acceptable for canary, monitor for full sweeps.)
  - Cell-id format upgrade in the runner: millisecond + 8-char random
    (e.g. 20260516T103045123Z-abc12345). Today is second-precision + 4 chars.
  - Make required (in cell-report.schema.json): model_server, machine,
    concurrent_cells_on_machine_at_start, harbor_runner_image_digest, plus the
    Identity / Schema versioning / Profile / Agent invocation / Outcome / Metrics /
    Attempt provenance groups below.
  - Add CI step to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
      - name: Validate fixture cells
        run: |
          for f in scripts/benchmark/testdata/cells/*/report.json; do
            node scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/validate-cell.js \"\$f\" || exit 1
          done
  - Add 5+ fixture cells to scripts/benchmark/testdata/cells/ (committed) for the CI
    gate to validate against. Use real cells from bench/results/.../sindri-lucebox/
    as the source.

SCHEMA — per-cell report.json fields (required unless marked optional):

  Identity:
    cell_id (string, pattern ^[0-9]{8}T[0-9]{9}Z-[a-z0-9]{8}\$)
    framework (string), dataset (string), dataset_commit (string),
    dataset_version (string), task_id (string), rep (integer >=1)

  Schema versioning:
    report_schema_version (integer, const 1 for now)

  Profile (embedded snapshot — already in current cells):
    profile (object with id, provider, sampling, limits, pricing,
             agent_timeout_multiplier, harness, surface, concurrency_group,
             metadata, versioning)
    profile_path (string, MUST be repo-relative not absolute — schema enforces
                  the regex ^(?!/) so absolute paths fail validation)
    pricing_source (object with path:string, sha256:string)

  Agent invocation:
    command (array of string), env_redacted (object: string->string),
    agent_name (string), agent_version (string), agent_binary_path (string)

  Runtime conditions:
    started_at (string, format date-time), finished_at (string, format date-time),
    wall_seconds (number), runner_version (string, sha),
    harness_adapter_version (string, sha), task_executor_version (string),
    harbor_runner_image_digest (string, pattern ^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}\$),
    model_server (object: name, version, commit, endpoint),
    machine (object: hostname, gpu_model, vram_gb, cpu_model, os, kernel),
    concurrent_cells_on_machine_at_start (integer >=0)

  Outcome (runner-written):
    process_outcome (enum: completed, timeout, killed, setup_failed, harness_crash)
    grading_outcome (enum: pass, fail, partial, error, ungraded)
    invalid_class (enum: invalid_setup, invalid_provider, invalid_quota, invalid_auth,
                   null)
    final_status (enum: ran, interrupted, budget_halted, setup_failed, harness_crash)
    passed (boolean), reward (number or null), exit_code (integer)

  Metrics:
    turns (integer or null), tool_calls (integer or null),
    tool_call_errors (integer or null), input_tokens, output_tokens,
    cached_input_tokens, retried_input_tokens, reasoning_tokens,
    reasoning_tokens_approx (boolean), total_tokens (all integer or null),
    cost_usd_at_run_time (number >=0)

  Attempt provenance:
    attempt_of (string or null — points to prior cell-id this supersedes)
    superseded_by (string or null — written by later retry)

IN-SCOPE files:
  - docs/benchmarks/schema/cell-report.schema.json (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/package.json (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/collector/bin/validate-cell.js (new)
  - scripts/benchmark/benchmark (edit — add validator invocation + cell-id format)
  - scripts/benchmark/testdata/cells/<cell-id>/report.json (new — 5+ fixtures)

OUT OF SCOPE:

  - The bench-set schema changes (PR C).
  - Forward-compat shim for fields that don't exist on old cells beyond
    fiz_tools_version -> report_schema_version mapping (PR D).

ROLLBACK: Validator failures during PR A's runner are non-fatal in that they don't
break the sweep — they mark cells invalid_setup. If the schema is wrong, fix the
schema; don't relax to accept invalid cells.

4. Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against the claude subscription harness routes through the claude harness (not fiz/openrouter). Verified by a test that sets up a claude harness mock and asserts dispatch destination.
5. The routing_decision event for any excluded same-harness tier carries an explicit filter_reason — silent absence of a candidate row is forbidden when the tier is known to the catalog. Verified by negative test: artificially break sonnet-4.6's catalog metadata and assert it appears with filter_reason:"power_missing" (or similar) rather than vanishing.

description:
PROBLEM
Under policy=default (auto-route mode, no --harness/--provider/--model pin), Fizeau collapses each subprocess harness to ONE scoring candidate — its DefaultModel. Multi-tier subscription harnesses like claude (which advertises sonnet/opus/haiku as siblings under one auth) therefore only ever offer their default tier to the auto-route scorer. Sonnet and haiku can never win on cost vs opus even though the catalog has full power/cost metadata for all three.

Concrete symptom from downstream DDx caller: every policy=default dispatch lands on claude/opus-4.7 because internal/harnesses/registry.go:51 sets DefaultModel="opus-4.7". For a 2207-token implementation prompt at role=implementer, opus won score 100.5. When the same dispatch is rerun with --model sonnet-4.6 pinned, sonnet scores 196.0 — it would have beaten opus by 95.5 points if it had been enumerated.

Cost gap is material: opus is 5x sonnet on input tokens, ~5x on output (cost_input_per_m: 15.0 vs 3.0; cost_output_per_m: 75.0 vs 15.0 per internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml). A project running ddx work continuously is defaulting every dispatch to the most expensive tier.

ROOT CAUSE (tentative — pointers, not patch)
service_routing.go:1172-1189 (auto-route candidate add loop):

    for _, h := range entries {
        if h.DefaultModel != "" {
            add(h.DefaultModel, true, status)      // adds opus-4.7 for claude
        }
        for _, modelID := range h.SupportedModels {
            add(modelID, true, status)              // SHOULD add sonnet-4.6 too
        }
        ...
    }

subprocessHarnessModelIDs("claude", cfg) at service_models.go:91-101 returns the full ["sonnet", "sonnet-4.6", "opus", "opus-4.7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"] set. So sonnet SHOULD be reaching the candidate pool. Evidence says it does not — three candidate causes I could not disambiguate without deeper reading of routing.Inputs construction:
1. Downstream aggregation/dedup is keeping one model per {harness, provider} key (picking DefaultModel as representative).
2. Eligibility map keyed by modelID at ~line 1170 is collapsing sonnet entries by family before the candidate list is emitted.
3. h.SupportedModels is being populated empty at the call site that builds entries (different from the metadata_billing.go:71 path).

The routing_decision event is unambiguous: no sonnet row at all in the candidates array, not even excluded. A row with eligible:false, filter_reason:... would be diagnostic; silent absence is consistent with "never enumerated."

CATALOG IS NOT THE GAP
internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml has complete sonnet-4.6 metadata: power 8 (vs opus 10), cost_input_per_m 3.0, cost_output_per_m 15.0, surfaces include agent.anthropic:sonnet-4.6 and claude-code:sonnet-4.6. This rules out the power_missing exclusion path (the one that drops openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 with filter_reason: "power_missing").

SECOND BUG SURFACED BY THE REPRO
Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 routes through fiz/openrouter (catalog's openrouter_id), NOT through the claude subscription harness. So pinning by model name does not produce a same-harness alternative tier even when one exists on the same auth. The openrouter route then fails 401 because no auth header is configured. So the workaround "pin sonnet by name" doesn't actually deliver sonnet on the claude subscription path; it falls back to a separate per-token provider.

PROPOSED FIX (direction, not prescriptive)
For multi-tier subscription harnesses, enumerate one candidate per tier (opus, sonnet, haiku) with each tier's catalog power/cost, all routing through the same harness. Let cost-aware scoring pick the cheapest tier that meets power_hint_fit for the prompt. Today the harness behaves like "one model, take it or leave it"; it should behave like "one auth, multiple tiers."

If aggregation-by-harness is intentional for some other reason, the alternative is to make the harness configurable to expose multiple DefaultModels per role/power-band, and have callers pass a power hint that selects the right one.

REPRO
1. Configure Fizeau with the claude harness (subscription path, no model pin).
2. Send any execute request with policy=default, no --harness/--provider/--model.
3. Inspect the routing_decision event — only claude/opus-4.7 appears under claude; no sonnet, no haiku.
4. Resend with --model sonnet-4.6. Route goes to fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 with score 196.0 (not to the claude subscription path), and fails 401 if openrouter is not authed.

CALLER CONTEXT
DDx CLI on main branch at commit 09881f3a5. DDx does not pre-resolve routing knobs (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010) and passes policy=default through verbatim. Verified by grep: no routing_decision/power_hint_fit/score_components emitter in DDx — only in github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.12.2.

NON-SCOPE
- The openrouter 401 (separate Fizeau config issue: that route needs an auth header configured).
- DDx-side changes; ddx work is doing the right thing per contract.
- Catalog metadata additions (sonnet-4.6 metadata is already complete).

CROSS-REF
Github issue: https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6 (mirrors this bead for external visibility).
notes: decomposed into fizeau-f7fa1d87 (engine: enumerate per-tier candidates for multi-tier subscription harnesses in default policy), fizeau-59df1d15 (routing: pin --model sonnet-4.6 routes through claude subscription harness, not openrouter fallback), fizeau-b11da788 (routing: emit filter_reason for excluded same-harness tier — no silent absence). Splits the 7 ACs across three subsystem-focused children: engine enumeration + per-tier cost (1,2,3 of parent); model-pin→subscription-harness preference (4); observability/filter_reason invariant (5). ACs 6,7 (green tests, no regression) apply to each child.
title: auto-route: enumerate per-tier candidates for multi-tier subscription harnesses (claude opus/sonnet/haiku)

description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau wastes 2m45s per call retrying a dead local endpoint (4 attempts with 1s/2s/4s/8s backoff + per-attempt 30s dial timeout), then surfaces a provider_connectivity error to the caller. Even when the caller (DDx) records the failed (provider, model) and asks for re-routing on the next attempt, Fizeau re-routes to the same dead endpoint because the caller has no way to express "exclude this (provider, model)" in the request, and routing decisions are made in isolation from caller-side health signals. Observed reproduction: with bragi:1234/qwen3.5-27b unreachable, a DDx drain of 12 beads attempted each bead, every bead routed to bragi/qwen3.5-27b, every bead spent ~2m45s in Fizeau retries, every bead failed identically. Net drain time ~33 minutes, zero beads completed.

ROOT CAUSE
Two contributing behaviors in internal/provider/* and internal/serviceimpl/*:
1. Connect-time errors (dial tcp ECONNREFUSED / ETIMEDOUT to a local IP) are treated the same as in-flight provider errors and run through the full retry curve (4 attempts × ~30s = 2m45s). A local-network dial that misses the SYN window will not succeed on subsequent attempts; the retry burns wall-clock for no value.
2. The route decision in internal/routehealth and the public RouteRequest type carry no caller-supplied exclusion list. Routes recorded as failed by the caller cannot be expressed to Fizeau. The internal routehealth Store tracks process-local feedback only — fine for within-call retries, but stateless across calls.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add ExcludedRoutes []ExcludedRoute to the public RouteRequest, where ExcludedRoute names (Provider, Model, optional Endpoint). The router must skip any candidate matching the list. Document in service.go and the routing API doc.
- For connect-time errors (errors.As to *net.OpError with Op=="dial" AND ECONNREFUSED/ETIMEDOUT/EHOSTUNREACH), bypass the retry curve: 1 attempt, fail fast within ~5s. Reserve the retry curve for in-flight 5xx / partial-response errors that may transient retry usefully.
- When the request lists multiple viable endpoints for the same model (current or future endpoint-first config), on connect-failure to one endpoint, immediately try the next endpoint in the same call rather than surfacing failure. The order matches the existing route-decision strategy.
- Expose a routing-quality event recording "route excluded by caller hint" so observability can distinguish caller-driven re-routes from internal health signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign provider config syntax. ExcludedRoutes is additive; endpoint-list support can land separately.
- Do not change the existing routehealth in-process Store contract; only extend RouteRequest.
- Do not alter retry behavior for non-transport-class provider errors (5xx, rate limit, content-policy) in this bead.

DEPS
None at Fizeau; DDx-side bead to actually populate ExcludedRoutes from execute-loop-failed-routes is filed separately under ddx (ddx-8a7a6843 covers the DDx escalation-signaling side).
title: routing: honor caller exclusions and fast-fail on transport timeouts

acceptance:

2. `go test . -run TestServiceStartup_SkippedProvidersRemainAbsentFromProbeUnreachable -count=1` passes and proves skipped providers are not projected into `routing.Inputs.ProbeUnreachable`.
3. `go test . -run TestProbeLoop_RetriesDeadProvidersOnInterval -count=1` still passes and unprobed endpoints remain `ProbeNeeded` for the background loop.

description:
Problem
The startup aliveness probe currently records endpoints that were never probed as failed when the total startup probe budget is exhausted. That creates false `endpoint_unreachable` evidence: one slow/down endpoint at the front of the list can mark later live providers as dead for the HealthSignalTTL window, and that false state may be persisted. Specs allow hard gates for known-down endpoints, but skipped is unknown, not dispatchability evidence.

Spec context
- FEAT-004 limits hard gates to explicit user constraints and dispatchability; known-down endpoints qualify, but unprobed endpoints do not.
- The model-first routing plan chose passive availability with bounded failover to avoid startup latency and probe-only failure modes.
- FEAT-003 says failed utilization probes preserve unknown/stale states rather than guessing unavailable; startup aliveness should follow the same evidence discipline.

In-scope files
- service_aliveness.go
- service_aliveness_test.go
- internal/routehealth/probe_store.go only if an explicit unknown/skipped representation is needed

Out of scope
- Route-time freshness orchestration; that is tracked in the dependent bead
- Discovery cache retry semantics; tracked separately
- Cloud/subscription health probing

Implementation guidance
Record a failed probe for the endpoint whose actual prober call timed out or returned false. Do not record failures for endpoints that were not contacted. Ensure the background probe loop still treats unprobed endpoints as needing a probe.
notes: Worker codex/gpt-5.5 produced a passing implementation in side worktree; DDx auto-land hit land_conflict_unresolvable, so the patch was integrated manually in the main tree. Verified locally: go test . -run TestServiceStartup_TotalTimeoutDoesNotRecordUnprobedProvidersAsFailed -count=1; go test . -run TestServiceStartup_SkippedProvidersRemainAbsentFromProbeUnreachable -count=1; go test . -run TestProbeLoop_RetriesDeadProvidersOnInterval -count=1; go test . ./internal/routehealth ./internal/routing -count=1; go test ./... all pass. Pending close after commit.
title: routing: keep startup-skipped providers unknown, not unreachable

Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against a configuration that includes the claude subscription harness routes to fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 (score 196.0) instead of the claude subscription path. description:
Implement the per-source on-disk cache per ADR-012. New package
`internal/discoverycache/`. Foundation for M2 (snapshot assembler) and
M3 (runtime signals); no consumers in this bead.

Also includes the canonical model identity helpers (`ModelRef`,
`ParseModelRef`) since they're the cache's primary key.

## In-scope files

- internal/discoverycache/cache.go — public API (Get, ForceRefresh,
  WriteRefresh, Prune)
- internal/discoverycache/lock.go — mutation lock (extends
  acquireMatrixLock idiom; PID+atomic-create with crash recovery)
- internal/discoverycache/marker.go — refresh marker (PID+StartedAt+
  Deadline; staleness detection)
- internal/discoverycache/atomic_write.go — tmp+fsync+rename helper
- internal/discoverycache/singleflight.go — composes
  golang.org/x/sync/singleflight with file marker for cross-process
- internal/discoverycache/cache_test.go — basic single-process tests
- internal/discoverycache/multiprocess_test.go — the 10 mandatory
  tests using `TestHelperProcess` / child-spawn pattern
- internal/modelref/ref.go — canonical identity helpers
  (ModelRef struct, Parse, String, validation rules)
- internal/modelref/ref_test.go — table tests for parser

## Algorithms to implement (per ADR-012)

All 6 from ADR-012:
1. claim_refresh(source) → (ClaimedByMe | AlreadyInFlight(marker))
2. refresh_and_commit(source, dataBytes) — atomic rename + marker
   cleanup
3. wait_for_refresh(source, marker, maxWait) — polling
4. read(source) → (data, age, fresh_bool)
5. force_refresh(source, refresher func()) — sync
6. In-process singleflight composition — use
   golang.org/x/sync/singleflight, composes with file-marker

## Public API sketch

```go
type Cache struct {
    Root string  // e.g. ~/.cache/fizeau
}

type Source struct {
    Tier            string  // "discovery" or "runtime"
    Name            string  // e.g. "openrouter", "claude-subscription"
    TTL             time.Duration
    RefreshDeadline time.Duration
}

type ReadResult struct {
    Data       []byte
    Age        time.Duration
    Fresh      bool  // true if Age < TTL
    Stale      bool  // true if Age >= TTL
}

func (c *Cache) Read(s Source) (ReadResult, error)

// Refresher is the source-fetch function. Cache handles single-flight
// + atomic write + marker lifecycle.
type Refresher func(ctx context.Context) ([]byte, error)

func (c *Cache) Refresh(s Source, fn Refresher) error          // sync, force
func (c *Cache) MaybeRefresh(s Source, fn Refresher)           // async, no-op if fresh
func (c *Cache) Prune(activeSources []Source) error             // remove unknown
```

## Canonical identity (`internal/modelref`)

```go
type ModelRef struct {
    Provider string  // slug: lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens, dots
    ID       string  // provider's canonical model ID; case-preserved
}

func Parse(s string) (ModelRef, error)  // split on first /
func (r ModelRef) String() string         // <provider>/<id>
func (r ModelRef) Validate() error        // empty checks, char rules
```

Edge cases per ADR / discussion:
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b` → provider=openrouter, id=qwen/qwen3.6-27b
- `vidar-ds4/deepseek-v4-flash` → provider=vidar-ds4, id=deepseek-v4-flash
- Empty either side → error
- Provider name with slash → error
- Multiple slashes → split on first only
- Future M5: `@<sub_provider>` suffix is a separate concern, not in this bead

## Mandatory test matrix (per ADR-012)

ALL 10 tests in `multiprocess_test.go` MUST pass:

1. concurrent claim → exactly one ClaimedByMe
2. reader during refresh → atomic rename, no torn read
3. crash during refresh (kill -9 child) → next process claims via dead-PID
4. refresh timeout → marker deadline expires, claim
5. force-refresh waits + returns fresh
6. concurrent normal+force → singleflight composition
7. atomic rename: 100 readers + 1 writer × 100 versions, no torn read
8. cache prune doesn't race active sources
9. stale-while-revalidate: stale returns immediately + bg refresh fires
10. PID reuse safety: deadline check is the fallback

Use `TestHelperProcess` pattern (Go stdlib pattern; spawn `os.Args[0]
-test.run=TestHelperProcess --` with custom args).

## Out of scope

- M2 (snapshot assembler) — uses this cache.
- M3 (runtime signals) — uses this cache.
- M4 (CLI) — top-level surface.
- Any actual provider/harness fetch logic — Refresher is injected.

## Depends on

- ADR-012 (M0) accepted before this bead executes (uses its design
  contract).
notes: Review 2026-05-12: AC4 is not actually covered as written; internal/discoverycache/cache_test.go has a no-contention max-latency loop, not p99 read latency under contention. Add contention coverage matching ADR-012.
title: Cache infrastructure: per-source disk cache + ParseModelRef + 10-test robustness matrix

description:
PROBLEM
Background discovery refresh and runtime liveness paths can still violate the routing hot-path contract: refresh fetchers may ignore the cache refresh deadline or inherit a caller context that is canceled immediately after ResolveRoute returns, and local runtime signal checks can build /v1/v1/models for base URLs that already include /v1.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/discoverycache/cache.go calls refreshers with context.Background() and does not apply Source.RefreshDeadline to the actual IO context.
- internal/modelregistry/discovery.go and internal/modelsnapshot/discovery.go prefer the caller context inside background refreshers, so a cache-first ResolveRoute can schedule a refresh that dies with the request context instead of refreshing asynchronously.
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go appends /v1/models directly to BaseURL, producing /v1/v1/models for normal OpenAI-compatible config.

PROPOSED FIX
Apply the Source.RefreshDeadline in discoverycache, make background discovery refresh use the cache refresh context while synchronous refresh still honors caller cancellation plus the refresh deadline, and normalize local runtime signal base URLs before appending /v1/models.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/discoverycache/cache.go and tests
- internal/modelregistry/discovery.go and tests
- internal/modelsnapshot/discovery.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go and tests

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change route scoring or provider selection.
- Do not change explicit HealthCheck/ListModels force-refresh semantics.
- Do not modify provider catalog contents.
title: discovery: bound async refresh contexts and normalize local runtime probes

Verified by `grep -c "func matrixErrorIsTransient" cmd/bench/matrix.go` == 1 and `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestMatrixErrorIsTransient -count=1`.
2. TestMatrixErrorIsTransient in cmd/bench/matrix_test.go passes; it covers connection refused, no route to host, i/o timeout, context deadline exceeded, connection reset, broken pipe, server closed, HTTP 502/503/504, unexpected EOF, JSON invalid character, raw EOF as transient; HTTP 429, HTTP 4xx, "context canceled", and plain harness_crash text as non-transient.

8. The retry wrapper is wired into both halt and parallel branches of the per-cell loop; verified by `grep -c "runTupleWithRetry" cmd/bench/matrix.go` >= 2.

description:
The matrix runner aborts a lane after 5 consecutive identical failures (cmd/bench/matrix.go:36 matrixConsecutiveFailureLimit). Today's sindri-lucebox sweep died this way: the lucebox endpoint stopped serving mid-sweep, every cell hit the same connection error, the abort fingerprint (sha256 of Error[:256] + FinalStatus) collapsed to the same hash across 5 cells, and the lane was killed even though the underlying server was momentarily down (and recoverable).

Fix: classify connection-class / HTTP 5xx / EOF / parse errors as transient. Transient errors (a) do not count toward the consecutive-failure halt counter, (b) trigger an indefinite per-cell retry loop with exponential backoff capped at 60s, interruptible via parent context (ctrl-C / SIGTERM). HTTP 429 is intentionally NOT classified as transient — rate-limits are persistent enough that an indefinite retry can stall the sweep, surface them as failures so they're visible and tunable at the resource-group / sampling level. New CLI flags --no-cell-retry (default off) and --cell-retry-backoff-max (default 60s).
title: bench/matrix: retry transient errors instead of aborting the lane

description:
Add concurrency-group locking, --jobs background pool, signal handling, and the concurrent-cells state file to scripts/benchmark/benchmark.

FLAGS ADDED: [--jobs N]

CONCURRENCY:
  - flock per concurrency-group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock.
  - setsid for each cell process group.
  - Background-job pool capped by --jobs.

SIGNAL HANDLING:
  - Trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells.
  - SIGTERM each in-flight cell's process group AND `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner container.
  - Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
  - Write final_status=interrupted to in-flight cells (process_outcome=killed, no metrics).
  - Exit non-zero.

IN-FLIGHT STATE:
  - ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json.
  - Runner appends its cell at start under flock, reads current count, writes into cell, removes at close.
  - Prune stale entries (PID dead) at next read.

DEPENDS ON A4 (execution loop must exist to interrupt).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. SIGTERM to a mid-sweep ./benchmark: in-flight cells marked final_status=interrupted, harbor-runner containers stopped via `docker stop`, runner exits non-zero. Verify: ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary & sleep 5; kill -TERM $! ; wait; jq .final_status bench/results/.../report.json prints "interrupted".
  2. Two concurrent ./benchmark invocations with the same concurrency-group serialize via flock; no overlapping cells.
  3. in-flight.json reflects the current cell count; stale PID entries pruned on next read.

description:
Update routing score semantics so policy decisions use comparable effective cost instead of treating subscription-backed models as equally included.

Desired scoring behavior:
- cheap selects the lowest effective-cost model with enough expected capability for the request. It should choose nano/mini/local over GPT-5.5 when they are sufficient and dispatchable.
- default selects the best routine tradeoff and should avoid maximum-quality frontier subscription unless lower-cost routine candidates are not sufficient or are materially worse after reliability/health/context scoring.
- smart can let quality/reliability dominate cost.
- Pay-per-token providers are hard-gated only when actual_cash_spend=true and metered opt-in is false.
- Subscription candidates use PAYG-equivalent effective cost for scoring while actual_cash_spend=false.
- Avoid frontier-specific if/else exclusion gates; the outcome should fall out of effective cost, power/task fit, reliability, latency, utilization, and risk scoring.

In scope: internal/routing scorer, service projection of snapshot effective_cost into routing candidates, focused score tests.

Out of scope: snapshot schema fields if prior bead has not landed; CLI display.
title: Score cheapest sufficient routes using effective cost

acceptance:
1. `go test ./internal/discoverycache -run 'Test.*Failed.*Refresh.*Retry|Test.*MaybeRefreshSync' -count=1` passes and proves an immediate second sync call after a failed refresh does not retry while the marker is active.
2. The same test proves a later sync call after marker staleness reclaims the marker and invokes the refresher again, succeeding and clearing `RefreshState.Failed`.
3. `go test ./internal/modelregistry -run TestAssembleRefreshFailureRecordsRefreshFailedAndDoesNotRetryImmediately -count=1` still passes.
4. `go test ./internal/discoverycache ./internal/modelregistry ./internal/modelsnapshot -count=1` passes.
description:
Problem
The current failed-refresh suppression can poison a discovery source forever. `internal/discoverycache.Cache.MaybeRefreshSync` returns nil whenever `RefreshState.Failed` is true. Failed refresh markers are intentionally retained with `LastError` so callers can observe `refresh_failed` and avoid immediate retry storms, but ADR-012 requires stale/orphan markers to be reclaimed by a later caller. Once the marker's deadline plus staleness threshold has elapsed, a sync refresh should claim the source and retry.

Spec context
- ADR-012 says a marker is stale when `now > deadline + staleness_threshold` or the owner process is dead, and stale markers are removed during claim before a new marker is written.
- FEAT-004 / CONTRACT-003 require route-time freshness to use the lock-coordinated refresh path, not permanently trust stale failed state.

In-scope files
- internal/discoverycache/singleflight.go
- internal/discoverycache/cache.go / marker helpers only if a small helper is needed to expose active-vs-stale marker state
- internal/discoverycache/*_test.go
- internal/modelregistry/refresh_coordinator_test.go only if an integration-level assertion is useful

Out of scope
- Changing snapshot scoring or routing gates
- Changing `fiz models` UX
- Sleeping through the production HTTP discovery deadline in tests

Implementation guidance
Suppress immediate retries only while the failed marker is still active. Once the marker is stale under ADR-012 rules, allow `Refresh` to reclaim and retry. Prefer a focused discoverycache unit test with a tiny RefreshDeadline over a slow modelregistry HTTP test.
notes: Worker codex/gpt-5.5 produced a passing implementation in side worktree; DDx auto-land hit land_conflict_unresolvable, so the patch was integrated manually in the main tree. Verified locally: go test ./internal/discoverycache -run 'Test.*Failed.*Refresh.*Retry|Test.*MaybeRefreshSync' -count=1; go test ./internal/modelregistry -run TestAssembleRefreshFailureRecordsRefreshFailedAndDoesNotRetryImmediately -count=1; go test ./internal/discoverycache ./internal/modelregistry ./internal/modelsnapshot -count=1; go test ./... all pass. Pending close after commit.
title: discoverycache: retry failed refresh after marker staleness

description:
PROBLEM
Under policy=default, the claude subscription harness contributes exactly one candidate (its DefaultModel=opus-4.7) to routing_decision.candidates, even though SupportedModels lists sonnet-4.6/haiku siblings on the same auth. Sibling tiers are silently absent — not present-and-excluded — so the cost-aware scorer never sees them.

ROOT CAUSE (pointers; confirm during fix)
- service_routing.go:1172-1189 — the candidate add loop iterates h.SupportedModels and calls add(modelID, true, status), but downstream the emitted candidate list contains only opus-4.7 for claude. Three candidate causes to disambiguate while reading: (a) aggregation/dedup keyed by {harness,provider} keeping DefaultModel as representative, (b) eligibility map keyed by modelID collapsing entries by family before emit, (c) h.SupportedModels populated empty at the entries construction site (distinct from the metadata_billing.go:71 path).
- service_models.go:91-101 — subprocessHarnessModelIDs("claude", cfg) is verified to return the full ["sonnet","sonnet-4.6","opus","opus-4.7","claude-sonnet-4-6"] set, so the SupportedModels source is not the gap.

PROPOSED FIX (direction)
For each subprocess harness entry, emit one candidate per modelID in SupportedModels keyed on (harness, modelID) so sibling tiers under one auth survive into the candidate pool. Each per-tier candidate must read power/cost from internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml for that tier and route to the same harness (not fall back to fiz/openrouter).

NON-SCOPE
- Scoring weight changes / asserting sonnet beats opus on a specific prompt (covered by sibling child cost-aware-tier-selection).
- --model pin routing destination (covered by sibling child model-pin-subscription-route).
- filter_reason emission for excluded tiers (covered by sibling child explicit-filter-reason).
- openrouter 401 auth (separate Fizeau config issue).
- DDx-side changes; DDx passes policy=default verbatim per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010.
- Catalog metadata additions (sonnet-4.6 metadata already complete in internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml).

CROSS-REF
https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6
title: auto-route: emit one candidate per supported tier for multi-tier subscription harnesses

acceptance:
1. `go test ./internal/sdk/openaicompat/...` passes including the five
   table cases above.
2. `go test ./internal/core/...` passes; the TokenUsage struct round-trips
   ReasoningTokensApprox through JSON.
3. `go test ./cmd/bench/...` passes; report.json carries
   reasoning_tokens_approx alongside reasoning_tokens.
4. Manual smoke: send a chat completion to ds4 vidar:1236 with
   `reasoning_effort: high`, parse the response with the updated extractor,
   verify the resulting TokenUsage has Reasoning > 0 and
   ReasoningTokensApprox == true.
5. `go vet ./...` passes.
description:
Today the reasoning-token extractor in `internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go`
(`extractReasoningTokens`) reads only from
`usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens` in the response body. ds4
and some other providers do thinking but do NOT populate that field — they
return the thinking inline as `message.reasoning_content` with no separate
token-count accounting.

Verified 2026-05-11 against ds4 (vidar:1236): a simple "What is 17 times
24?" prompt returned `message.reasoning_content` of 92 chars but
`usage.completion_tokens_details` was absent entirely. Result: report.json
records reasoning_tokens=0 for every ds4 cell even when thinking ran for
thousands of tokens.

Per ADR-010 Amendment §8, the resolution chain must be:
1. If usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens is present, use it.
2. Else if message.reasoning_content is non-empty, derive an approximation
   from char-count / 4. Annotate with reasoning_tokens_approx=true.
3. Else reasoning_tokens=0 (honest zero, not silent truncation).

Implementation:

1. Extend `extractReasoningTokens` in
   `internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go` to take both the raw usage JSON
   AND the raw message JSON (or refactor to read message.reasoning_content
   from the parsed response if it's already structured).
2. Apply the char-count fallback when usage path is absent AND
   message.reasoning_content is non-empty.
3. Add a new boolean field `ReasoningTokensApprox` to
   `internal/core/agent.go` `TokenUsage` struct. Default false.
4. Wire ReasoningTokensApprox through into the matrix runner's report.json
   (`cmd/bench/matrix.go` — alongside the existing ReasoningTokens
   plumbing).
5. The char-count/4 estimator is a v1 placeholder; a future bead can
   replace with a real tokenizer (tiktoken-go or model-specific) if
   needed. Document the v1 method in the field's GoDoc.

Test cases (table-driven in
`internal/sdk/openaicompat/client_test.go`):
- usage path present, reasoning_content also present → use usage (more
  authoritative; report approx=false).
- usage path absent, reasoning_content present (92 chars) → derive 23
  tokens (92/4). approx=true.
- both absent → 0, approx=false.
- usage path = 0 (explicit zero), reasoning_content present → still use
  usage's zero (caller signaled no reasoning); approx=false. NOTE: this
  is a judgment call; document it.
- Streaming path: aggregate reasoning_content across deltas, char-count
  the aggregated string.

In-scope files:
- internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go
- internal/sdk/openaicompat/client_test.go (new tests)
- internal/core/agent.go (add ReasoningTokensApprox field to TokenUsage)
- cmd/bench/matrix.go (extend report.json to carry the approx flag)

Out-of-scope:
- Real tokenizer integration (tiktoken-go etc.) — v1 uses chars/4. Filed
  as a follow-up after we have measurements on how much approx error
  matters in practice.
- Streaming-specific token-counting optimizations.
title: Derive reasoning_tokens from reasoning_content when usage.completion_tokens_details is absent

Final verification closes the epic when deterministic control-plane and telemetry checks pass:
- Focused reasoning/catalog tests prove `reasoning: low` on the OpenRouter Qwen3.6 surface selects catalog `tokens` wire and emits the PortableBudgets low cap (`reasoning.max_tokens=2048`).
- Verification reports carry `reasoning_tokens > 0`, an accurate `reasoning_tokens_approx` flag, and recorded `reasoning_intent`/`reasoning_emitted` data where the lane substitutes or approximates intent.

description:
EPIC for the work described in ADR-010 (and its 2026-05-11 amendment).

## End goal

A benchmark caller writes `reasoning: <intent>` once in a profile. Across
every lane in the matrix, that intent is honored to the maximum extent
the upstream supports. The actual wire emitted, the upstream's published
capabilities, and the observed reasoning depth are all recorded per cell
in report.json. Cross-lane comparison happens at analysis time on
truthful data, not by forcing identical wire-level behavior across
providers that genuinely differ.

## Architectural shape

Wire form is determined by a layered source-of-truth chain at provider
construction time:

  L1: Live introspection. ds4 /props exposes alias maps, supported
      params, ctx-window constraints. llama-server /props exposes
      chat-template details and server-side reasoning_format. Read once
      at provider construction (8-15ms LAN, 316ms cloud) and merged into
      ProtocolCapabilities.
  L2: Catalog `reasoning_wire` field. Used when L1 is silent. Carries the
      effort/tokens choice for opaque providers (OR-Qwen3).
  L3: Static ThinkingFormat per provider type. Final default for
      unaudited models.

A new `ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort` wire variant handles ds4's flat
top-level `reasoning_effort` shape; existing `ThinkingWireFormatQwen`
is fixed to use the `chat_template_kwargs` envelope per the 2026-05-11
sindri probe (top-level enable_thinking is silently dropped by
llama-server).

Telemetry: when the upstream omits OpenAI-shape
`usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens` but populates
`message.reasoning_content`, fizeau derives an approximation via
char-count / 4 and flags `reasoning_tokens_approx=true`. Cells never
silently zero reasoning_tokens when thinking occurred.

Capability honesty: when a lane physically cannot honor caller intent
(ds4 has no token budget; sindri's budget hint doesn't bind; ds4's
low/medium/xhigh aliases all collapse to high), fizeau sends the closest
representable wire form and records both intent and emission in
report.json. Cross-lane analysis is honest at read time, not forced at
write time.

## Child beads

- **fizeau-1567453f** (schema) — extend reasoning_wire enum with `effort`
  and `tokens` values. Worker actively executing.
- **fizeau-e80c156e** (introspection adapters) — read /props at
  provider construction for ds4 and llama-server; merge into
  ProtocolCapabilities. THIS IS THE HEART OF THE ARCHITECTURE.
- **fizeau-d72b32a0** (probe tool) — `cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning`.
  Rescoped to snapshot + verification, not behavioral discovery.
- **fizeau-144bd2f9** (translator) — consult L1/L2/L3 chain; fix
  ThinkingWireFormatQwen to use chat_template_kwargs envelope; add
  ThinkingWireFormatOpenAIEffort for ds4.
- **fizeau-8f62bcbb** (token extraction) — derive reasoning_tokens from
  reasoning_content when usage.completion_tokens_details is absent.
- **fizeau-b83502c0** (populate + integration) — run the probe,
  populate the catalog with any L2 values still needed (OR-Qwen3 wire
  form, ds4 catalog entry), fix the surface-id lookup mismatch, revert
  benchmark profiles to named-tier intent.

## Final verification (closes the epic)

A fresh sweep at `reasoning: low` against all four lanes shows
report.json carrying:
- reasoning_tokens > 0 (populated either directly or via the
  reasoning_content fallback)
- reasoning_tokens_approx flagging the source accurately
- reasoning_intent and reasoning_emitted reflecting any
  upstream-imposed substitution
- The captured upstream wire matches what L1/L2/L3 selection
  predicted for that (provider, model) pair

## Out of scope

- Catalog refresh tool (upstream /models diffing) — partially absorbed
  by L1 introspection for ds4/llama-server; deferred for OR.
- CI staleness gate on `last_probed_at`.
- Model-card URL/citation fields in catalog entries.
- Startup behavioral probing — kept rejected. Construction-time /props
  reads are fine (measured cheap); request-time dummy completions are
  not.
- vLLM introspection adapter — defer until we re-add a vLLM lane.
- Real tokenizer for reasoning_content length (use chars/4 in v1).

See ADR-010 (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-010-reasoning-wire-form-from-
catalog.md) including the 2026-05-11 Amendment for the full rationale,
consequences, evidence (2026-05-11 ds4 + sindri probe), and end-goal
articulation.
notes: Acceptance corrected after ADR-010 clarification: PortableBudgets values are emitted request caps/control signals, not deterministic predictions of observed reasoning token spend. Current verification target is emitted wire + truthful telemetry, not forcing `reasoning_tokens ~= 2048`.
title: Catalog-driven reasoning wire form (effort vs tokens)

description:

After the execution/transcript slice, the next large root-package implementation bucket is routing/status/quota state. ADR-008 names `internal/routehealth`, `internal/quota`, and `internal/routingquality` as intended implementation homes, but root files such as `service_routing.go`, `service_route_attempts.go`, `service_route_leases.go`, `service_routestatus.go`, `service_status.go`, `service_subscription_quota.go`, `service_refresh_scheduler.go`, `service_snapshot*.go`, `provider_burn_rate.go`, `provider_quota_state.go`, and `service_routing_quality.go` still own a large amount of mechanics.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` says root stays the public facade and concrete mechanics belong in internal packages. `service.go:18-39` defines the stable public service boundary, including `ResolveRoute`, `RecordRouteAttempt`, and `RouteStatus`. `website/content/docs/routing/_index.md:30`, `website/content/docs/routing/_index.md:65`, and `website/content/docs/observability/_index.md:59` still point readers at root implementation files for routing/quota/status internals.

PROPOSED FIX
Extract routing health, quota state, scheduler/sticky-lease, status, snapshot, and routing-quality mechanics behind internal packages while preserving CONTRACT-003 public route/status/request/decision vocabulary and root constructor methods.

NON-SCOPE

description:
PROBLEM: The new bash runner needs production lifecycle controls for concurrent cells, process-group cleanup, Harbor container cleanup, shared in-flight state, and budget caps before it can replace the Go execution path. Governing artifact: plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Dependency ID: fizeau-163efede. Sibling dependency: expects A2 runner core to exist, but this child must add isolated fake-task tests so its behavior is verifiable without spending real model budget.
ROOT CAUSE: cmd/bench/matrix.go:1 centralizes run lifecycle in the old Go runner, so the new scripts/benchmark/benchmark entry point has no bash-owned flock, setsid, signal, in-flight, or budget implementation.
PROPOSED FIX: Extend scripts/benchmark/benchmark with background-job pooling capped by --jobs. Read concurrency-group YAML through yq -o=json and acquire per-group flock locks at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock. Launch each cell under setsid and track process groups. Maintain ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json under flock: prune dead PIDs on read, append the cell at start, record current in-flight count into the cell, and remove on close. Add SIGINT/SIGTERM traps that stop accepting new cells, send SIGTERM to each in-flight cell process group, docker stop each in-flight harbor-runner container, wait 30 seconds, send SIGKILL if needed, write final_status=interrupted with process_outcome=killed and no metrics to in-flight cells, and exit non-zero. Add budget accounting: compute cost_usd_at_run_time per cell from profile.pricing and token streams at close, accumulate into <out>/budget.json, halt before the next cell when --max-cost-usd would be exceeded, and write budget_halted placeholder report.json files with process_outcome=setup_failed and a note field for skipped cells.
NON-SCOPE: cell-report.schema.json (PR B); final tasks_from / task_executor bench-set schema (PR C); Node collector (PR D); Node importers (PR E); deleting cmd/bench (PR F); docs beyond required local command help and existing redirect references (PR G). Do not change adapter contracts, Harbor image build logic, planning-mode Go behavior, parity fixtures, or Go runner deactivation in this child.
ROLLBACK: If interrupted, cell-state.json sentinels and in-flight.json stale entries are acceptable recovery anchors; the next runner read must prune dead PIDs.

2. `go test . -run 'TestRouter_DeadLocalProvider_FallsThroughToSubscription|TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast|TestResolveRoute_KnownFailedLocalProbeIsEvidence|TestResolveRoute_DoesNotProbeFreshHealthOnEveryRun' -count=1` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Route-time aliveness evidence for providers with multiple configured endpoints can be treated as provider-wide evidence. A failed probe for one local endpoint can therefore poison the whole provider, causing routing to reject healthy sibling endpoints or report the wrong endpoint as unreachable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `service_aliveness.go:57-86` owns aliveness endpoint enumeration and must preserve the configured endpoint name alongside the provider and base URL; provider/baseURL-only probe keys are too coarse for multi-endpoint providers.
- `service_aliveness.go:236-267` must convert cached failed probe evidence into route-health keys for both the base provider and endpoint-qualified provider refs when applicable.
- `service_routing.go:754-758` feeds proactive probe failures into routing inputs, so provider-level `UnreachableProviders` data can incorrectly hard-gate all endpoint candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
Record startup, route-time, and loop aliveness probes with the configured endpoint key. Build `ProbeUnreachable` from endpoint-aware probe records and include endpoint-qualified provider refs such as `local@desk-a`, while preserving provider-level fallback behavior for legacy records. Keep the route hot path nonblocking.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- service_aliveness.go
- service_aliveness_test.go
- service_routing.go

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change route scoring weights.
- Do not change dispatch retry or provider-attempt persistence.
- Do not change CLI request routing behavior; that is tracked separately.
title: routing: scope aliveness probe health to provider endpoints

description:
PROBLEM
  internal/core/agent.go and internal/core/loop.go thread RequestedModelRef and ResolvedModelRef through the core agent execution path; telemetry/telemetry.go declares KeyRequestedModelRef (and possibly KeyResolvedModelRef) constants; internal/session/event.go schema includes RequestedModelRef and ResolvedModelRef JSON fields. With ModelRef removed from the public surface (Phase 4), these become dead weight and the session-log schema changes shape.

ROOT CAUSE
  Core agent threading was added when ModelRef was the canonical routing input. Telemetry and session-log fields followed. None of these have a future once ModelRef is gone.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - All RequestedModelRef and ResolvedModelRef fields removed from internal/core types and session-log schema.
  - Telemetry keys for model_ref deleted from telemetry/telemetry.go.
  - Session-log JSON shape change: pre-v0.11 logs containing requested_model_ref/resolved_model_ref keys decode silently (Go's JSON unmarshaler ignores unknown keys); replay continues to work; new logs do not emit those keys.
  - Add a regression test that pre-v0.11 fixture session logs round-trip through fiz replay without error.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - internal/core/agent.go:333-340 Request struct (RequestedModelRef, ResolvedModelRef fields)
  - internal/core/agent.go:432-440 Result struct (RequestedModelRef, ResolvedModelRef fields)
  - internal/core/loop.go:34-35 event-attr decls
  - internal/core/loop.go:105-106 event emission with model_ref keys
  - internal/core/loop.go:887-888 result struct field reads
  - internal/core/loop.go:935 appendStringAttr telemetry.KeyRequestedModelRef
  - internal/session/event.go:24-25,98-99 RequestedModelRef, ResolvedModelRef JSON fields
  - telemetry/telemetry.go:38 KeyRequestedModelRef constant (verify if KeyResolvedModelRef also exists)

PROPOSED FIX
  1. internal/core/agent.go: drop RequestedModelRef and ResolvedModelRef from Request and Result structs.
  2. internal/core/loop.go: drop the four event-attr lines (34-35, 105-106, 887-888) and the appendStringAttr line (935).
  3. internal/session/event.go: drop RequestedModelRef and ResolvedModelRef JSON fields from session event types.
  4. telemetry/telemetry.go: drop KeyRequestedModelRef constant. Verify and drop KeyResolvedModelRef if present.
  5. internal/core/loop_test.go and telemetry_contract_test.go: drop assertions about requested_model_ref / resolved_model_ref event attrs.
  6. New test internal/session/event_replay_test.go::TestReplaysPreV011SessionLog: load a fixture file containing requested_model_ref/resolved_model_ref keys (pre-v0.11 shape) and assert it round-trips through the replay path without error and without re-emitting those keys.

NON-SCOPE
  - Public service surface (service_session_log.go field assignments) — Phase 4.
  - CLI flag --model-ref — Phase 6.

PARENT + DEPS
  Depends on Phase 4.
notes: Implemented in b3670312: removed RequestedModelRef/ResolvedModelRef from internal/core Request/Result, core session start/end payloads, root span attrs, internal/session start/end schemas, and telemetry KeyRequestedModelRef. Added pre-v0.11 replay fixture/test and TestTelemetryNoModelRefAttrs. Updated CLI/cmd tests that read the slimmer session schema. Verified: grep -Rn 'RequestedModelRef|ResolvedModelRef' internal/core telemetry internal/session --include='*.go' returned no matches; grep -n 'model_ref|model-ref' telemetry/telemetry.go returned no matches; go test ./internal/core/... ./internal/session/... ./telemetry/... -count=1 passed; go test ./agentcli -run TestCLI_Run_ModelByModelName|TestCLI_Run_ModelByBackendPool -count=1 passed; go test ./cmd/fiz -count=1 passed; go test ./... -count=1 passed; lefthook run pre-commit --all-files passed.
