description:
Add the per-cell execution loop, resume default, harbor invocation glue, report.json composition, sweep.json metadata capture, and per-cell bounded retry to scripts/benchmark/benchmark. Concurrency/signal handling lives in A5; budget enforcement lives in A6.

FLAGS ADDED in this bead: [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N]

RESUME DEFAULT:
  - If cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
  - --force-rerun ignores.
  - --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR orphan cell-state.json.

PER-CELL LOOP:
  - Write cell-state.json sentinel.
  - Invoke harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json.
  - Compose task-spec.
  - Invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes cell_dir/result.json).
  - Compose report.json (embed profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/).
  - Delete cell-state.json on terminal close.

SWEEP.JSON:
  - Capture task_executor_version + harbor_runner_image_digest once per ./benchmark invocation into <out>/sweep.json; runner copies into each cell.

PER-CELL RETRY:
  - Bounded exponential backoff for transient errors (connection refused, 5xx, EOF parse).
  - Write attempt_of: link in the new cell; back-write superseded_by: into the prior cell (the one allowed mutation of a closed cell).

DEPENDS ON A3 (skeleton + matrix expansion), A1, A2.

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary produces cells under bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/cells/, each with report.json containing embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/.
  2. <out>/sweep.json contains task_executor_version and harbor_runner_image_digest; each cell's report.json references them.
  3. Re-running without --force-rerun skips cells with terminal report.json.
  4. --retry-invalid reruns cells with invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json; new cell has attempt_of link, prior cell has superseded_by back-link.
  5. Inducing a transient 5xx during a cell triggers bounded exponential backoff and eventual success (or terminal failure after retry cap).
title: bench-pr-A4: bash runner execution loop + harbor invocation + report.json + sweep.json + per-cell retry

description:
Problem
Fixing startup-skipped providers to remain unknown is necessary, but route correctness must not depend on a background worker eventually probing them. FEAT-004 and CONTRACT-003 define routing as `route(client_inputs, fiz_models_snapshot)` with `ResolveRoute`/`Execute` refreshing routing-relevant stale fields before scoring. If a local provider has no fresh health/discovery evidence because startup skipped it, automatic routing must not blindly dispatch to it as if it were healthy. It must either obtain fresh-enough evidence through the coordinated refresh/probe path or record a refresh failure/timeout in route evidence before using stale data.

Spec context
- FEAT-004 AC-08: ResolveRoute and Execute refresh stale routing-relevant snapshot fields through the lock-coordinated refresh path before scoring.
- CONTRACT-003: Fizeau has no required daemon; long-running DDx heartbeat is an optimization, not a correctness dependency.
- The May 12 auto-routing plan amendment says autorouting refreshes stale routing-relevant snapshot fields synchronously before scoring while `fiz models` remains quick by default.

In-scope files
- service_routing.go / route-time input assembly
- service_aliveness.go only for small helpers shared with route-time freshness
- service_routing_test.go and/or service_aliveness_test.go
- internal/modelsnapshot tests only if needed for evidence propagation

Out of scope
- Reintroducing request-time probe-on-every-run for already-fresh providers
- Changing score weights
- Changing `fiz models` default stale-while-revalidate behavior

Implementation guidance
Add a route-time freshness check for local provider health/discovery evidence that covers the `unknown because never probed/skipped` state. The check should be bounded and use the existing coordinated refresh/probe mechanics. Successful evidence should allow the candidate; failed/timed-out evidence should become typed candidate evidence (`endpoint_unreachable`, `unhealthy`, or refresh_failed as appropriate). Explicit provider pins remain the operator escape hatch subject to existing policy requirements.
notes: Dependent route-time freshness bead remains open and blocked on closing fizeau-8e1661b5 and fizeau-828b0a7c. Do not run the general picker for this incident: ddx work plan currently selects unrelated harness refactor beads first. Use targeted ddx try/work after the lower-level fixes are committed/closed.
title: routing: refresh unknown local health before automatic dispatch

3. Add TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast proving all configured providers unreachable returns no_viable_provider, not provider_connectivity, within the bounded test timeout.
4. Add or preserve coverage proving the failed provider is not retried again within the same routing decision.
5. cd . && go test ./internal/routing/... ./... -run "TestRouter_DeadLocalProvider_FallsThroughToSubscription|TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast|TestLocalFirstDefaultCooldownFallback|TestServiceStartup_.*ProbeUnreachable" -count=1 passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The routing reliability epic still lacks service-level proof that a dead local provider falls through to a healthy subscription/cloud route, and that a request fails fast only when every configured provider is unreachable. The current coverage mostly simulates harness cooldown in the routing engine, which does not prove the full Fizeau service path handles provider probe failures and no-viable-provider errors correctly.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
service_routing.go:740-754 feeds providerProbe.UnreachableProviders into routing.Inputs.ProbeUnreachable, but there is no service-level TestRouter_DeadLocalProvider_FallsThroughToSubscription or TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast. internal/routing/preference_test.go:297-306 only covers an in-memory cooldown case, not a first-dial/probe failure observed by the service. The reliability epic fizeau-361f4f4b therefore still has unimplemented AC for dead-provider fallback and all-dead fail-fast behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
Add integration-style tests around ResolveRoute/service routing fixtures that seed or simulate unreachable local providers, verify subscription/cloud fallback in one routing decision, and verify all-dead provider sets return no_viable_provider within the bounded timeout. Fix routing/probe filtering only where those tests expose a real failure.

NON-SCOPE
Do not tune dial timeout constants unless required by the failing test. Do not alter cross-process persistence; fizeau-7cd66e6b already closed that scope. Do not introduce a new provider-health store or duplicate route-health logic.
title: routing: add dead-provider fallback and all-dead fail-fast coverage

PROBLEM
The repo root contains the service package implementation and tests, so navigation, gopls indexing, and root-level package cohesion all degrade as the package keeps growing.

ROOT CAUSE

description:
PROBLEM
Harbor and its harbor_adapters import from repo Python; the current runner adds repo root to PYTHONPATH on the host (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105). To eliminate host Python, Harbor must run inside a Docker image with its adapters baked in, called by the bash runner.

ROOT CAUSE
Host-side PYTHONPATH injection couples Harbor execution to a host Python environment matching the repo's deps.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add scripts/benchmark/harbor-runner/Dockerfile that installs Harbor + scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/ + their pinned Python deps. Tag the image fizeau-harbor-runner:<short-sha or 'dev'>.
- Add scripts/benchmark/harbor-runner/build.sh that builds the image deterministically.
- Add scripts/benchmark/task-executors/harbor shell script that reads task-spec.json on stdin, runs `docker run --rm --name fizeau-harbor-<cell-id> ... fizeau-harbor-runner ...` with mounts for the cell directory and any required artifact paths, and writes result.json to the cell directory.
- Pass through env needed by Harbor; never mount host site-packages.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not wire the bash runner's per-cell loop to call this task-executor — that's sibling A.4.
- Do not delete scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/*.py from repo root (still imported by Go runner until removal PR).
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. `scripts/benchmark/harbor-runner/build.sh` builds fizeau-harbor-runner image without contacting host Python.
2. `scripts/benchmark/task-executors/harbor` invoked with a fixture task-spec.json on stdin produces a well-formed result.json; covered by TestHarborTaskExecutorSmoke (Go test, gated on `docker` availability via t.Skip otherwise).
3. TestHarborRunnerImageNoHostPython asserts the Dockerfile does not COPY or bind-mount host Python site-packages and that build.sh uses only files under scripts/benchmark/harbor-runner/ and scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: bench-pr-A.3: fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image + container invocation wrapper

acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel` proving `resolveExecuteRoute(ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"codex", Policy:"default", Prompt:"x"})` returns a Codex-supported model, not `qwen3.5-27b` or any non-Codex model.
2. Add `TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape` proving `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` are copied from `ServiceExecuteRequest` into the `RouteRequest` path and affect routing candidates.
3. Add `TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch` proving a subprocess harness decision with an unsupported model returns a routing/validation error before the runner is invoked.
4. `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` succeeds or fails with a pre-dispatch no-viable-route error; it must not invoke Codex with `qwen3.5-27b`.
5. `go test ./internal/routing/... -run "HarnessPolicy|PromptShape|UnsupportedEngineDecision" -count=1` passes.
6. `go test . -run "TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel|TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape|TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` routes to an unsupported model and fails before useful DDx work can start: `model "qwen3.5-27b" is not supported by harness "codex"; supported models: gpt, gpt-5, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini`. This blocks DDx `ddx work --watch --harness codex --profile default` from using the standard policy band without an explicit model pin.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`service_execute.go:198-211` intentionally sends empty-model subprocess harness requests with Policy/MinPower through the routing engine, but the engine path is not enforcing the selected model against the subprocess harness before dispatch. `service_routing.go:1732-1745` also drops `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` when constructing the `RouteRequest`, so Execute and ResolveRoute can disagree on prompt/tool capability gates. Finally, `internal/routing/gating.go:128-135` treats any explicit Harness as a full bypass of catalog power eligibility, even when Policy/MinPower are the only model-selection input and the engine is supposed to choose within that harness's eligible models.

PROPOSED FIX
Make harness+policy empty-model Execute routing produce only harness-supported concrete models before subprocess dispatch. Preserve explicit model pins and provider-routed harness semantics, but when `Harness` is set and `Model` is empty, apply policy/min-power selection within that harness's supported model set. Forward `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` from `ServiceExecuteRequest` into the `RouteRequest` used by `resolveExecuteRouteWithEngine`. Add pre-dispatch validation so any engine decision for a subprocess harness fails with a typed routing error before invoking the runner if the resolved model is outside that harness allow-list.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx routing policy. Do not add legacy `standard` policy aliases. Do not remove the existing explicit `--model gpt-5.4` workaround from operators; this bead is only the Fizeau routing fix.
title: routing: constrain harness policy auto-resolution to supported models

description:
## Problem

Projects that include .ddx/executions/ in their .gitignore (including fizeau and
any repo using the standard DDx .gitignore template) silently fail to land any bead.
The failure is invisible: the implementation is correct, tests pass, but agent.Land()
preserves instead of landing on every attempt.

## Root Cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701 — RealLandingGitOps.StageDir:

  args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
  out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()

git add without --force silently skips gitignored paths (exit 0, no output, no error).
When .ddx/executions/ is gitignored, StageDir stages nothing. CommitStaged sees an
empty index and returns sha="". landEvidence calls evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles which
also returns false (gitignored files not tracked), so it returns error:
"no staged evidence files under .ddx/executions/...". preserveAfterEvidenceFailure
fires, rolls back master, and creates a preserve ref.

Compounding issue: ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps ALL non-pre-merge-check
preserve reasons (including "evidence commit failed") to reasonForStatus="merge
conflict", which ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus returns as "land_conflict" (escalatable).
This causes runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts to burn through the entire model escalation
ladder (min_power 5→7→8→9) before exhausting.

Confirmed in fizeau-bd5193ca: all 4 attempts preserved by evidence commit failure.
Investigation: .ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/fizeau-bd5193ca-investigation.md

## Proposed Fix

Part 1 (fix): In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701, StageDir, change:
  args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, ...)
to:
  args := append([]string{"add", "--force", "--", relPath}, ...)

Evidence files are DDx infrastructure, not user source. They must be committed even
when the project's .gitignore covers .ddx/executions/.

Part 2 (defensive): In execute_bead_status.go, add "evidence commit failed:" as a
non-escalatable prefix in isPreservedNeedsReviewReason so infrastructure failures do
not burn the escalation ladder. Fixing Part 1 eliminates the failure path; Part 2
prevents wasted escalation if any other evidence-commit edge case arises.
execute-loop-last-detail: bead misfiled — target source files do not exist in this repository ## Summary This bead (fizeau-af9d5f1c) is filed in the fizeau bead queue, but the changes it prescribes belong to the DDx CLI repository (github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx), not to fizeau (github.com/easel/fizeau). An operator must move/refile the bead into the DDx repo's queue, or explicitly redirect it to patch the vendored skill snapshot under `.agents/skills/cli/` here in fizeau. ## Evidence 1. This worktree's `go.mod` declares `module github.com/easel/fizeau`. There is no top-level `cli/` directory: $ ls cli ls: cannot access 'cli': No such file or directory 2. The bead names `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go`. Neither path exists at the repository root. The only copies present in this repo are read-only skill-bundle mirrors: ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go These are vendored snapshots of the DDx CLI source distributed with the DDx skills package; their `go.mod` says `module github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`. They were last touched by commit `d49a2428 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260508T205324-8992c1f5`, which is a generic checkpoint rather than feature work — consistent with them being a refresh-from-upstream artifact, not a maintenance target. 3. The acceptance criteria assume the DDx repo layout: AC1/AC2: `cd cli && go test -run … ./internal/agent/...` AC3:     `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` `cd cli` is not satisfiable here. The closest analogue (`cd .agents/skills/cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`) builds, but it tests vendored upstream code that fizeau does not run — fizeau invokes the `ddx` binary from PATH. Patching the vendored snapshot would not alter the behavior of the installed `ddx` binary that triggers the bug. 4. Patching the vendored snapshot would also be undone the next time the fizeau repo refreshes its skills bundle from upstream DDx, so any local change there is non-durable by design. 5. The bug itself is real and well-diagnosed (fizeau's `.gitignore` does contain `.ddx/executions/`, confirmed at line 28 of `.gitignore`). It is simply that fizeau is the *consumer* exposed by the bug, while the *fix site* is upstream DDx. ## What an operator needs to do Either: - **(preferred)** Recreate this bead in the DDx CLI repository's bead queue with the same title/description/AC, then close fizeau-af9d5f1c with a pointer to the relocated bead; or - **(only if intentional)** Amend the bead description and AC to specify patching `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` and `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` here, replace `cd cli` with `cd .agents/skills/cli` in the AC, and acknowledge in the bead that the patch will be overwritten on the next skills refresh from upstream and therefore needs to be mirrored upstream regardless. Without one of these operator decisions, an agent has no scope-legal way to satisfy this bead in the fizeau worktree. ## Why `proposed` and not `open` The blocker is not retryable by re-running an agent: the source files the bead prescribes are not in this repository, and that condition will not change between attempts. A higher-power model would reach the same dead end. Only an operator can refile or rescope the bead.

1. `go test ./internal/reasoning/... ./internal/harnesses/...` passes,
   including:

     [low, medium] snaps to `medium` and emits a structured warning

description:
Mirror of bead B but for the reasoning-effort axis.
ModelDiscoverySnapshot already captures `ReasoningLevels []string` per
harness via `--effort <level> ... (low,medium,high)` parsing. Today
nothing reads it.

## Scope

1. When reasoningRequestOptions resolves the wire form for a harness
   request, if the discovery snapshot for that harness includes a
   ReasoningLevels list, validate the caller's policy against it:
   - If caller passed `KindNamed` and the value IS in the harness's
     ReasoningLevels: emit as-is.
   - If caller passed `KindNamed` and the value is NOT supported: snap
     to nearest supported tier via PortableBudgets distance, log a
     structured signal.
   - If caller passed `KindTokens`: convert to nearest tier via bead
     A's PortableBudgets path, then validate against ReasoningLevels.

2. When discovery snapshot has no ReasoningLevels (older cassette,
   discovery failure), fall through to today's behavior (no validation).

3. Surface the resolved effort + the source (caller / snapped /
   default) in the route status / session jsonl.

## In-scope files

- internal/reasoning/policy.go (or wherever resolution lives) — add
  validation against discovery's ReasoningLevels
- internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/runner.go — pass discovery snapshot
  to reasoning resolution
- internal/reasoning/policy_test.go — table tests for snap-to-supported
  behavior

## Out of scope

- Auto-detecting ReasoningLevels from the harness CLI live (already
  captured by existing model_discovery.go code; this bead just consumes
  the result).
- Reasoning effort × model interaction (some models in the catalog
  declare their own `reasoning_levels` in the catalog entry; that
  signal is already consumed elsewhere — this bead adds the
  harness-level signal as an additional source).
title: Wire ReasoningLevels from harness discovery into reasoning policy validation

GitHub CI is still failing the root package Test job after the modelregistry and rename-noise fixes. description:
PROBLEM
When a candidate from a multi-tier subscription harness is excluded (e.g., the catalog has no power metadata for that tier), the routing_decision event drops the row silently rather than emitting it with an explicit filter_reason. This makes downstream diagnosis impossible — "absent" is ambiguous between "never enumerated" and "filtered out".

SCOPE
- Verify the routing engine emits an ineligible Candidate row (with FilterReason set) when a same-harness tier is known to the harness (in AutoRoutingModels / SupportedModels) but missing required catalog metadata.
- If absent today, ensure buildHarnessCandidates emits a row with eligible=false, filter_reason=FilterReasonPowerMissing (or similar) instead of dropping it.
- Negative test: stub a claude HarnessEntry with sonnet-4.6 enumerated but ModelEligibility lookup returning power=0 for sonnet — assert the candidate appears in Decision.Candidates with FilterReason=FilterReasonPowerMissing.

DEPENDS ON
fizeau-f7fa1d87 (per-tier enumeration must work first).

CROSS-REF
Parent: fizeau-80d4bf41
title: routing: emit filter_reason for excluded same-harness tier (no silent absence)

description:
Replace hardcoded default model selection in `internal/harnesses/codex/runner.go` and `internal/harnesses/claude/runner.go` with cassette-backed `ModelDiscoverySnapshot` selection. Explicit `--model <id>` pins still win.

## Current state

- `internal/harnesses/registry.go` embeds model discovery snapshots for Codex and Claude.
- `internal/harnesses/types.go` defines `ModelDiscoverySnapshot` with `Models []string`, `ReasoningLevels []string`, and `FreshnessWindow`.
- `internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go` from `fizeau-b2c5c826` provides the version-aware ranker: `modelcatalog.Parse(id)` returns `ParsedModel`, and `ParsedModel.Compare(other)` orders same-family models newest and strongest first.
- `internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go` exposes `Catalog.ModelEligibility(id)` and `ModelEntry.AutoRoutable()` for catalog IDs and declared surface IDs.
- The embedded catalog maps Codex model IDs on `modelcatalog.SurfaceCodex` and Claude Code model IDs on `modelcatalog.SurfaceClaudeCode`.

## Problem

Codex and Claude runner defaults are still hardcoded. Adding a newer auto-routable model should require only discovery/catalog evidence, not another runner code edit.

## Scope

1. Add an in-process discovery snapshot cache in `internal/harnesses/discovery_cache.go`, keyed by harness name and snapshot source. For this bead, the source is the embedded cassette snapshot already present in the registry; no live PTY refresh is required.
2. When no operator `--model` pin is provided, each runner loads the cached snapshot, filters `snapshot.Models` through `Catalog.ModelEligibility(modelID)`, keeps only `AutoRoutable == true`, sorts same-family candidates with `modelcatalog.Parse(...).Compare(...)`, and chooses the highest-ranked candidate.
3. Use `modelcatalog.SurfaceCodex` for Codex runner catalog projection and `modelcatalog.SurfaceClaudeCode` for Claude runner catalog projection. The concrete discovery strings `gpt-5.5` and `opus-4.7` are valid catalog surface IDs today.
4. When an explicit `--model <id>` pin is provided, keep using that exact pin. Discovery may warn if the pin is absent from the snapshot, but must not override it.
5. Surface a structured runner/default-resolution signal when the resolved default differs from the prior hardcoded default, so operators can see when discovery changes the selected model.
6. If snapshot lookup fails, fall back to the prior hardcoded default and emit a warning. Do not fail runner construction on discovery errors.

## Non-scope

- ReasoningLevels wiring; that is `fizeau-b0366923`.
- Persistent on-disk discovery storage.
- Live PTY refresh or background goroutine refresh.
- Cross-harness selection or cross-family ranking.

## Expected embedded defaults

Using the current embedded snapshots and catalog:

- Codex no-pin default resolves to `gpt-5.5`.
- Claude no-pin default resolves to `opus-4.7`.
- Codex explicit `--model gpt-5.4` remains `gpt-5.4`.
notes: Operator resolved intake ambiguity: named the closed ranker API, fixed catalog surfaces, pinned Claude expected default, and removed live-refresh contradiction.

description:
Add a small parser + ranker primitive in `internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go`
that orders model IDs within a family by (version, tier) so harness
discovery results can be sorted "newest most powerful first." Foundation
for bead B (wire harness discovery into runner as model authority).

## Scope

1. New file `internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go`:

```go
type Tier int
const (
    TierSmart    Tier = 0  // unsuffixed gpt, opus claude, pro gemini
    TierStandard Tier = 1  // mini gpt, sonnet claude, flash gemini
    TierCheap    Tier = 2  // nano gpt, haiku claude, flash-lite gemini
    TierUnknown  Tier = 99
)

var FamilyTiers = map[string]map[string]Tier{
    "gpt":    {"": TierSmart, "mini": TierStandard, "nano": TierCheap},
    "claude": {"opus": TierSmart, "sonnet": TierStandard, "haiku": TierCheap},
    "gemini": {"pro": TierSmart, "flash": TierStandard, "flash-lite": TierCheap},
}

type ParsedModel struct {
    Family     string  // "gpt", "claude", "gemini", ...
    Version    []int   // [5, 5] for gpt-5.5; [4, 7] for opus-4.7; normalized so [5] -> [5, 0]
    Tier       Tier    // smart/standard/cheap/unknown
    PreRelease bool    // true for "preview", "alpha", etc.
    Raw        string  // original ID
}

func Parse(id string) ParsedModel
func (a ParsedModel) Compare(b ParsedModel) int  // returns 0 across families
```

2. Lexicographic version comparison. `[5,5] > [5,4] > [5,3]`. Missing
   minor segments normalized to 0 on parse so `[5]` and `[5,0]` compare
   equal.

3. Cross-family Compare returns 0 (no opinion). Use catalog `power`
   field for cross-family ranking.

4. Pre-release suffix detection: `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, `rc`. GA
   beats pre-release of the same version.

5. Unknown tier suffix (e.g. `gpt-5.3-codex-spark`) parses to
   `TierUnknown`. Sort order: TierUnknown ranks LOWER than all known
   tiers. The model is still returnable for explicit pin or fallback
   pool routing (downstream concern, not this bead).

## Test cases (locked in)

In `internal/modelcatalog/tiers_test.go`:

```
gpt-5.5             → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierSmart,    pre=false}
gpt-5.5-mini        → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierStandard, pre=false}
gpt-5.5-nano        → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierCheap,    pre=false}
gpt-5.4             → {gpt,    [5,4],   TierSmart,    pre=false}
gpt-5.4-mini        → {gpt,    [5,4],   TierStandard, pre=false}
gpt-5               → {gpt,    [5,0],   TierSmart,    pre=false}    # normalized
gpt-5.5-preview     → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierSmart,    pre=true}
gpt-5.3-codex-spark → {gpt,    [5,3],   TierUnknown,  pre=false}
claude-opus-4-7     → {claude, [4,7],   TierSmart,    pre=false}
claude-sonnet-4-6   → {claude, [4,6],   TierStandard, pre=false}
claude-haiku-4-5    → {claude, [4,5],   TierCheap,    pre=false}
```

Sort orderings:

```
sort([gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.5-nano, gpt-5.4]) ==
     [gpt-5.5,      gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5-nano, gpt-5.4]

sort([gpt-5.5-preview, gpt-5.5]) == [gpt-5.5, gpt-5.5-preview]

sort([opus-4.6, opus-4.7, sonnet-4.7, haiku-4.7]) ==
     [opus-4.7, sonnet-4.7, haiku-4.7, opus-4.6]
     # within version: tier wins; across versions: newer version wins
```

Cross-family compare returns 0:

```
Parse("gpt-5.5").Compare(Parse("claude-opus-4-7")) == 0
```

## Catalog monotonicity invariants

Extend `internal/modelcatalog/embedded_power_test.go` to assert:
- For each (family, tier) cohort in the embedded catalog: `power` is
  non-decreasing as version increases.
- For each (family, version) cohort: `power` is non-increasing as tier
  rank increases (smart > standard > cheap).

Build fails if catalog values violate these. Cross-family comparisons
are not asserted (that's catalog-power's job, not the ranker's).

## In-scope files

- internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go (new)
- internal/modelcatalog/tiers_test.go (new)
- internal/modelcatalog/embedded_power_test.go (extend with monotonicity
  assertions)

## Out of scope

- Wiring into routing or harness runners (bead B).
- ReasoningLevels parsing (bead C).
- IncludeByDefault filter (bead D).
- quota_pool catalog field (bead E1).
- Cross-family ranking (catalog power's job, not the ranker's).

description:
PROBLEM
With the runner skeleton (A.1), shell harness-adapters (A.2), and harbor-runner image (A.3) in place, the runner still needs the execution engine: per-cell loop, resume logic, concurrency-group flocks, signal handling, and the preflight subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
These behaviors live in cmd/bench/matrix.go's cell-execution block (process-group setsid, per-group locks, SIGINT/SIGTERM trap, resume-by-report.json). They must move to bash to complete the runner.

PROPOSED FIX
In scripts/benchmark/benchmark, implement:
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; pipe profile.json → harness-adapters/<name> → command-spec.json; compose task-spec.json; pipe → task-executors/<name> (which writes cell_dir/result.json); compose cell_dir/report.json; delete cell-state.json on terminal status.
  - Resume: skip cell when cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status. --force-rerun overrides. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR orphan cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock; setsid for each cell; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each in-flight cell process group AND `docker stop` each in-flight fizeau-harbor-<cell-id> container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
  - `preflight` subcommand: verify yq/jq/docker/setsid/flock present, fizeau-harbor-runner image buildable or pullable, required state dirs writable; exits non-zero with a checklist on failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete cmd/bench/matrix.go or harbor_adapters/*.py here — a later PR removes them.
- Do not change adapter contracts established in A.2.
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. End-to-end: `./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --reps 1 --jobs 2 --out tmp/` runs cells via shell adapter + harbor task-executor and produces report.json per cell; covered by TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd (skips when docker unavailable).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted asserts re-run with same --out skips cells with terminal report.json unless --force-rerun.
3. TestBenchmarkRunnerRetryInvalid asserts --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json.
4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock asserts two cells in the same concurrency-group serialize via the documented lock path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling asserts SIGTERM stops new cells, terminates in-flight cell process groups, and issues `docker stop` for in-flight harbor containers within 30s before SIGKILL.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight asserts `./benchmark preflight` returns 0 on healthy host and non-zero with a per-check checklist when a dependency is missing.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: bench-pr-A.4: per-cell loop + flock concurrency + signal handling + preflight

2. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutConfigurable asserts ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config overrides the default.
3. TestProviderDeadLocalHost_FailsUnder5s — integration test: aim at an unroutable RFC 5737 address (e.g., 192.0.2.1:1) and assert the provider call returns within 5.5s with a dial error. (No process listening means kernel-level connect timeout exercises the bounded dial.)

description:
PROBLEM
A dead local provider endpoint costs ~30 seconds of wall-clock per call before Fizeau surfaces the dial error. The fizeau-8134d6db AC required dial-class failures to bail in under 5 seconds; the shipped implementation only short-circuits the *retry loop* (one attempt instead of four), but the *dial itself* still uses Go's default TCP connect timeout (~30s on Linux). Observed today: every bead routed to bragi:1234 / bragi:8020 / grendel:1234 / grendel:1235 / grendel:8000 took ~30s to fail with "dial tcp ... i/o timeout". With 14 beads in the ddx queue all routing to a single dead host, the drain spent ~7 minutes establishing that the host was dead — meeting fizeau-8134d6db's letter (single attempt) while missing its spirit (under 5s).

ROOT CAUSE
internal/core/loop.go:643 (IsDialError short-circuit) is correctly wired. The retry-loop layer fails fast.

What is NOT bounded: the underlying http.Transport's DialContext. internal/provider/openai/openai.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat construct openaicompat.Config without overriding the HTTP client's transport. Go's net/http default Transport has no explicit DialContext, so it uses net.Dialer{} with Timeout=0 — which on Linux waits for the kernel's full TCP SYN retransmit timeout (≈21s + RTO backoff = 30s observed). For a known-local endpoint that the operator just unplugged, 30s is pure waste.

PROPOSED FIX
- Configure an explicit net.Dialer with Timeout=5s (or ServiceOptions-driven, defaulting to 5s) in the openaicompat client construction path.
- Wrap http.Transport.DialContext so the connect phase is bounded independent of the request streaming timeout.
- Keep the request-streaming timeout (Timeout field) separate — large requests legitimately take minutes once connected.
- Expose ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config so callers (DDx, benchmarks) can tune for high-latency networks if needed; default 5s.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the retry-loop short-circuit (already correct).
- Do not change in-flight 5xx retry behavior.
- Do not bound the request body / streaming timeout in this bead.

DEPS
None.
title: routing: bound TCP dial timeout to ~5s, not Go default ~30s

description:
PROBLEM
After publishing v0.14.12, branch CI on master failed in two gates: `make gosec` reported G304/G306 in `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, and `website-smoke` failed in `TestBenchmarkWorkbenchSmoke` because Chromium discovery did not find the browser installed by Playwright.

ROOT CAUSE
Route-health probe persistence used direct `os.ReadFile`/`os.WriteFile` on an intentional user-selected path and wrote 0644, bypassing the repo safefs wrapper used for gosec-scoped filesystem operations. Website smoke only searched the older Playwright `chrome-linux` layout and missed newer Chrome-for-Testing `chrome-linux64` / headless-shell layouts.

PROPOSED FIX
Route probe persistence should use `internal/safefs` and private 0600 permissions. Website Chromium discovery should recognize both old and new Playwright cache layouts without changing smoke-test behavior.

description:
PROBLEM
  internal/routing engine carries legacy Profile field naming, treats MinPower/MaxPower as hard candidate filters, threads a ModelRef field the v0.11 surface no longer uses, and resolves catalog refs through Inputs.CatalogResolver callbacks the service layer no longer needs. ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier is a field-name bug — it always held the policy name.

ROOT CAUSE
  Engine predates the v0.11 routing-surface redesign. "Profile" was the canonical routing input, "model-ref" was a kitchen-sink string accepting policies/targets/aliases, and power bounds were enforced as exclusion gates rather than scoring signals.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - "Policy" is the single canonical word for the named routing-intent bundle. No aliases.
  - ModelRef removed; callers pass concrete req.Model when pinning, otherwise rely on req.Policy.
  - MinPower/MaxPower preserved on the request but become asymmetric scoring weights, NOT filters. Undershoot 5x worse than overshoot: power_score = -5*max(0, min_power - cand.power) - 1*max(0, cand.power - max_power).
  - New per-policy bool req.AllowLocal controls whether local-billing candidates are included. Smart's allow_local=false produces fail-closed when subscription is dry (no candidates → ErrNoLiveProvider).
  - require[] is a list of hard-constraint predicates applied as candidate filter. v0.11 ships with one: "no_remote" (cand.DeploymentClass == "local").
  - Pin-pin conflicts (harness×model, harness×provider, provider×model) fail with ErrUnsatisfiablePin BEFORE scoring.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - internal/routing/engine.go:16-34 Request struct: Profile, ModelRef, MinPower/MaxPower fields
  - internal/routing/engine.go:284-308 ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier (field-name bug)
  - internal/routing/engine.go:305 ProfileEscalationLadder
  - internal/routing/engine.go:1175-1209 ModelRef-driven catalog resolution branch
  - internal/routing/engine.go:1229-1257 EscalateProfileAware
  - internal/routing/errors.go:11-15,46-98 errProfilePinConflict, errNoProfileCandidate sentinels
  - internal/routing/score.go:28,36,58-101 scorePolicy switch on profile name strings
  - internal/routing/gating.go pin-collision predicates

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Rename Request.Profile → Request.Policy. Add Request.AllowLocal bool, Request.Require []string. Drop Request.ModelRef.
  2. Add asymmetric soft-power scoring in score.go (formula above).
  3. Add pin-pin compatibility predicate in gating.go. Apply Require[] predicates as candidate filter. Only "no_remote" defined in v0.11.
  4. Delete Inputs.CatalogResolver and Inputs.CatalogCandidatesResolver fields. Drop the ModelRef branch in resolveModel (engine.go:1175-1209).
  5. errors.go: replace ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate with single ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied{Policy, Requirement, AttemptedPin string, Rejected int}. Add ErrUnknownPolicy{Policy string}, ErrUnsatisfiablePin{Pin, Reason string}.
  6. Rename ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier → StartingPolicy. Rename ProfileEscalationLadder → PolicyEscalationLadder; values ["cheap", "default", "smart"].
  7. Rename EscalateProfileAware → EscalatePolicyAware. Internal helpers: missingProfileCapability → missingPolicyCapability; explicitProfileConstraint → explicitPolicyConstraint; harnessViolatesProfileConstraint, modelPinViolatesProfileConstraint similarly.
  8. score.go switch cases canonicalized: "cheap" / "default" / "smart". Format strings "profile=%s" → "policy=%s".

NON-SCOPE
  - Public service surface (service*.go) — Phase 4.
  - Catalog manifest schema — Phase 2.
  - CLI flags — Phase 6.
  - DDx coordination — Phase 9.

PARENT + DEPS
  Leads phase chain. Phase 2 depends on this.
  9. ErrNoLiveProvider enhancement: in addition to renaming StartingTier→StartingPolicy, add RejectedCandidates []CandidateRejection field. CandidateRejection carries {Provider, Harness, Model string, Reason string} where Reason is a typed enum-string ("quota_exhausted", "unhealthy", "model_unavailable", "harness_unhealthy", "policy_filtered"). Update ErrNoLiveProvider.Error() to surface rejection counts when len(RejectedCandidates) > 0:
     - empty rejected list: "no candidates match policy %q (power %d-%d, allow_local=%v); check policy fit and provider configuration"
     - non-empty: "policy %q has %d candidate(s) matching power %d-%d but all rejected: %d quota-exhausted, %d unhealthy, %d unavailable"
     This addresses the operator-visible error language gap raised by fizeau-0d15ab58 (DDx pre-claim readiness): today `no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens at tier >= smart` collapses two distinct operator scenarios (no smart route exists vs all smart routes are quota-dry); after this change the message tells the operator which bucket they're in.

acceptance:
1. `TestSweepShutdownKillReapsProcessTree` covers `bash scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase canary --lanes ds4 &` followed by `kill $!`, leaving zero surviving `fiz-bench` or `harbor run` processes within 30s as verified with `pgrep -f "fiz-bench|harbor run"`.
2. `TestSweepShutdownTermGraceful` covers `kill -TERM $!`, proving each in-flight Harbor invocation receives SIGTERM, can run docker-compose teardown through the existing `--delete` finalizer, waits up to 60s, then exits.
3. `TestSweepShutdownNoOrphans` in `scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash` spawns a sweep, sends SIGTERM, and asserts all sweep-related processes are gone within 60s.
4. `TestSweepShutdownReportState` in `scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash` asserts report.json files for in-flight cells are either complete or absent, with no half-written state.
description:
Killing a sweep parent (run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh PID) does not currently kill its descendant matrix-runner / harbor children. They are reparented to init (PPID=1) and keep executing. Concrete failures this session:

1. **2026-05-11 ~22:30Z** — Killed the local-lane sweep (PID 463900). Vidar matrix-runner child kept running on the dead oMLX server, wasted 70 cells with retry-spam pattern before I noticed.

2. **2026-05-11 ~18:15Z** — Killed the timing-baseline ds4 sweep (PID 942197) after the reasoning-not-supported bug. The matrix-runner became orphan (945491, PPID=1). Then I launched a replacement (991522 → 999500) and a second iteration (975660, orphan). All three were running simultaneously against ds4's single graph worker, contending for the slot and writing to the same report.json paths. Smoke tests timed out because of slot contention. Took ~30 min to notice and clean up.

The general pattern: any time we `kill -KILL <sweep_pid>` to restart with new config (multiplier change, profile fix, server-side issue), one or more children survive. They consume identical work, race the new sweep, and corrupt the per-cell results.

PROPOSED FIX

Two interlocking pieces:

A. **run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh: trap signals + propagate.** Add a trap handler in the shell script that on SIGTERM/SIGINT/EXIT:
   - Records the matrix-runner PIDs as they spawn
   - On signal, sends SIGTERM to each matrix-runner PID; if still alive after 10s, SIGKILL
   - Then exits

B. **cmd/bench (sweep + matrix subcommands): handle SIGTERM by killing harbor children.** When fiz-bench receives SIGTERM, propagate to in-flight `harbor run` subprocesses (exec.CommandContext already supports this via context cancellation). The existing `cmd.Cancel = func() error { return cmd.Process.Signal(syscall.SIGTERM) }` is correct shape; verify it actually fires on parent SIGTERM and not just on context.WithTimeout.

C. **Alternative (single-knob fix): process groups.** Have the sweep script start fiz-bench in its own process group (`setpgid`), and have the kill path send SIGTERM to the entire process group (`kill -TERM -<pgid>`). One signal takes out the whole tree atomically. Simpler than A+B; downside is no graceful per-process shutdown order.

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh — trap handler + PID tracking
- cmd/bench/sweep.go and cmd/bench/matrix.go — signal handling on the Go side (verify existing exec.CommandContext + cmd.Cancel work as intended on SIGTERM, not just context cancel)
- Tests / integration: confirm a `kill -TERM $sweep_pid` reaps the entire process tree within 30s.

Out-of-scope:
- Persistent state recovery (sweep resume from interrupted cell). Already handled by --resume + report.json being the durable state.
- Distinguishing "user-initiated kill" from "supervisor-initiated kill" — both should cleanly shut down.

ACCEPTANCE IS A COMMAND

1. `bash scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase canary --lanes ds4 &` then `kill $!` results in ZERO surviving fiz-bench or harbor processes within 30s (verify via `pgrep -f "fiz-bench|harbor run"`).

2. Same scenario but `kill -TERM` — the shutdown is graceful: each in-flight cell's harbor invocation gets SIGTERM (lets it tear down its docker-compose stack via the existing `--delete` finalizer, see cmd/bench/matrix.go cmd.Cancel handler), waits up to 60s for it to finish, then exits.

3. New integration test scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash spawns a sweep, sends SIGTERM, asserts (a) all sweep-related processes are gone within 60s, (b) report.json files for in-flight cells are either complete or absent (no half-written state).
notes:
Operator made ACs mechanically verifiable with explicit test names and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.

Review 2026-05-12: bash scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash fails locally, timing out waiting for fake-harbor started; process-tree shutdown is not end-to-end verified.

Review 2026-05-12 final pass: timeout 120s bash scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash failed locally at TestSweepShutdownKillReapsProcessTree: timed out waiting for log fake-harbor started. Parent fizeau-1a5353cd remains open until the named shell acceptance test passes reliably.

Review 2026-05-12 follow-up: after the worker closed this bead, local verification failed again: timeout 120s bash scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash exited at TestSweepShutdownKillReapsProcessTree with 'timed out waiting for log fake-harbor started'. Go coverage passed, but the shell acceptance remains non-deterministic/failing, so parent fizeau-1a5353cd stays open.

description:
Operator-driven integration bead. Depends on schema (fizeau-1567453f),
introspection (fizeau-e80c156e), translator (fizeau-144bd2f9), probe-tool
(fizeau-d72b32a0), and token-extraction (fizeau-8f62bcbb) beads all

with any L2 values still required (mainly OR-Qwen3), fixes the surface-id
lookup mismatch, updates the ds4 catalog entry, and reverts benchmark
profiles to portable named-tier intent.

Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11, lanes with rich introspection (ds4 via
/props, llama-server via /props) get their wire form auto-derived at
construction time — catalog L2 values are unnecessary for them. L2 is
only needed where introspection is thin: OR-Qwen3.

Steps (must land as a single commit set — partial state regresses runs):

1. **Run the probe** against each of:
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.yaml
   - scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-ds4.yaml
   Save snapshots + verification matrix artifacts under
   docs/research/probe-reasoning-2026-05-11/<lane>/.

2. **Catalog updates** in internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:
   - `qwen3.6-27b` entry: set `reasoning_wire: tokens` (L2 value, used
     when the provider is OR — introspection adapters cover the local
     Qwen3 lanes). Today the entry has `reasoning_wire: model_id`
     which is wrong per ADR-010 §Latent-bug-and-the-fix; replace.
   - `deepseek-v4-flash` entry (CREATE if missing): catalog ID

3. **Fix surface-id lookup** at internal/config/config.go:925-944
   modelReasoningWireMap():

     mismatch for any model where catalog ID != surface ID (e.g.

4. **Revert all four Qwen + ds4 benchmark profiles** from

description:
PROBLEM
The current root has an uncommitted but coherent route-health fix: execute dispatch failures emit `routing_actual` in the final event, but the service does not record that failed provider/model attempt into route health. A subsequent `ResolveRoute` can pick the same failed provider again even when the final event already classifies the failure as transport/availability/protocol.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `service_execute.go:705` and `service_execute_dispatch.go:82` finalize native/dispatch executions without feeding final routing evidence into `RecordRouteAttempt`.
- `service_execute.go:811-814` handles subprocess final events but does not persist route-attempt health from them.
- `service_route_attempts.go:31-55` has `RecordRouteAttempt`, but no helper that maps `harnesses.FinalData.RoutingActual` into a `RouteAttempt`.

PROPOSED FIX
Record route attempts from final execution events when `RoutingActual` is present. Record successes and dispatchability failures; ignore semantic/non-dispatch failures. Split endpoint-qualified providers with the existing endpoint/provider helper. Add a regression test proving a transport failure from `Execute` causes the next `ResolveRoute` to reject the failed provider as unhealthy.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- service_execute.go
- service_execute_dispatch.go
- service_route_attempts.go
- service_route_attempts_test.go

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change route scoring weights or route selection policy beyond feeding existing route health.
- Do not record semantic/model-output failures as provider health failures.
- Do not modify DDx tracker files by hand.

DEPS
None.
title: routing: record execute dispatch failures into route health

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead fizeau-11e9c095 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the first bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: bench-pr-A: bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + --prese: part 1

description:
Add an optional `quota_pool: <name>` field to ModelEntry in the catalog
manifest. Foundation for ADR-011 (bead E2) cost-based routing — models
that share a quota allocation share a pool name; models with
independent quotas (e.g. gpt-5.3-codex-spark) get their own pool.

This bead is schema-only. No routing consumers yet. Lands as preparation
so ADR-011 can reference a real field.

## Scope

1. Add `QuotaPool string` field to ModelEntry in
   `internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go`. YAML tag `quota_pool,omitempty`.
2. Default semantic when unset: model's quota pool == its provider
   system name (e.g., openai-hosted models share the "openai" pool by
   default; ds4 models share "ds4"). Documented in the field's GoDoc.
3. Add a helper `ModelEntry.EffectiveQuotaPool() string` that returns
   the explicit pool if set, else the default-derived pool.
4. Round-trip test: a manifest with `quota_pool: openai-codex-spark` on
   a model entry round-trips through Load and serialization without
   loss.
5. Validator: pool name must be non-empty when set; characters
   restricted to lowercase, digits, dash (no spaces, no special chars).

## Catalog updates included in this bead

Annotate ONE example entry to exercise the field:
- `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` (if it exists in the catalog; else add it):
  `quota_pool: openai-codex-spark`. Documents the canonical fallback-
  pool case.

If `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` is not yet in the catalog, this bead does NOT
add it — that's an operator decision. Just exercise the field on any
existing entry.

## In-scope files

- internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go
- internal/modelcatalog/manifest_test.go
- internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml (only if exercising on an
  existing entry)

## Out of scope

- Routing consumers of the field (bead E2 / ADR-011 implementation).
- Quota signal infrastructure (separate, builds on commit 7776890e).
- Per-model pricing fields (already exist).
title: Catalog quota_pool field schema for cost-based routing fallback

Bench runner stamps runtime_props into each cell when the platform supports it; missing/failed extraction is logged but non-fatal. go test ./... passes. docs/benchmarks/ has a runtime-props.md note.
description:
When a bench cell runs, capture the server-reported runtime properties for the (provider, model) it ran against and persist them in the cell evidence. The point: many cell-cell deltas (MTP on/off, speculative decoder configs, kv-quant variants) are NOT in the lane definition — they vary by server config or per-request flags. Capturing server-reported props per cell makes the comparison grid self-describing and lets us run more attempts with parameter sweeps rather than pre-declaring every combination as a separate lane.

Scope:
1. Schema: extend scripts/benchmark/benchmark-evidence.schema.json with a new optional 'runtime_props' object on the evidence record. Fields it should accommodate (all optional): base_model, model_quant, kv_quant, draft_model, draft_mode (e.g. mtp, dflash, eagle), max_context, sampling defaults (temperature, top_p, top_k, repeat_penalty), build_info, server_version, gpu_layers, mtp_enabled, speculative_n, plus a free-form 'platform_raw' blob preserving the original server response for grep-ability.

2. Per-platform extractors in a new package (e.g. internal/benchmark/runtimeprops/ with one file per platform):
   - llamacpp: GET /props (the canonical metadata endpoint)
   - vllm: GET /v1/models + best-effort /metrics or /server_info
   - ds4: investigate the server's exposed metadata endpoint (try /v1/models, /health, /props, /metadata; pick the richest one returning useful fields)
   - omlx: similar — likely just /v1/models for now
   - rapid-mlx: similar
   - lucebox: investigate the dflash server's metadata surface
   - openai/openrouter: just record model_id from response, no extra fetch
3. Integration: the bench runner calls the appropriate extractor once per cell (after preflight, before bench start), stamps runtime_props into the evidence record. Failure to fetch must NOT fail the cell — log a 'runtime_props.extraction_failed: reason' and continue.

4. Tests: per-platform extractor unit tests with canned response fixtures; schema validator confirms runtime_props is accepted.

5. Documentation: brief notes in docs/benchmarks/ on what runtime_props captures, where it lives in the cell, and how to add a new platform extractor.
title: Capture per-cell runtime props from supported platforms

description:
DEFERRED. Filed proposed-status for future work.

OpenRouter routes a single model (e.g. qwen/qwen3.6-27b) to multiple
upstream sub-providers (DeepInfra, Together, Fireworks, etc.). Each
has its own pricing, latency, context window, and rate limits. For
high-fidelity routing, the snapshot should distinguish them.

## Syntax (per ADR-012 naming convention)

`<provider>/<model>@<sub_provider>`. Examples:
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b@deepinfra`
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b@together`
- `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6@anthropic`

## Source

OR's `/api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints` returns:
```
{
  "data": {
    "endpoints": [
      {"provider_name": "DeepInfra", "context_length": ..., ...},
      {"provider_name": "Together", "context_length": ..., ...}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Why deferred

- Per-OR-model API call (N+1 problem; OR has hundreds of models)
- Cardinality blowup in the snapshot (each model becomes N entries)
- Requires routing-layer changes to actually USE the sub-provider
  selection
- Marginal value for current benchmark-mode use case (we're not
  routing through OR at scale)

## Re-evaluate when

- We ship cost-based routing (ADR-011, bead E2) and quota signals
  start mattering across OR sub-providers
- We benchmark OR-Qwen3 across multiple upstream sub-providers and
  see measurable performance differences
- An operator explicitly requests it

## Depends on

- M2 (snapshot assembler) — extension, not parallel implementation

description:
PROBLEM
GitHub CI run 25933751955 for master commit a6a34bdd passed build, vet, lint, security scan, vulnerability scan, format, rename noise, normal go test, website smoke, adapter pytest, and internal/harnesses/codex under race, but failed in Test (race) in internal/harnesses/gemini.

OBSERVED FAILURE
make test-race runs CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race -count=1 ./... . internal/harnesses/gemini.TestReadGeminiQuotaViaPTY_CapturesTierUsage failed with: want 3 tier windows, got 2. The captured windows were Flash and Flash Lite; Pro was missing.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_pty.go:120-127 has geminiQuotaComplete return true once ParseGeminiModelManage finds at least one tier. ptyquota.Run then snapshots the terminal screen immediately. Gemini CLI renders /model manage tier rows incrementally, and the test fake script also writes rows through a PTY. Under race/CI timing, the probe can stop while the screen contains only the first one or two tier rows even though the process is still alive and more output is arriving.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_pty.go
- internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_pty_test.go if a focused regression assertion is needed
- internal/harnesses/ptyquota/probe.go only if the stable-output behavior belongs in the shared probe primitive

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not disable make test-race.
- Do not remove the Pro-tier assertion from the test.
- Do not change Gemini routing or quota interpretation semantics except for when PTY capture decides output has settled.

EXPECTED FIX
Make the PTY quota capture wait for parsed Gemini quota output to settle long enough that incremental tier rows are included, while still allowing real Gemini sessions that only surface a subset of tiers to complete after their displayed output stabilizes.
title: ci: make Gemini PTY quota race capture stable

description:
PROBLEM
  Public service surface (service*.go, routing_errors.go, internal/serviceimpl, internal/core) exposes Profile naming, ModelRef fields, ResolveProfile/ProfileAliases methods, and a sprawling FizeauService interface (17 methods). The v0.11 surface trims this to ~14 methods, renames Profile→Policy, drops ModelRef entirely, and removes target-language from comments/error strings.

ROOT CAUSE
  Public surface predates v0.11. Profile/ModelRef were the canonical inputs; ResolveProfile returned per-surface preview data tied to the dropped Surface concept. Target language is sprinkled through service_models.go and other files as legacy of the dropped target abstraction.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - FizeauService interface methods: rename ListProfiles→ListPolicies; drop ResolveProfile and ProfileAliases entirely.
  - Types: ProfileInfo→PolicyInfo (no AliasOf field, no Target field); drop ResolvedProfile, ProfileSurface entirely.
  - Request fields: Profile→Policy on RouteRequest and ServiceExecuteRequest. Add AllowLocal bool, Require []string. Drop ModelRef from RouteRequest, ServiceExecuteRequest, and ResolvedModelRef field. Keep MinPower/MaxPower as soft signals.
  - RouteDecision.RequestedProfile→RequestedPolicy. RoutePowerPolicy{Profile, MinPower, MaxPower}→RoutePowerPolicy{PolicyName, MinPower, MaxPower}.
  - Public errors: ErrProfilePinConflict/ErrNoProfileCandidate/ErrUnknownProfile→ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied + ErrUnknownPolicy + ErrUnsatisfiablePin (mirroring Phase 1 routing errors).
  - HarnessInfo: drop IsLocal/IsSubscription bools, replace with Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel.
  - ProviderInfo: add IncludeByDefault bool, Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel.
  - ModelInfo: add Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel; drop CatalogRef field.
  - Scrub all routing-context "target" language from production code (comments, error strings, helper function names like catalogRefForModel). Add scoped lint rule preventing reintroduction in service_*.go, internal/routing/, internal/modelcatalog/.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - service.go:22-43 FizeauService interface (17 methods)
  - service.go:228-249 HarnessInfo (IsLocal/IsSubscription)
  - service.go:260-313 ProviderInfo, ModelInfo
  - service.go:321-360 ProfileInfo, ResolvedProfile, ProfileSurface
  - service.go:368-404 RouteRequest (Profile, ModelRef, MinPower/MaxPower fields)
  - service.go:441-484 RouteDecision, RoutePowerPolicy
  - service.go:676-796 ServiceExecuteRequest (Profile, ModelRef, ResolvedModelRef, MinPower/MaxPower)
  - service_profiles.go (~190 lines, rename to service_policies.go and rewrite)
  - service_routing.go (59+ Profile/ModelRef hits)
  - service_models.go:491,559 catalogRefForModel + target-level pricing comments
  - service_session_log.go:79-80,130-131 RequestedModelRef/ResolvedModelRef field assignments
  - service_override.go:108 ModelRef field assignment
  - routing_errors.go:170-251 ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate, ErrUnknownProfile

PROPOSED FIX
  1. service.go: rename interface methods (ListProfiles→ListPolicies; drop ResolveProfile, ProfileAliases). Replace ProfileInfo/ResolvedProfile/ProfileSurface with PolicyInfo. Update all request/response types (RouteRequest, RouteDecision, RoutePowerPolicy, ServiceExecuteRequest, HarnessInfo, ProviderInfo, ModelInfo) per design intent.
  2. Rename service_profiles.go → service_policies.go. Rewrite: implement ListPolicies returning []PolicyInfo from cat.Policies(). Drop everything else.
  3. service_routing.go: rename req.Profile→req.Policy; drop ModelRef reads; map cat.Policy(req.Policy) → routing.Request{Policy, AllowLocal, Require, MinPower, MaxPower}; rewrite escalateProfileLadder→escalatePolicyLadder; rewrite serviceRoutingReasoningResolver to query cat.LookupModel(modelID).ReasoningDefault directly (no surfaces).
  4. service_models.go: drop catalogRefForModel function (dead after ModelRef removal); rewrite cost fallback to use cat.LookupModel(modelID).CostInputPerMTok directly (no PricingFor target-level lookup).
  5. service_session_log.go: drop RequestedModelRef/ResolvedModelRef field assignments.
  6. service_override.go: drop ModelRef passthrough.
  7. routing_errors.go: replace public error types per design intent. Rename ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier→StartingPolicy.
  8. Scrub target-language: comments referring to "target" in routing context → "policy" or "model"; HealthTarget stays (different concept).
  9. Add scoped lint: new file internal/lint/targetlint/analyzer.go (modeled on DDx's routinglint) that flags identifiers and comments containing "target" in routing context inside service_*.go, internal/routing/, internal/modelcatalog/.
  10. Update all service_*_test.go inline YAML fixtures and struct field references (~25 files).

NON-SCOPE
  - Internal core (internal/core/) — Phase 7.
  - CLI flags — Phase 6.
  - Provider config (user-config) — Phase 5.
  - DDx coordination — Phase 9.

PARENT + DEPS
  Depends on Phases 1, 2, 3.

description:
Refactor/verify autorouting purity: ResolveRoute and Execute autorouting decisions must consume only explicit client inputs plus the current fiz models snapshot.

No hidden provider-health, quota, discovery, or utilization probes should run in the scorer or candidate builder. Freshness updates are done by the snapshot refresh path before routing, not by a parallel route-side fact path. This makes routing decisions replayable from snapshot fixtures and operator-visible via fiz models.

Hard gates remain allowed for true constraints or dispatchability represented in the snapshot: exact pins/pin conflicts, no_remote, metered actual-spend refusal, missing required tools/context/reasoning, known down endpoints, quota exhausted, and policy require[] constraints. Unknown/stale/refresh_failed facts should normally become penalties unless the snapshot explicitly says known-down or exhausted.

In scope: service ResolveRoute inputs, internal routing input projection, tests with fixture snapshots and fake providers that panic if probed.

Out of scope: changing score weights; changing fiz models CLI behavior.
title: Route only from client inputs and the models snapshot

description:
PROBLEM
The Fizeau repo has 103 .go files at the repo root, all in `package fizeau` (or `package fizeau_test`). This includes service.go, service_routing.go, service_execute*.go, harness_golden_integration_test.go, contract003_*_test.go, metadata_billing.go, options_*.go, provider_*.go, public_api*.go, role_correlation.go, routing_errors*.go, and ~85 other files spanning the entire service surface.

This violates standard Go project layout:
- Library code lives under `internal/` or `pkg/`, not at the repo root.
- Root is typically reserved for CHANGELOG, README, LICENSE, go.mod, and (optionally) the `main` package binary.
- 103 files in one package is hard to navigate; package-level cohesion is lost.

It also creates spurious friction:
- IDE / `gopls` slows on the big package.
- Adding a new top-level helper requires choosing between "another root .go file" and "carve out a subpackage" with no precedent.
- The default ddx execution worktree clones the entire repo including the root .go pile, multiplying disk during the parallel-drain.

ROOT CAUSE
Historical accident. The Fizeau repo grew from a single-file prototype; new files kept landing at the root because that's where the existing service.go lived. There was never an explicit decision to move to internal/.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Create `internal/service/` (or `internal/fizeau/` — choose based on import-name ergonomics).
2. Move all 103 root .go files into the new directory.
3. Rename their package declarations from `package fizeau` / `package fizeau_test` to `package service` / `package service_test` (or the chosen name).
4. Update all imports across the repo (internal/serviceimpl, cmd/fiz, cmd/fizeau, etc.) to reference the new path.
5. Add a thin `service.go` at the root if a public top-level entry point is still needed (or rely on cmd/).
6. Update CONTRACT-003 cross-references that point at root-level types (they're already documented by their type names; just file paths change).

NON-SCOPE
- Don't change the public API surface in CONTRACT-003. Type names and method signatures stay.
- Don't change the test surface — test names + assertions stay.
- Don't reshape the internal/ tree (internal/harnesses, internal/routing, etc. are fine where they are).
- Don't touch internal/serviceimpl until step 5 of the consuming refactor: that package is currently the only place service code is allowed to import per the harness-interface-refactor plan.

DEPS
- Don't start until the harness-interface refactor (plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md) drain completes. The refactor's per-harness migration touches service*.go consumers; doing both in parallel would create constant merge conflicts. Block on the lint rule landing (BEAD-HARNESS-IF-10 / fizeau-1e5bcee1) since that's the final harness-refactor step.
- Coordinate the auto-merge of any other in-flight beads that touch root-level files.

RISK
- Big-diff bead. Every consumer file changes (imports). Tests have to compile and pass against the new path. Recommend a single sweeping PR with `git mv` to preserve blame.
- Use `go build ./...` + `go test ./... -count=1` as the verification command; if either is red, the rewrite isn't done.

description:
PROBLEM
The routing reliability epic requires RouteDecision evidence to name each candidate scored and include the scoreComponents() breakdown that explains why the winner was selected or rejected. The public RouteCandidate currently exposes an aggregate Components struct, but it does not preserve the raw score component map emitted by internal/routing. Operators still cannot audit exact contributions such as base, power, cost, quota_health, deployment_locality, utilization, sticky_affinity, context_headroom, and performance from the route decision payload.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
service.go:575-577 exposes RouteCandidate.Components as a shaped aggregate. service_routing.go:183-196 reads selected ScoreComponents keys to synthesize that aggregate but drops the original routing.Candidate.ScoreComponents map when building the public RouteCandidate at service_routing.go:197-214. service_events.go emits routing decisions from the public RouteDecision, so the raw map is unavailable in operator evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Carry the internal ScoreComponents map through to RouteCandidate and the routing-decision event without creating a second scoring path. Keep the existing Components struct for compatibility, but add a raw map or equivalent named field copied directly from routing.Candidate.ScoreComponents. Add a regression that compares the public map to the internal score components for a candidate.

NON-SCOPE
Do not redesign scoring weights. Do not replace the existing Components aggregate. Do not add new readiness or bead metadata inputs.

description:
PROBLEM
While independently verifying `fizeau-ae932179` on 2026-05-15, the first `go test ./... -count=1` run failed in `internal/modelregistry/TestRefreshStorm`: `expected 1 actual 2` for the assertion "refresh storm should coalesce to one live request". An immediate focused rerun (`go test ./internal/modelregistry -run TestRefreshStorm -count=10`) and a second full `go test ./... -count=1` passed, so this is a timing-sensitive flake in a gate test.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`internal/modelregistry/refresh_storm_test.go:58-85` starts two `Warmup` goroutines and releases the test server after the first `/v1/models` request starts, then asserts only one request occurred. `internal/modelregistry/warmup.go:30-42` maps `RefreshRouting` to `AssembleOptions{Refresh: RefreshForce}`. `internal/modelregistry/discovery.go:151-160` turns `RefreshForce` into `cache.Refresh`, and `internal/discoverycache/singleflight.go:16-20` coalesces only calls that overlap on the same singleflight key. If the second goroutine is not inside `Refresh` before the first request is released, the second `RefreshForce` can legitimately issue a second request after the first completes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decide and encode the intended Warmup contract: either `Warmup` should avoid a second live refresh when another goroutine just refreshed the cache, or the storm test should only assert coalescing for calls known to overlap.
- If the product contract is no immediate redundant warmup refreshes, change the routing warmup path to use stale-aware refresh behavior and add a regression that two near-simultaneous `Warmup` calls produce one request.
- If forced refresh is intentional, rewrite `TestRefreshStorm` with an explicit barrier that proves both callers are waiting on the same in-flight refresh before releasing the server, or narrow the assertion to overlapping refreshes only.
- Keep the test free of sleeps as synchronization when a channel or test hook can express the ordering.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change provider discovery wire formats or model snapshot JSON.
- Do not weaken unrelated modelregistry coverage.
- Do not add DDx/Fizeau routing policy changes.

PARENT / DEPS
No parent. No deps.
title: modelregistry: stabilize refresh storm coalescing test

description:
PROBLEM
The routing_decision event currently omits sibling tiers under a multi-tier subscription harness entirely when they are not chosen — no row, no filter_reason. This is diagnostically harmful: silent absence is indistinguishable from "never enumerated." An excluded tier should always appear in candidates with eligible:false and an explicit filter_reason (e.g., power_missing, surface_mismatch, cost_excluded), the same pattern used today for openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 which surfaces with filter_reason:"power_missing".

ROOT CAUSE (pointers; confirm during fix)
- service_routing.go:1172-1189 candidate add loop — when a candidate is filtered after add(), the row is dropped from the emitted list instead of being marked eligible:false with a reason. The eligibility map keyed by modelID may also lose entries before emit.
- The routing_decision serializer needs to include excluded-candidate rows; verify in the event emitter (likely same package as score_components emitter).

PROPOSED FIX (direction)
Every catalog-known tier under a configured harness must produce either (a) an eligible candidate, or (b) a candidate row with eligible:false and a non-empty filter_reason. No silent drops permitted for catalog-known tiers under configured harnesses.

description:
Third of three sub-beads for parent fizeau-0bc93861. Adds the network-probe half of the openrouter health gate built on the engine plumbing from fizeau-458f10b0 and credential-check from fizeau-73a00a6b. Calls openrouter GET /api/v1/credits with a per-provider in-memory cache (configurable TTL, default 5 minutes) and projects results into ProviderEligibilityOverrides at routing-input build time.

State machine:
- HTTP 200, balance >= threshold → no override (eligible).
- HTTP 200, balance <  threshold (default $0.50) → credit_exhausted; embed observed balance in the candidate Reason text.
- HTTP 401 → credential_invalid (distinct from credential_missing).
- Transport failure / non-2xx other than 401 → provider_unreachable; record probe time; next routing pass eligible for a fresh probe.

Refresh follows the existing local-aliveness pattern (requestLocalHealthRefreshForRouting): synchronous routing pass reads cached state only; staleness triggers an async refresh goroutine. No background ticker.

Configurable: threshold ($0.50 default) and TTL (5m default) plumbed through ServiceOptions or ServiceConfig provider entry. Documented in docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md or the dedicated openrouter section (whichever houses the openrouter provider config docs).

Depends on fizeau-458f10b0 and fizeau-73a00a6b.
title: openrouter health gate: credit-balance probe with TTL cache; surface credit_exhausted, credential_invalid, provider_unreachable

description:
Build a CLI at cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning that snapshots provider
introspection AND verifies whether the upstream actually behaves the way
introspection claims. Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11, the probe is now
primarily a verification tool, not a discovery tool — introspection
adapters (fizeau-e80c156e) handle discovery where it's available.

Three modes of output per (provider, model):

1. **Snapshot** — verbatim capture of every introspection endpoint the
   provider exposes:
     ds4:           /props, /v1/models, /v1/models/<id>
     llama-server:  /props, /v1/models, /slots, /health, /metrics
     OR:            /api/v1/models, /api/v1/models/<id> if supported
     others:        whatever GET <base>/v1/models returns
   Written verbatim into the artifact directory as JSON files. This is
   the auditable record of \"what the server claimed on date X.\"

2. **Verification matrix** — send a small set of seeded chat completions
   that exercise each documented wire knob:
     - reasoning: off
     - reasoning: low (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=2048)
     - reasoning: medium (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=8192)
     - reasoning: high (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=32768)
     - reasoning: 4096 (KindTokens explicit)
     - reasoning: 16384 (KindTokens explicit)
   For each row capture: wire body sent, finish_reason,
   usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens (if present), len of
   message.reasoning_content, wall time. Hash of <think> block content
   when present (so flat-mapping is visible).

   Use a stable single-prompt with seed pinning when the upstream supports
   it (sampling.seed). Prompt: \"What is 17 times 24? Show your work step
   by step.\" Small enough to run fast (~30-60s per request on local
   lanes); large enough to produce measurable thinking.

3. **Verdict** — one of:
     introspection-trusted: claims and observations match (e.g. ds4's
       alias map predicts low/medium/xhigh produce identical thinking
       depth, and the matrix confirms within sampling noise).
     introspection-divergent: claims and observations disagree. Print
       the disagreement and flag for operator review.
     introspection-absent: no useful introspection endpoint; catalog L2
       required. Print recommended `reasoning_wire` value.

CLI surface:
  fizeau-probe-reasoning --profile <path> [--artifact-dir <path>]
                          [--json] [--snapshot-only]
                          [--matrix-only] [--seed <int>]
  fizeau-probe-reasoning --provider <type> --base-url <url> --model <id>
                          [--api-key-env <var>] ...

Default artifact-dir: /tmp/fizeau-probe-<timestamp>/<provider>/

Run timeout per request: 5 minutes (long-thinking Qwen3 / DS4 can take
60s+ on local hardware).

Makefile: add `make probe-reasoning PROFILE=<path>` target.

In-scope files:
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/main.go (new)
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/snapshot.go (new) — introspection endpoint
  capture per provider type.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/matrix.go (new) — verification matrix
  runner.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/verdict.go (new) — claim-vs-observation
  diff logic.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/probe_test.go (new) — table tests against
  recorded fixtures (no live endpoint needed for unit tests).
- Makefile — `probe-reasoning` target.

Out-of-scope:
- Auto-writing back to the catalog (operator reviews and writes
  manually).
- Sweeping multiple models in one invocation (shell loop is fine).
- Real tokenizer for reasoning_content length conversion (use chars/4
  approximation, matching the telemetry bead fizeau-8f62bcbb).

Depends on: fizeau-e80c156e (introspection adapters — probe uses the
same snapshot logic so the two share code).
title: Build fizeau-probe-reasoning CLI for wire-form measurement
