description:
PROBLEM
A timed-out or cancelled TerminalBench Harbor run can leave Fizeau harness subprocesses alive after Harbor reports the cell as failed. In the local incident on 2026-05-16, the OrbStack VM had 610 gemini-matching processes and the process/swap aggregate showed `gemini` holding about 69,659 MiB total (22,929 MiB RSS + 46,730 MiB swap). System memory was 60/62 GiB used, swap was effectively full, and `/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.events` showed `oom_kill 132`. Cleaning up the Gemini/Node processes immediately raised available RAM from about 2 GiB to about 28-36 GiB.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py:347-356` builds a bash `cmd=(/installed-agent/fiz ...)` array and runs it as `"${cmd[@]}" 2>&1 | stdbuf -oL tee /logs/agent/fiz.txt`, then reads `PIPESTATUS[0]`. There is no `trap` for TERM/INT/EXIT and no child-process-tree cleanup. If Harbor cancels the Docker exec shell on AgentTimeout, the shell can die while `fiz` or its wrapped subprocess harness (`gemini`, `claude`, `codex`, etc.) remains orphaned.
- `agentcli/run.go:339-341` creates the execution context with `signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)` only. A SIGTERM from Harbor/runner cancellation is not converted into service context cancellation, so subprocess harness cleanup such as `internal/harnesses/gemini/runner.go:274-287` may not run before the `fiz` process exits.
- `cmd/bench/matrix.go:1097-1104` sends SIGTERM to the Harbor process and gives it 60s `WaitDelay`, but that only assumes Harbor and Docker Compose finalizers clean up the trial. It does not verify or enforce cleanup of the nested `fiz -> harness CLI` process tree that the FizeauAgent shell wrapper starts inside the task container.

PROPOSED FIX
Make cancellation cleanup explicit at both levels:
1. Teach the `fiz` CLI to treat SIGTERM like os.Interrupt for run cancellation on Unix, so the service context is cancelled and harness runners can kill their subprocess groups before the process exits.
2. Rewrite the FizeauAgent generated bash in `scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py` so it wraps the `fiz` invocation with TERM/INT/EXIT cleanup. Preserve tee logging and the `fiz_rc` status, but keep the child PID, terminate the child process tree on cancellation, wait a short grace interval, then SIGKILL leftovers. Avoid a bare foreground pipeline where killing the shell loses the child PID.
3. Add or update benchmark-runner cancellation tests so parent cancellation exercises the cleanup path and the generated command structure cannot regress to an untrapped pipeline.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `agentcli/run.go`
- `agentcli/cli_process_test.go`
- `scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py`
- `scripts/benchmark/test_harbor_agent.py`
- `cmd/bench/matrix.go` and `cmd/bench/matrix_test.go` only if runner-level process-group cancellation must change to make the cleanup complete.

NON-SCOPE
- Rewriting the benchmark runner beyond cancellation/cleanup behavior.
- Changing TerminalBench task Docker images.
- Changing Gemini/Codex/Claude harness routing or model selection.
- Bulk deleting existing leaked processes; this bead is for preventing recurrence.

NOTES FOR IMPLEMENTER
The existing `TestCLI_CancelSignal_WritesSessionEndEvent` covers os.Interrupt. Add a SIGTERM equivalent rather than replacing that test. The Python command-builder tests already mock `exec_as_agent`, so they are the right place to assert the generated bash contains the trap/cleanup structure without needing a live Harbor run.
title: bench: kill fiz harness descendants on Harbor timeout

description:
PROBLEM
internal/routing/score.go:60 and :108 wrap the policy-aware scoring (cheap/default/smart preferences for local/subscription/cost) and the provider-preference bias in `if !hasPowerBounds`. When a caller sets --min-power N or --max-power M, those branches are skipped entirely. Result: among candidates that all satisfy the bounds, the routing falls through to raw base+quota+power-9-bonus scoring, which systematically prefers higher-power-metered models over equivalent-quality subscription-free models.

Observed today: ddx try --harness claude --min-power 8 routed to claude/opus (power 10, score ~76, metered ~$0.045/turn) instead of claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 (power 8, score 186 per fiz route-status without bounds, subscription/free). Across the 11 harness-interface refactor bead executions, this cost real opus tokens for work sonnet would have handled fine.

ROOT CAUSE
- score.go:52: hasPowerBounds := cand.MinPower > 0 || cand.MaxPower > 0
- score.go:60: `if !hasPowerBounds { switch policy ... }` — the entire policy preference block (deployment locality, quota health, cost-class ranking) is gated off
- score.go:108: `if !hasPowerBounds { switch cand.ProviderPreference ... }` — provider-preference bias gated off

The intent was probably: 'when user expresses explicit power bounds, just enforce bounds and ignore default biases.' But power bounds are orthogonal to policy preference — a user asking for power>=8 still wants the cheaper, subscription-backed candidate among the eligible set.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove the `if !hasPowerBounds` guards at score.go:60 and score.go:108. Apply policy preference and provider-preference bias unconditionally to in-bounds candidates. Keep the below-MinPower / above-MaxPower penalties as they are (lines 227-238) — those correctly demote out-of-bounds candidates without disabling policy scoring for in-bounds ones.

The natural-power bonus at line 222 (`Power * 12` when no bounds set) can stay gated — that's a different signal: 'when no bounds expressed, treat higher power as a quality proxy.' When bounds ARE expressed, the bounds themselves encode the power preference; double-counting via natural-power-bonus would be wrong.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the below-MinPower / above-MaxPower penalty values (lines 227-238).
- Do not change the natural-power bonus gating at line 222.
- Do not change quota-pressure, cooldown, sticky-affinity, or perf scoring.
- Do not introduce new score components.
title: routing: policy-aware scoring is skipped when MinPower/MaxPower set, preferring expensive opus over free sonnet

description:
Fix the bench-set 'tasks: all' misdesign. Bench-sets reference task lists explicitly
via tasks_from: and name their task-executor explicitly via task_executor:.

CONTEXT: today scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/tb-2-1-all.yaml declares 'tasks: all'.
cmd/bench/plan.go:214 rejects this as 'tasks: all requires harness-side enumeration
not yet supported by plan helper'. The misframe is that the harness should enumerate
tasks — task lists are repo data. The full task catalog already lives at
scripts/benchmark/task-subset-tb21-all.yaml (89 entries). PR C makes the bench-set
reference the file explicitly.

DEPENDS ON: PR A (runner needs to consume the new fields).

SCOPE:
  - Move scripts/benchmark/task-subset-tb21-{all,canary,full,or-passing,openai-cheap,
    timing-baseline}.yaml to scripts/benchmark/task-subsets/terminal-bench-2-1-
    {all,canary,full,or-passing,openai-cheap,timing-baseline}.yaml. (Use git mv to
    preserve history. New canonical directory: scripts/benchmark/task-subsets/)
  - Update each scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/*.yaml to add:
      task_executor: harbor                                 # all current bench-sets use harbor
      tasks_from: ../task-subsets/terminal-bench-2-1-<X>.yaml  # for the 'all' variant
                                                                # OR inline tasks: [...]
                                                                # for canary which is small
      description: \"<one-line summary>\"                    # for ./benchmark bench-sets listing
    Delete the 'tasks: all' keyword everywhere.
  - Modify scripts/benchmark/benchmark (runner from PR A):
      - Resolve task_executor: <name> by looking up scripts/benchmark/task-executors/<name>
        (must exist and be executable; error otherwise).
      - Drop the PR A framework-based default ('framework: terminal-bench' -> harbor).
        Bench-set explicitly names its executor now.
      - Resolve tasks_from: <relative-path> by reading the YAML file at the path
        (resolved relative to the bench-set's directory) and using its 'tasks:' list.
        Inline 'tasks:' still works.
  - Extend ./benchmark validate to:
      - Reject bench-sets whose task_executor doesn't resolve to an executable file
        in scripts/benchmark/task-executors/.
      - Reject bench-sets whose tasks_from path doesn't exist or doesn't parse.
      - Reject bench-sets that still use 'tasks: all' (with a clear error pointing at
        this PR's documentation).
  - Extend ./benchmark bench-sets to print description: text per bench-set.

IN-SCOPE files:
  - scripts/benchmark/task-subset-tb21-*.yaml (rename to task-subsets/ subdir)
  - scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/*.yaml (edit each — add task_executor,
    tasks_from or inline tasks, description; delete 'tasks: all')
  - scripts/benchmark/benchmark (edit — task-executor resolution, tasks_from
    resolution, validate updates, bench-sets listing format)

OUT OF SCOPE:
  - Adding new bench-sets or new task-subsets. This PR only restructures existing ones.
  - The per-cell schema (PR B).
  - The collector (PR D).

ROLLBACK: rename moves are reversible via git mv. Bench-set YAML edits are localized.

ACCEPTANCE — all pass on a clean checkout:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --plan --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-all
     resolves all 89 tasks and prints them (one line per (profile, task, rep)).
  2. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark validate succeeds.
  3. Create a synthetic bench-set with task_executor: nonexistent; ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark
     validate rejects it with a clear error mentioning task-executors/.
  4. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark bench-sets prints one line per bench-set with the
     description: text after the id.
  5. grep -rn 'tasks: all' scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/ returns nothing.
  6. ls scripts/benchmark/task-subsets/terminal-bench-2-1-all.yaml exists; the old
     scripts/benchmark/task-subset-tb21-all.yaml does not.
title: bench-pr-C: bench-set tasks_from: + task_executor: fields (replace tasks: all)

description:
PROBLEM
GitHub CI run 25932885597 for master commit 83583bd6 passed build, vet, lint, security scan, vulnerability scan, format, rename noise, normal go test, website smoke, and adapter pytest, but failed in Test (race). The failing package was internal/harnesses/codex.

OBSERVED FAILURE
make test-race runs CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race -count=1 ./... . internal/harnesses/codex.TestParseCodexStream_CommandExecutionDuration failed with: tool_result duration = 17ms, want >=20ms.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
The test sleeps for exactly 20ms and asserts DurationMS >= 20. Under race instrumentation and scheduler/timer behavior, the measured wall duration can round/truncate below 20ms even though the parser behavior is correct. This is a test determinism issue, not evidence of a routing or safefs regression.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/harnesses/codex/runner_test.go

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- No production parser behavior changes unless required by the test investigation.
- No CI workflow broadening or check disabling.
- Do not rerun CI until it happens to pass; make the assertion robust.

EXPECTED FIX
Make TestParseCodexStream_CommandExecutionDuration deterministic under race instrumentation while still proving command execution durations are captured as positive elapsed time and associated with the emitted tool result.
notes: Codex race flake fixed by a6a34bdd. GitHub CI run 25933751955 confirmed internal/harnesses/codex passed under make test-race; the remaining CI failure is a separate Gemini PTY quota capture flake tracked as fizeau-c3c50ee2.
title: ci: make codex stream duration test race-stable

description:
PROBLEM
Discovery cache refreshers can ignore `Source.RefreshDeadline` because the cache calls the refresher with `context.Background()`. A hung provider discovery or runtime signal collector can therefore block a refresh beyond the source deadline instead of timing out through the normal marker/deadline contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `internal/discoverycache/cache.go:254-258` runs slow refresh IO outside the cache lock, but passes an unbounded background context into the refresher.
- `internal/discoverycache/cache_test.go` lacked a regression proving `Cache.Refresh` gives the refresher a context that is cancelled at `Source.RefreshDeadline`.

PROPOSED FIX
Wrap the refresher context with `context.WithTimeout` when `Source.RefreshDeadline` is positive, defer cancellation, and pass that bounded context to the refresher. Add a focused test that waits on `ctx.Done()` and expects `context.DeadlineExceeded` within the source deadline.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- internal/discoverycache/cache.go
- internal/discoverycache/cache_test.go

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change marker-file layout or cache lock semantics.
- Do not change model registry or runtime signal collection logic.
- Do not change route scoring policy.
title: discoverycache: pass refresh deadline context to refreshers

acceptance:
1. go test ./internal/modelregistry/... ./internal/runtimesignals/... ./agentcli/... -run 'Refresh|SingleFlight|Coalesc|Stale|Warm|Heartbeat' -count=1 passes.
2. A test proves repeated stale refresh requests create at most one in-flight refresh per provider/field key.
3. A test proves refresh failure records refresh_failed or stale freshness state without immediately retrying in a loop.
4. A test proves successful refresh atomically advances snapshot version or captured_at metadata.
5. A public sync refresh/warmup entrypoint exists for long-running callers, and tests show a caller can invoke it periodically without bypassing locks.
6. rg -n 'single.?flight|coalesc|refresh_failed|refresh_all|RefreshAll|RefreshRouting|Warm' internal agentcli service*.go returns production coverage.
description:
Implement or tighten Fizeau's synchronous refresh coordinator so stale health/quota/model facts are refreshed through one lock-coordinated path instead of each CLI process launching its own probes.

Desired behavior:
- Fizeau has no required daemon; its contract is synchronous refresh plus cross-process locks, single-flight/coalescing, TTLs, cooldowns, bounded concurrency, and atomic snapshot writes.
- Repeated refresh requests for the same provider/field class coalesce into one in-flight operation per TTL/cooldown window.
- Refreshes are bounded, concurrent, and per-provider rate/cooldown aware.
- Routing-relevant refresh covers health, quota, model availability/discovery, context/tools/reasoning support, billing/effective cost metadata where dynamic, and utilization if available.
- Refresh results update the fiz models snapshot atomically with version/timestamp/freshness metadata.
- Refresh failure records refresh_failed/stale state for scoring and diagnostics; it must not create an unbounded retry loop.
- Long-running callers such as a DDx server can maintain async freshness by invoking this synchronous API from their own heartbeat; route correctness must not depend on that integration.

In scope: cache/refresh coordinator internals, process lock/single-flight logic, public refresh/warmup entrypoint for long-running callers, focused tests using fake refreshers.

Out of scope: CLI flag UX; route scoring; changing DDx upstream server behavior if that lives outside this repo.
title: Add refresh coordinator semantics without CLI refresh storms

   configured providers returns a snapshot listing each (provider,
   model) pair with parsed Family/Version/Tier and catalog-derived

3. `fiz models <provider>/<id>` returns detail view of that model.
4. Cache is consulted on `fiz models` (verified by absence of

description:
EPIC for the available-models snapshot architecture. The user-facing
deliverable is a `fiz models` CLI subcommand. The architectural
deliverable is the foundation for the auto-router: a runtime-resolved
view of "what can fizeau actually use right now," assembled from all
discovery sources, enriched with catalog metadata, surfaced through a
multi-process-safe disk cache.

## End goal

For a given fizeau install at a given time, there is a canonical set
of known models — one entry per (provider, model_id) — with defined
properties: identity, parsed family/version/tier, catalog-derived
power/cost/reasoning_levels/quota_pool, and runtime-derived status/
quota_remaining/recent latency. Operators introspect via `fiz models`;
routing reads the same snapshot to score candidates. Single source of
truth; no separate "what does routing think exists" vs "what does
operator see" divergence.

## Child beads (in dependency order)

- **M0** (ADR-012): per-source on-disk cache for discovery + runtime
  signals. Defines file layout, locking semantics, single-flight
  refresh algorithm, atomic-write contract, test matrix.
- **M1**: cache infrastructure — implements ADR-012's algorithm with
  the 10-test robustness matrix. Includes `ParseModelRef`/canonical
  identity helpers.
- **M2**: snapshot assembler — enumerate providers (incl harnesses-
  as-providers) → discover via cache → parse via tier-ranker
  (depends on routing bead A) → enrich from catalog (joins on
  power, cost, reasoning_levels, quota_pool from bead E1).
- **M3**: runtime signal collector — status, quota_remaining, recent
  p50 latency → runtime cache.
- **M4**: `fiz models` top-level CLI subcommand. Default filtered
  view; `--include-noise --refresh --no-refresh --json --provider X
  --power-min/max`. Detail view via `fiz models <ref>` with shortform
  fuzzy resolution and full-canonical exact match.
- **M5** (deferred): OR sub-provider expansion via `@<sub_provider>`
  syntax. Filed proposed-status; future work.

## Architecture invariants

1. **Identity = (provider, model_id).** Canonical form `<provider>/
   <model_id>`. Provider names slug-safe; model IDs preserved
   provider-side. Split-on-first-slash.
2. **Harness == provider** for snapshot purposes. Subscription harness
   wrappers participate as named providers (e.g.,
   `claude-subscription`, `codex-subscription`).
3. **Catalog is implementation detail.** No catalog-only entries
   appear in `fiz models`. If no provider serves a model, it's not
   "known."
4. **No aliases in snapshot.** Aliases are a search/discovery
   concern (CLI-side fuzzy resolution); snapshot stores canonical
   entries only.
5. **Per-source on-disk cache**, multi-process-safe, with strict
   100ms UI budget. Discovery cache long-TTL; runtime cache short-TTL.
6. **Single-flight refresh** across processes via PID+marker pattern
   (extending fizeau's existing `acquireMatrixLock` idiom).
7. **Stale-while-revalidate** by default. Force-refresh waits.
8. **Atomic write** via tmp+fsync+rename. No torn reads.

## Governing doc updates

- **NEW: ADR-012** — per-source disk cache for discovery + runtime
- **FEAT-006** (standalone CLI) — add `fiz models` subcommand
- **FEAT-004** (model routing) — addendum: routing reads snapshot
- **ADR-009** (routing surface redesign) — addendum: harness-as-
  provider in snapshot

## Closes when

All five active children (M0, M1, M2, M3, M4) closed. M5 is filed
proposed-status as future work and does not block this epic.
notes: Review 2026-05-12: all active children are closed and the unified snapshot contract now holds across modelregistry, routing, agentcli, and root facade checks. Verified with: go test ./internal/modelregistry/... ./internal/routing/... ./agentcli/... . -run 'TestAutoRoutingUsesUnifiedModelSnapshot|TestExecuteRouteEvidenceMatchesModelsSnapshot|TestRouteStatusMatchesModelsSnapshot|TestModels.*Snapshot|TestModelsConcurrentRefreshSingleFlight|TestResolveRoute|TestRouteStatus|Score|Power|Preference|Context|Sticky|FilterReason' -count=1. Acceptance satisfied; closing against implementation head 18cf091a63f636beb2aaec0f2fa6a2b85d999f90.
title: fiz models — assembled snapshot per (provider, model) for routing + introspection

description:
PROBLEM
The matrix-side harness adapters live in Python under scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/ and are invoked in-process by the Go runner. To drop host Python they must become shell scripts the bash runner can exec.

ROOT CAUSE
scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/*.py is imported via the PYTHONPATH injection at cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105. Each adapter reads a profile.json on stdin and emits a command-spec.json on stdout that the runner feeds to a task-executor.

PROPOSED FIX
Create scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/<name> shell scripts (one per existing Python adapter). Each script:
  - Reads profile.json from stdin.
  - Uses `jq` to translate fields into a command-spec.json on stdout.
  - Exits non-zero with a diagnostic on malformed input.
Keep behavior bit-identical to the Python originals for the fixtures in scripts/benchmark/testdata/ (or add fixtures if absent). Leave the Python files in place this PR — sibling A.4 wires the bash runner to call these and a later PR deletes the .py originals.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove Python harness_adapters in this child.
- Do not modify Harbor-side adapters (those move into the Docker image in A.3).
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. For each Python adapter under scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/, a matching shell script exists at scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/<name> and is executable.
2. TestHarnessAdaptersShellParity feeds each fixture profile.json to both the Python and shell adapter and asserts JSON-equivalent command-spec.json output.
3. TestHarnessAdaptersMalformedInput verifies non-zero exit + stderr diagnostic on bad input.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: bench-pr-A.2: shell harness-adapters ported from Python

description:
P1 refinement on top of the dialect-fix bead fizeau-073fe18b.
Introspection adapters read provider /props at construction time and
override the static ThinkingFormat default with what the live server
actually claims.

Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11 the L1 layer (live introspection) is the
authoritative source where available; this bead implements L1 for ds4 and
llama-server. The static defaults that fizeau-073fe18b sets become the L3
floor; L2 (catalog reasoning_wire) sits in between.

After this bead lands, fizeau auto-detects:
- ds4's alias map → effective reasoning_levels without catalog curation
- llama-server's chat_template variant → auto-detect kwargs vs top-level
  envelope (today's wire choice is hardcoded by fizeau-073fe18b)
- server-side reasoning_format default → can override per-request only
  when needed

Cost model (measured 2026-05-11):
  ds4 /props on LAN:         p50=13ms  max=51ms  (n=10)
  sindri /props on LAN:      p50=9ms   max=13ms  (n=10)
  OR /api/v1/models cloud:   p50=316ms max=325ms (n=5)

Three adapters in this bead:

1. **ds4 adapter** (internal/provider/ds4/introspection.go, new):
   GET http://<base>/props. Parse reasoning.{supported_efforts, aliases,
   default, effective_default, think_max_min_context},
   api.supported_request_parameters, sampling, runtime. Derive effective
   ThinkingFormat=OpenAIEffort and effective reasoning_levels from
   supported_efforts minus aliased duplicates.

2. **llama-server adapter** (internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection.go,
   new): GET http://<base>/props. Parse
   default_generation_settings.params.reasoning_format, chat_template
   (substring match for `enable_thinking` to detect kwargs envelope),
   chat_template_caps.supports_preserve_reasoning, build_info.

3. **Common plumbing** (internal/provider/registry/introspection.go, new):
   ProviderIntrospection struct, registry of adapters by provider type,
   cache by (base_url, model), non-fatal failure with structured warning.

Construction order: instantiate provider → run introspection adapter (if
registered) → merge into capabilities → ready. Per-request path is
unchanged; no introspection IO at request time.

In-scope files:
- internal/provider/ds4/introspection.go (new)
- internal/provider/ds4/introspection_test.go (new)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection.go (new)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection_test.go (new)
- internal/provider/registry/introspection.go (new)
- internal/provider/registry/registry.go (wire adapter invocation)
- internal/provider/ds4/ds4.go (consume introspection)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/llamaserver.go (consume introspection)
- internal/provider/ds4/testdata/props/ (recorded fixtures)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/testdata/props/ (recorded fixtures)

Out-of-scope (separate beads):
- OR adapter (introspection thin; catalog L2 covers it).
- Anthropic-native / OpenAI-native adapters.
- vLLM adapter (defer until lane re-added).

Depends on: fizeau-073fe18b (wire-format dialect fixes — adapters set
the EFFECTIVE ThinkingFormat to the new variants).
title: Provider introspection adapters: read /props at construction and merge into ProtocolCapabilities

acceptance: 1. New fixture testdata/matrix-invalid/no-llm-response.json + test case asserts classification as invalid_provider. 2. scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run on existing history flags the 54+ known no-response cells transitioning from '' to 'invalid_provider'. 3. Apply pass leaves those cells tagged and excluded from pass@k denominator. 4. A subsequent sweep with --retry-invalid re-runs them.
description:
Currently when fiz makes an llm.request and gets no llm.response back (cloud-side hang, 5xx, stream-aborted, rate-limit drop, etc.), the cell ends up as a normal graded_fail with reward=0, turns=0, terminated_mid_work=None. The matrix runner treats it identically to a real model failure — even though no actual model attempt happened. Result: 54 of OR-Qwen's 239 "failures" (22%) on TB-2.1 are this class, polluting the pass-rate denominator and wasting the cell entirely.

Two complementary fixes:

A. **Matrix-side classifier rule** (low-effort, high-impact):
   In cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go, add: if final_status="graded_fail" AND
   (turns is nil OR turns==0) AND terminated_mid_work is nil AND (input_tokens
   is nil OR input_tokens==0), classify as `invalid_provider`. The existing
   `--retry-invalid` flag will then re-run these on the next sweep pass.
   Mirrors the same structural-rule pattern as the recent harness_crash →
   invalid_setup and ran-no-attempt → invalid_setup additions.

B. **In-fiz request-level retry** (deeper, may belong in a separate bead):
   When fiz issues an llm.request and gets no streamed tokens within
   provider_timeout, retry with exponential backoff (e.g. 1s, 4s, 16s)
   up to 3 attempts before failing the turn. Should be capped by total
   wall budget. This stops the request from ever reaching "no llm.response"
   state in the first place. Requires care to avoid amplifying real
   server-side stalls into runaway retries.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With a stub provider that returns HTTP 200 with empty body (simulating
   the no-response state), `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestClassifyMatrixInvalid`
   has a new fixture report (`no-llm-response.json`) with the structural
   shape above, asserted to classify as `invalid_provider`.
2. `scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run` on the existing
   3700+ historical reports flags the 54 known OR-Qwen no-response cells
   plus any other lanes' equivalents as transitioning from `""` to
   `invalid_provider`. After apply, those cells are excluded from the
   pass@k denominator and tagged for `--retry-invalid` reruns.
3. (Optional, defer to Part B bead) `internal/core/loop_test.go` covers
   the retry-on-empty-response behavior with a stub provider that yields
   nothing on the first 2 attempts and a normal response on the 3rd.

In-scope files for Part A (this bead):
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — add the classifier branch
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid_test.go — add fixture test
- cmd/bench/testdata/matrix-invalid/no-llm-response.json — new fixture
- scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py — mirror the Go logic
- scripts/benchmark/backfill-terminated-mid-work.py (already handles
  this case via the structural rule but not the classifier tag)

Out-of-scope:
- Part B (in-fiz request retry) — file as a separate bead if/when needed.
- Distinguishing "5xx" vs "rate-limit" vs "timeout" — at the report.json
  level we just see "no llm.response"; downstream telemetry could refine
  but this bead just gets us to "retriable cell".
- Adjusting harbor's own retry behavior.

Why this matters for the QWEN_BASE experiment:
The OR-Qwen 2.5x rerun experiment (in flight as of 2026-05-11 15:29Z) will

with "no llm.response", we expect roughly the same fraction to hit the
same class on the rerun. This bead would catch them with `--retry-invalid`

acceptance:
1. New exported method on the catalog cache: `RecordDispatchError(endpoint string, err error)` (or equivalent name). Called from every chat-completions code path after a failed dispatch. Classifies err using the same isNetworkFailure/isServerError predicates as probeOpenAIModels; on classification as ReachabilityError-equivalent, sets `e.UnreachableAt = now` and `e.LastErr = err`.
2. TestCatalogCacheRecordsDispatchFailureAsUnreachable — given a successful probe at T0 followed by a chat-completions dispatch failure at T1 with `dial tcp ... i/o timeout`, the cache entry has `UnreachableAt = T1` after the dispatch returns. Verified by direct cache-state inspection.
3. TestRoutePicksAlternateAfterDispatchFailureFeedback — given two equally-scored endpoints A and B, both initially `available` in cache. First dispatch picks A (sticky lease acquired) and fails with i/o timeout. Second dispatch in same service instance: routing_decision shows A as `eligible: false, filter_reason: "endpoint_unreachable"`, and B is selected.
4. TestNoSilentRecoveryFromUnreachable — after dispatch-failure marks an endpoint unreachable, the next routing pass within UnreachableCooldown does NOT re-select it even if the cache's stale entry is otherwise within StaleTTL.
5. FreshTTL for `deployment_class: local` providers is ≤ 15s by default (configurable). Verified by a unit test that constructs a local-class catalog entry and asserts the resolved FreshTTL.
6. Existing probe-driven path remains unchanged: probe failures continue to set UnreachableAt as today; probe successes still clear it on next probe.
7. No regression in service_routing tests, service_probe tests, or service_adr005_test.go.

description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau routes to local LMStudio/MLX endpoints that are demonstrably dead at dispatch time, burning 5-second i/o timeouts per dispatch and pinning sticky leases to dead hosts. Symptom from this morning: bragi-club-3090 / vidar / grendel all timed out on direct TCP probes at the time of investigation, yet Fizeau routed to bragi-club-3090 with `source_status: "available"` and `eligible: true` in the routing_decision event.

ROOT CAUSE
The catalog cache (service_catalog_cache.go) treats GET /v1/models probe failures as the SOLE signal source for the UnreachableAt timestamp. Chat-completions dispatch failures (POST /v1/chat/completions returning `dial tcp ...: i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, etc.) are NOT fed back into the cache. The next routing pass within FreshTTL (60s) re-reads the same stale "available" entry and re-selects the dead endpoint.

Concrete code path:
- service_catalog_cache.go:80 — `UnreachableAt time.Time // zero unless last attempt produced a ReachabilityError`
- service_catalog_cache.go:295-307 — the ONLY writes to UnreachableAt are inside `probe()`, set when probeFn returns `*openai.ReachabilityError`.
- service_probe.go:164-184 — probeOpenAIModels is the probeFn; it classifies GET /v1/models failures into ReachabilityError. Chat-completions failures never reach this function.
- Dispatch code in service_execute.go / the harness HTTP path returns transport errors directly to the caller. No code path calls `cache.MarkUnreachable(endpoint)` (no such API exists) or otherwise touches `e.UnreachableAt` from the dispatch flow.

Compound the cache behavior with two further amplifiers:
1. **Per-CLI-invocation cache isolation.** Each `ddx work` (and each `ddx run`) spawns its own fizeau service in-process; the catalog cache is local heap. ddx-on-project-A's worker may know bragi is unreachable; ddx-on-project-B's worker has an empty cache and probes bragi successfully once (at startup), then trusts that result for FreshTTL (60s) + StaleTTL (10min stale-while-revalidate).
2. **Stale-while-revalidate widens the false-positive window.** service_catalog_cache.go:240 returns the stale-but-cached "available" entry and kicks an async refresh. Synchronous callers receive `available` without waiting for the refresh result.

EVIDENCE FROM DOWNSTREAM DDX
Routing decision for the failed dispatch (.ddx/executions/20260516T174611-788fbe14/embedded/svc-*.jsonl):

  candidate: bragi-club-3090
  source_status: "available"
  eligible: true
  score: 35
  model_discovery_freshness_at: 2026-05-16T17:46:14.952Z

Session.end event 7 seconds later:

  status: error
  error: "agent: provider error: openai: Post \"http://bragi:8020/v1/chat/completions\": dial tcp 100.127.38.115:8020: i/o timeout"
  duration_ms: 5027

Direct TCP test from the same host at the time of investigation: `timeout 5 bash -c 'echo > /dev/tcp/bragi/8020'` failed. Same result on grendel:* and vidar:* (all dead). Yet fizeau's auto-route enumerated bragi/grendel/vidar as `source_status: "available"` because the catalog cache had not been told they were unreachable — only chat-completions had observed the timeout, and the chat-completions path does not write back to the cache.

A separate ddx routing-decision from 24 minutes earlier (different project, same hosts, different fizeau service instance) DID correctly mark bragi-club-3090 as `eligible: false, filter_reason: "endpoint_unreachable"` — but that instance had freshly probed and the probe had failed. The per-instance cache isolation means the "dead" knowledge does not propagate to other service instances.

PROPOSED FIX

1. **Add a dispatch-feedback hook on the catalog cache.** Add a method `cache.RecordDispatchError(endpoint, err error)` (or similar). The dispatch code path in service_execute.go (and any other place where chat-completions is invoked through an OpenAI-compatible adapter) calls this after a failed request. RecordDispatchError classifies err via the same `isNetworkFailure` / `isServerError` predicates that service_probe.go:188-223 already uses; if classified as ReachabilityError-equivalent, set `e.UnreachableAt = time.Now()` and `e.LastErr = err`. Honor the same UnreachableCooldown semantics so the next route pass excludes the endpoint.

2. **Tighten FreshTTL for local subprocess endpoints.** 60s is reasonable for stable cloud APIs but too lenient for laptop-class local servers that suspend/resume (LMStudio on a sleeping MacBook). Either make FreshTTL configurable per provider class, or default it to 10s for `deployment_class: local` and keep 60s for managed_cloud_*.

3. **Make UnreachableCooldown start counting from MOST RECENT failure, not first.** Today UnreachableAt is set once on probe failure and cleared on next success. If dispatch failures repeatedly recur but no probe runs in between, UnreachableAt may be stale. (3) is dependent on (1); without (1), only probes write UnreachableAt so this is moot.

4. **(Optional, larger)** Persist UnreachableAt across service instances via a small on-disk cache file (`~/.cache/fizeau/route-health.json` or similar) with TTL. So `ddx work` invocations across projects share aliveness knowledge, eliminating the per-process false-positive window. NON-SCOPE for this bead; track as follow-up if (1)+(2) prove insufficient.

NON-SCOPE
- Sticky lease vs aliveness interaction (separate bead worth filing; today's evidence is that sticky DID NOT override aliveness in this case — the bug is upstream of sticky in the cache).
- DDX-side changes; ddx passes policy=default verbatim per CONTRACT-003. Fix is entirely Fizeau-side.
- Building the persistent cross-process cache (option 4 above).

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-80d4bf41 (auto-route candidate enumeration collapses multi-tier subscription harnesses to DefaultModel only) — same symptom surface, different root cause.
- fizeau-0bc93861 (openrouter: drop from candidate pool when credential or credit is missing) — same pattern (provider health gating), different provider class.
- Github issue mirror: file as a follow-up to https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6 once this is filed.
title: catalog-cache: dispatch failures must invalidate UnreachableAt; today only probes do

The official medium/frontier TerminalBench matrix must stop invoking raw Harbor Claude, Codex, pi, or opencode adapters and must use one Harbor installed agent, scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py:FizeauAgent. Profiles select execution lanes by passing explicit fiz hard pins such as FIZEAU_HARNESS=claude, FIZEAU_HARNESS=codex, FIZEAU_HARNESS=pi, FIZEAU_HARNESS=opencode, or FIZEAU_PROVIDER=openrouter. Child beads should land the CLI harness pin, FizeauAgent env pass-through for all fiz-wrapped harnesses, profile/script rewrite, invalid-run classification, docs/tests, and final canary validation.

description:
A dead local OpenAI-compatible provider (example: grendel rapid-mlx at http://grendel:8000/v1) can still delay or poison unpinned route selection before the routing engine chooses a healthy subscription harness. The current shared service path calls buildRoutingInputsWithCatalog(..., RefreshIfStale), which synchronously calls refreshLocalHealthForRouting and synchronous model snapshot refresh before scoring. This affects ResolveRoute directly, Execute via resolveExecuteRouteWithEngine, route-status, and override telemetry's stripped ResolveRoute call.

Required contract: local/fixed provider liveness and model discovery must be cached signals on the route hot path. Stale or missing local-provider facts may request a coordinated asynchronous refresh, but they must not block unpinned autorouting or explicit non-local harness selection. Provider contact failures must be recorded as route evidence/cooldown when observed, but failure to contact a local provider must not prevent selection of another eligible harness/provider. If every candidate is unavailable, routing must still fail with a typed no-viable-provider style error rather than hanging or aborting the process.

In-scope files:
- service_routing.go
- service_aliveness.go
- service_execute.go
- service_override.go only if context propagation requires it
- internal/modelsnapshot/types.go and internal/modelregistry/assemble.go comments if the freshness contract text is stale
- service_aliveness_test.go and service_routing_test.go for regression coverage
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md and/or docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-012-per-source-on-disk-cache.md to align the spec with the nonblocking route hot path
- CHANGELOG.md

Out-of-scope:
- DDx retry policy changes
- provider config schema changes
- changing explicit provider pins to silently fall back to a different provider
- requiring a DDx daemon for correctness
title: Make route-time local liveness nonblocking for autorouting

description:
PROBLEM
Final release-candidate CI passed gosec, website-smoke, and rename-noise, then failed in `make test-no-race`. One reproducible local failure is `internal/modelregistry/TestAssembleFixtureIncludesDiscoveredProviderModels`: `TempDir RemoveAll cleanup: unlinkat .../discovery: directory not empty`.

ROOT CAUSE
The fixture-oriented modelregistry assemble tests call `Assemble`, whose default is `RefreshBackground`. For providers that also have props/endpoint discovery sources without test fixtures, Assemble can start background refresh work under the test temp cache. The test can finish while that goroutine is still writing/removing marker files, racing `t.TempDir` cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Use `AssembleWithOptions(... RefreshNone)` in fixture-only assemble tests that are asserting preloaded cache data, preserving explicit background-refresh coverage in the dedicated refresh coordinator tests.
title: ci: stabilize modelregistry fixture assembly cleanup

acceptance:
1. TestServiceStartup_ProbesConfiguredProviders asserts that Service.New() runs one TCP-connect probe per configured non-cloud provider within startup-bounded time (e.g. 5s total cap regardless of provider count).
2. TestProbeLoop_RetriesDeadProvidersOnInterval asserts that a provider marked unreachable by probe is re-probed every HealthProbeInterval and recorded back to reachable when it comes online.
3. TestRouter_FiltersUnreachableEndpointsWhenAlternatesExist asserts that when bragi:1234 is recorded unreachable AND claude/sonnet is a valid candidate, the router emits exactly one routing decision selecting claude/sonnet without first dialing bragi.
4. TestRouter_AcceptsUnreachableEndpointWhenSoleCandidate asserts that when the only configured candidate is the unreachable one (explicit operator pin), the router still attempts the request and surfaces the dial failure honestly.
5. TestRouteHealth_PersistsProbeResults — paired with fizeau-7cd66e6b's persistence story, asserts a fresh process reads the prior probe results within HealthSignalTTL.
6. RouteDecision evidence includes per-candidate {last_probe_at, last_probe_success} fields where applicable.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes the aliveness probing + new ServiceOptions knobs (HealthProbeInterval, HealthSignalTTL).
8. go test ./internal/routehealth/... ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau has no proactive aliveness check for configured providers. A dead local provider (e.g. bragi:1234, grendel:1234) is only learned to be dead by attempting a request against it and eating the dial timeout. The failure feedback is stored in internal/routehealth.Store, which is:
1. Process-local (sync.RWMutex map; not persisted to disk).
2. Short-TTL (DefaultCooldown = 30s; entries expire and are re-tried).

Result for an operator with bragi unplugged but configured:
- Every fresh `ddx work` or `ddx try` process re-probes bragi on its first routing decision, eating a 5s dial timeout (post fizeau-b5757624) or 30s pre-fix.
- Even within a long-running `ddx work --watch`, the route exclusion expires every 30s and bragi gets re-attempted on the next cycle.
- Aggregate cost over a 27-bead drain on 2026-05-14: minutes of dial timeouts and routing thrash, leaking into operator-visible queue stalls.

A quota-recovery probe loop exists at service_probe.go for providers marked `quota_exhausted` (periodic, with retry-after backoff). It does NOT cover `provider_connectivity` failures — those have no proactive recovery path.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/routehealth/store.go owns failure tracking; it is in-memory only, with no persistence interface (verified by file listing: only store.go / lease_store.go / utilization_store.go — no persist.go).
- service_probe.go's QuotaRecoveryProber only handles ProviderQuotaStateStore — not RouteHealth failures.
- No service-startup probe for configured providers. Providers in fizeau config are trusted to be reachable until a request proves otherwise.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup aliveness probe to internal/provider/openai/reachability.go (or a new internal/routehealth/probe.go). For each configured provider with a non-cloud base_url, run a single TCP-connect probe (bounded ~2s) on Service.New(). Record the result in routehealth.Store via a new RecordProbe(key, success, observedAt) method.
2. Add a periodic background probe loop (mirroring runQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop in service_probe.go): re-probe providers whose last probe is older than HealthProbeInterval (default 60s). Bound to local-ish providers — skip the cloud subscription routes that handle their own health.
3. Make routehealth.Store optionally persistent. ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth path (already proposed in fizeau-7cd66e6b) writes the probe + failure cache to a JSON snapshot with HealthSignalTTL (default 10 min). Subsequent processes read the snapshot at startup so a fresh ddx try doesn't re-discover bragi-is-dead.
4. Extend the routing gate: a candidate whose endpoint has a Probe.Success=false within HealthSignalTTL is FILTERED (not just score-demoted) when alternates exist. New FilterReason: endpoint_unreachable.
5. RouteDecision evidence: include the probe state (last_probe_at, last_probe_success) for each candidate so operators can audit why a candidate was filtered.

NON-SCOPE
- Don't probe cloud subscription routes (claude, openrouter, etc.) — those have their own health story via quota probing.
- Don't change the connection-failure retry policy (fizeau-8134d6db already shipped: 1 attempt, fail fast).
- Don't introduce a daemon — the periodic probe runs inside Service goroutines, same lifetime as quota-recovery loop.
- Don't probe at request time — request-time dial is the existing reactive path; this bead adds proactive probing on a separate schedule.

DEPS
- Composes with fizeau-7cd66e6b (persist route-health across processes) — same persistence layer.
- Composes with fizeau-361f4f4b (router reliability epic) — provides the missing aliveness signal that epic's AC #1 ("fall through to subscription within one decision") depends on.
title: routing: proactive endpoint aliveness probing and durable cache for local providers

description:
PROBLEM
Under policy=default (no --harness/--provider/--model pin), the routing engine only emits ONE candidate per subprocess harness (its DefaultModel). For multi-tier subscription harnesses like claude (opus/sonnet/haiku under one auth), sonnet and haiku are never enumerated, so cost-aware scoring cannot pick the cheaper tier.

ROOT CAUSE (verified by reading internal/routing/engine.go:1410)
routingModelsForProvider enumerates h.AutoRoutingModels only when req.Harness != "" (i.e., harness is explicitly pinned). Under default policy with no pins, the function falls through to resolveModel, which returns a single model (DefaultModel).

ALSO needed: applySubscriptionRoutingCost in service_routing.go currently builds a single ProviderEntry with the cost of the DefaultModel only. For per-tier scoring to work, the ProviderEntry must carry per-model cost via ContextWindows-style maps (or equivalent), so the engine's candidateCost path can pick up the correct cost for each model in AutoRoutingModels.

SCOPE
- internal/routing/engine.go routingModelsForProvider: enumerate AutoRoutingModels when no Model/Provider pin AND the harness is a multi-tier subscription harness (h.IsSubscription && len(h.AutoRoutingModels)>1).
- internal/routing or service_routing.go: ensure each enumerated model uses its own catalog cost, not the harness DefaultModel cost.
- New unit tests in internal/routing covering per-tier enumeration for a subscription harness.

NON-SCOPE (deferred to sibling beads under fizeau-80d4bf41)
- Pin --model X routing through subscription harness instead of openrouter fallback.
- Negative test asserting filter_reason for an excluded same-harness tier.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead fizeau-11e9c095 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the first bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
Create scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension, executable bash) with arg parsing, listing subcommands, profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML reading, matrix expansion, preflight that triggers harbor-runner rebuild, and --plan output. No execution loop in this bead (covered by A4).

FILES:
  - scripts/benchmark/benchmark (new bash script).

DISPATCH:
  ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands: profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate | preflight | (no subcommand = run mode).

RUN-MODE FLAGS (only --plan is required for this bead):
  --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan] [--out DIR]

MATRIX EXPANSION:
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:` (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:`.
  - PR C will finalize the bench-set schema; for this bead default `task_executor` to `harbor` when `framework == terminal-bench`.

PREFLIGHT:
  - `./benchmark preflight` invokes scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh if cached image label sha differs from current source sha.

PLAN OUTPUT:
  - `--plan` prints the matrix (one line per cell) to stdout and exits 0; creates NO files; builds NO Docker images.

DEPENDS ON A1 (preflight references the image build) and A2 (listing subcommands enumerate harness-adapters/ and task-executors/).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark profiles, bench-sets, harness-adapters, task-executors each list available entries.
  2. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --plan --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary prints the matrix, creates NO files, builds NO Docker images.
  3. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark preflight rebuilds harbor-runner only when sha differs.
  4. shellcheck passes on the new script.
